name: Rachel Aguilar
review: Amazing experience here! I highly recommend it. We had wonderful timely service by the wait staff. The drinks were great (old fashion,  whiskey sour and cosmo) and all the food we tried was so good (the ravioli, the larded beef and the pasta was all so good). Also, the tuna tartare was really great.
label: Resident

name: Tiffany Sloan
review: Overall, we had a lovely dining experience at Impronta. The gluten-free fish appetizer was beautiful! One of my favorite dishes ever. The gf pasta dishes were delicious but very salty. The pasta with cuttlefish in black ink was so salty that I wasn¡¯t sure if it was a mistake or the nature of the dish. The panna cotta was simple and elegant. It was a wonderful atmosphere with great service. I would definitely return, but I¡¯d avoid the pasta due to salt.
label: Resident

name: Yeow Yang Neo (YY)
review:
We had dinner here and everything was amazing. The black thin tagliatelle with seafood sauce and the baked sea bass were both super tasty and perfectly cooked. The tiramisu was heavenly and honestly one of the best we have ever had. I also tried the grapefruit spritz, which was smooth and really refreshing.

The place has a nice cozy vibe and the staff were friendly and attentive. Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Diana Lacramioara
review: I had a wonderful experience at this restaurant. The food was truly delicious and full of flavor. I ordered the mixed fried fish, which was perfectly cooked, crispy, and very fresh. To go with it, I had a glass of prosecco that was light and refreshing, pairing really well with the seafood. For dessert, I tried both the tiramisu and the panna cotta. The tiramisu was rich and creamy, full of authentic taste, and the panna cotta was absolutely amazing - so smooth and delicate. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, and the service was friendly. Overall, it was a really enjoyable evening, and I would definitely recommend this place to anyone who loves great food and a cozy dining experience.
label: Resident

name: Bridgette Delva
review:
Amazing food, atmosphere and service. We are visiting from the US and the restaurant was recommended by the owners of our hotel.

Their menu is delectable and everything is fresh & hand crafted. I highly recommend the mixed fish appetizer, it was phenomenal. I had the ricotta ravioli and my husband had the black ink tagliatelle. We finished with the carpaccio pineapple dish and a complimentary tiramisu. We were stuffed by the end however the dishes were so good, we had to finish! This is a must try. Nikola was the most polite, patient and knowledgeable server - be sure to ask for him!
label: Resident

name: Kristel Chan
review:
My boyfriend and I truly enjoyed our meal at Impronta. Tuna tartare, lobster pasta, ravioli were all excellent. Our kind server even offered us an extra pasta for us to have a taste as I have asked about it during the ordering. We even ended our meal with pork chops. Pork chops were tenderly cooked, despite expecting normal ¡°fries¡±, their version of crispy potato was indeed a better fit for the dish itself.

We lost a point in food because we really didn¡¯t like the cod fish appetizer with onion ice cream. However, it¡¯s likely more a personal preference than anything else.
label: Resident

name: nayast
review: Absolutely fantastic place. The pasta is so delicious I¡¯d come back even on the rainiest day! comfort from the first bite to the last?? The staff are friendly, attentive and genuinely warm, you feel taken care of without any fuss. A special thank you to Roman, he made the whole experience even better with his kindness and professionalism?? Great menu, fantastic taste, good bar!Sure, it is the best restaurant in Venice
label: Resident

name: Lynn N.
review:
Food was good and the price is very affordable as well. The shrimp one was flavourful, tomatoey, fresh. The cuttlefish pasta was amazing as well, although I would appreciate slightly more flavour with the sauce, the cuttlefish itself was so so fresh, cooked to perfection and generous portion as well.

The only thing is that the bread is a bit stale and tough at the same time haha, out of the 2 breads they have us one was really really chewy, while the other was hard like a rock. Service is also slightly rushed and forgetful, had to asked 3 times to get the olive oil and salt.

Other than that definitely recommend this place because it is cheap, good, and quick!
label: Resident

name: Taylor E
review: I had this restaurant saved as a recommendation and we went after the Vivaldi concert since it was near the church. It was later in the night on a Friday and the restaurant was mostly empty. However, everything was great and delicious. Service was perfect and the food was fantastic. The bread was the fluffiest and softest bread we had in all of Italy.. it was so delicious. I ordered the spinach ravioli with sausage and my partner ordered duck gnocchi. We then split the tiramisu for dessert and watermelon was provided on the house for us. The interior of this restaurant was beautiful and charming. They had a seasonal/daily menu as well as the standard menu and it is evident that this changes often which is neat. Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: TJ
review:
What a spectacular dining experience! I can't recommend this place enough. From the moment we walked in, we were treated like family. The food was simply incredible¡ªevery dish was a work of art.
We started with the octopus salad, which was fresh and tender, and the mussels and clam soup, a rich and flavorful broth that was perfect for dipping. For our main courses, the grilled fish was cooked to perfection, flaky and moist. The cuttlefish ink pasta was a rich, inky delight, and the Samara pasta Venetian style was a revelation of local flavors. We finished the meal with a classic tiramisu and a perfect cappuccino, the ideal end to a perfect evening.
The service by Mechile and Marco were impeccable¡ªattentive but not intrusive. The atmosphere was warm, lively, and authentically Venetian. If you're looking for a true taste of Venice, this trattoria is an absolute must.
label: Resident

name: William Fathers
review: Was walking by and happened to stumble upon a gem of a restaurant. Was greeted at the door and service was impeccable. Salam was an amazing waiter. Food was top tier, pasta was fresh and shrimp was cooked perfectly. Would recommend if you are in the area.
label: Resident

name: Richard Mah (Richard)
review: I love the location, and corner where it is, away from the main scene. Good memories of places, are usually because of good food. And this is one of them. But what makes it memorable is the service and staff. Valentina, Rona and Miah were excellent hosts, each with their own uniqueness. I thought I'd just have a couple glasses of wines and spaghetti vongole. But ...  I ended up with more wines and with Valentina's suggestion of Tagliatelle Gamberi e Zucchini. Then , tiramisu and espresso. I ended the night being full and happy. Grazie!
label: Resident

name: Jaena Lee
review:
We went to this restaurant because it¡¯s famous for its food ¡ª and it truly lived up to its reputation!
The fried squid was thin, crispy, and perfectly seasoned. It was fried so delicately that it didn¡¯t hurt the roof of my mouth ¡ª a true work of art!
I don¡¯t usually enjoy seafood, but the risotto was so delicious I couldn¡¯t stop eating it. The rich, creamy shrimp flavor was amazing ??
When we ordered, Valentina recommended the dish, calling it ¡°absolutely fantastic,¡± and it really matched my taste perfectly.
We were so full that we couldn¡¯t finish everything, and when she saw that, Valentina smiled warmly and asked, ¡°Would you like me to pack it for you?¡± Her kindness was so touching.
Thanks to Valentina, we ended the day with a wonderful meal and a happy heart. Thank you so much! ??
label: Resident

name: Rachel S
review:
The best gluten free pasta I've had in Venice!
The sauce was delicious. The service was friendly and attentive.
We sat outside and it has a lovely atmosphere, not too busy.
So glad we came here.
label: Resident

name: R T
review: Probably one of the cheapest places to eat in Venice. Meal sets at 15/18 which include your food + drinks + coffee is an absolute steal! And no cover charge. Don¡¯t expect handmade fresh pasta for the price, of course. The pastas were delicious with a homey feel, probably what an average Italian home cooked meal would taste like. That said, the flavour of the risotto was amazing. Coffee was also pretty good (balanced flavour with good foam) and rounded out the meal nicely. The waiters were very friendly and smiley, great service. Overall, come in with the right expectations that this is a more affordable average local option and you¡¯ll have a great time
label: Resident

name: Kristin W
review: Lovely, pokey little pasta/ risotto spot in Venice. Tucked away, friendly service (seemingly a rarity in Venice) with fresh pasta and seafood and incredible value for money on the island. We paid 20 each, which included a glass of wine, seafood pasta and espresso. The clam spaghetti came recommended by the server and it was a beautiful, balanced, fresh dish with subtle flavours and gorgeously paired with the glass of house white. The tomato prawn (served in the shell) spaghetti is also amazing. A must-go for affordable, authentic pasta in Venice. (Bathroom was not pleasant).
label: Resident

name: David Cruz
review:
Amazing food. High quality. Nice price. No coperto.
Really good atmosphere. Classical music. Takes the #1 spot in Venice on my book.
label: Resident

name: S. Park
review: Great food, excellent prices, and friendly service. They have set menus  (pasta/risotto, drink, and coffee) at prices you will pay for pasta alone at other places. The pasta was one of the best I had in Venice. Every ingredient was fresh and I was able to taste them. The location is hidden and the dining room is tiny, but all other good qualities make it a worthy place to visit while in Venezia!
label: Resident

name: Esther Lee
review:
The best restaurant we have in Venice! The carbonara is DELICIOUS it¡¯s so creamy and thick. It¡¯s impressive to make such a basic dish so fantastic!
The 15 set menu comes with a main course, a soft drink and a cup of nice coffee. Definitely good value for the money.

The staff were also very friendly and welcoming. Once we were seated, the restaurant became very busy but he remained professional and ensured the food was of good quality!
label: Resident

name: Viktorija Stojanovska
review: Very cute little place with amazing and super delicious food! The fish and the appetizers were delicious and I would definitely recommend it to anyone who wants to try an authentic Italian cuisine with genuine local vibes!
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Dennis Diaz
review:
Dal Moro¡¯s in Venice absolutely exceeded my expectations! The carbonara prepared in a cheese wheel was hands down the best I¡¯ve had anywhere in Italy ¡ª rich, creamy, and full of flavor. The Caesar salad was made table side, adding a fun and personal touch to the meal. The Bolognese was incredible ¡ª perfectly cooked pasta with a deep, savory sauce that tasted authentically homemade. To top it all off, the tiramisu was also prepared right in front of us and was the perfect sweet finish.

Every dish was not only delicious but also presented with care and flair. A must-visit spot in Venice for anyone who loves authentic Italian food with a bit of showmanship!
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Kero Chan
review:
Walked in this place without reservation. I have been wanted to try pasta prepared on a cheese wheel so we went straight for that. Seasonings were on point. Reasonable portions. Except the carbonara pasta did get cooled down a bit after stirring in the cheese wheel.

The place has very nice deco and a laid back vibe. The staff was polite, friendly and cultural-sensitive. He greeted us in Cantonese instead of Mandarin, which was impressive.
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Cassandra Longino
review: This place was wonderful. The service was top notch. We absolutely loved the pasta mixed together in the cheese wheels. The decorations were adorable and a fun tropical garden vibe. The only thing I can say is that it was a little too warm inside, but there were a lot of customers and frequent fires in the cheese wheels. Overall, I really loved eating here.
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Deean Purnama-Ong
review: The best experience for fresh pasta and authentic dessert making. They will cook the pasta in front of you and let you interact. Our fav is beef tartar and 2 kinds of pasta. Best services in Venice. I didnt expect
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: S Bhushan
review:
Fresh Pasta Worth the Hype¡ªBut Room to Improve

Dal Moro¡¯s delivers on its promise of fresh, flavorful pasta to go. The food preparation was excellent¡ªsauces were rich, pasta perfectly cooked, and the freshness was unmistakable. It¡¯s clear why this spot has become a must-visit for pasta lovers in Venice.

That said, the overall experience could be smoother. The space is very small, and the menu isn¡¯t visible until you¡¯re practically at the counter. With a long line behind us, we felt rushed to choose, and probably would¡¯ve picked differently if we¡¯d had more time. A more visible or posted menu¡ªespecially with translations¡ªwould help guests make better decisions without pressure.

Also, since this is a takeout-only spot, they recommend eating the pasta immediately. That¡¯s fair, but it¡¯s surprisingly hard to find a garbage bin nearby in Venice. Given the disposable packaging, it would be helpful if Dal Moro¡¯s provided a bin in the vicinity and took responsibility for keeping the area clean.

Still worth a visit for the pasta alone¡ªjust come prepared and maybe scout the menu ahead of time.
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Writer5647
review: We went based off the reviews we saw online, and it was definitely worth it! Of course we had to try the bacon carbonara served in the cheese wheel - 10/10! They prepare it at the table, talk you through the whole process, and it¡¯s a very generous portion. Ended the dinner with some fresh espresso to wake us back up! Would highly recommend, just keep in mind it¡¯s roughly 25-30 per person
label: Resident

place_title: Dal Moro's Venice | Fresh Pasta Restaurant
name: Matt Roberts
review:
After hearing about Dal Moro's takeaway pasta before our visit to Venice, we decided to try a sit down meal at the restaurant instead.

We heard that it was small (which is true) and we expected to queue for a table so we arrived for late lunch at around 4pm. When we arrived, we got a table straight away and most were empty.

The staff were very friendly, and the service was very quick - our food came only a few minutes after ordering. We ordered one Bolognese and one Carbonara, both of which were delicious.

We will definitely come back for more!
label: Resident

name: cassy oneill
review: Our 1st taste of Pizza and it was delicious! The lady was really friendly and welcoming, the food was fast but not rushed. My son loved and devoured his gnocchi! My pizza was just perfect.
label: Resident

name: neha sharma
review: "Amazing atmosphere, delicious food, sitting arrangments also very nice with beautiful view ??waitress was friendly, attentive, and made sure we had everything we needed. Excellent service!" I highly recommend this restaurant with great vibe
label: Resident

name: Annahans
review:
We picked Trattoria DiGiorgio from many options while looking for a restaurant with a cozy vibe. Tables placed outside, right by the water, with the heat lamps, so it was possible to sit without a jacket.
The staff were friendly, and what¡¯s most important, food was delicious. We definitely recommend this restaurant
label: Resident

name: Matthias Miesbauer
review:
We (two) were here to have a late dinner from 9PM to about 10:30PM.

Everyone was really friendly and the food was excellent.

The ambiente is perfect for a Venice holiday.

Would absolutly recommand.
label: Resident

name: Ben Morris
review: Best spaghetti with seafood I've ever had. The staff couldn't do enough for us. Very accommodating. Did us a calamari starter to share, prepared a plain spaghetti dish for my 6 year old girl, with some oil and Parmesan, which she loved. The portions are amazing and the quality of the food is excellent. Been in Italy just over a week. Eat in Peschiera, Verona and now here, this was our best meal out.
label: Resident

name: Karen Saez
review: We had a great evening! Very good service in a super cozy and atmospheric setting. The pasta with onions was extremely tasty. I also loved my vegan pizza (veggie pizza with no cheese) - the olive oil and fresh basil were delicious. Recommend!
label: Resident

name: Ella Fucanan
review: Great food to welcome us upon arriving! We ordered seafood - Catch of the Day and Seafood Risotto. Everything was perfect, seafood was fresh, and lovingly prepared and served by the staff. Pictures don¡¯t give justice, the fish was already deboned by this time. They bring it straight to your table and we stood in awe of the mastery of removing the bones live.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review: Al Vecio Forno, without a shadow of doubt, had the best service of any place we ate at in Venice. We stopped here for a light bite to eat around dinner time, we got sat in the back patio which sits literally right on a canal. What's nice is this spot is also covered so if it were raining you could still sit outside! For dinner we had a Greek Salad and Fried Calamari. Both were great, but the service is what really brought it over the top. The staff were very friendly, we joked around, and had a great laugh.
label: Resident

name: Andrew Snape
review:
A fantastic lunch/dinner in beautiful surroundings.
The food and service was 10/10, antipasti sardines and scallops, the perfect starters.

Secondi of lasagna and tagliatelle bolognese were the best we¡¯ve ever had. Drinks & dessert all perfect.
Service was excellent, friendly and funny and we were made to feel special.
The restaurant is a little tucked out of the way , but is well worth seeking out.
Very highly recommended. There is indoor seating, but three tables under a covered arch are the highlight. You can eat/drink and watch the fish and locals float by.
label: Resident

name: Jeanie Pearson-Wanek
review: Delicious - the waiter recommended the caprese with a local seasonal heirloom tomato. It was the best caprese salad I've ever had, by far. Incredibly delicious. The service was excellent. We enjoyed all of our dishes (forgot to take a photo of the main pasta dishes because we wanted to start eating them right away!)
label: Resident

name: Dickson Au
review:
A fantastic place for a meal. Foods are great. Staff services are excellent, especially Cristian. Cristian has giving the warmest feeling to us during our lunch time, he is also capable to speak Cantonese with us. Sincerely recommend foods and dessert as well to us. We do having a great time of lunch in this restaurant.

It is highly recommended to have the meal here and I m sure I will be here for dinner and lunch again when I have another visit in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Lina Marican
review: Stuffed and super happy with dinner at Gio¡¯s. We were well taken care of by Veronica - who recommended the best local wine - and Thomas - who took such good care of us from start to finish. We had the tasting menu and we were so full by the end of it. Can¡¯t decide on our favorite .. love the beautifully tender octopus as well as the pasta. Beautiful location, and top notch service and food.
label: Resident

name: Mia Black
review: Truly next-level delicious food with gluten free options! Especially the roasted duck, and the monkfish saffron pasta. And edible flowers on everything! Very fair prices. Adel was a delight. We hope to come back someday!
label: Resident

name: Joe K
review: This was the absolute best restaurant we had been to during our 3 night honeymoon stay in Venice. The service, food, and atmosphere were all incredible. We sat outside in the piazza and there was live music playing in addition to the lovely vibes of the restaurant itself. The staff were so warm, kind, and attentive, to a degree that stunned and astounded us. Adele in particular was an absolute gem. Food wise, we had outstanding burrata pugliese and seafood, the spaghetti with clams will forever live in my mind rent-free. So will the fish dish. You can¡¯t go wrong here!
label: Resident

name: xyyy
review: stumbled upon this place and decided to have lunch. ordered the burrata, seafood risotto and ragu and it was soo good! the seafood especially was really fresh and tasty. great service by aymen too, we'll definitely drop by again if we're here!
label: Resident

name: Stanley Qian
review:
Wow, what a fantastic find in Venice! We stumbled upon this place and loved it right away. It's set in a quiet, open square¡ªsuch a nice and relaxing atmosphere.

We started with the raw fish and octopus appetizer, and it was amazing! The combination with balsamic vinegar and olive oil was just perfect. For the main course, the salmon was seriously delicious¡ªcooked so well and full of flavor.

Big thanks to our waiter for the great recommendations! If we ever come back to Venice, we¡¯ll definitely eat here again. Highly recommend! ??
label: Resident

name: Marlena Mlynarska
review: The dinner was incredible! They had delicious pasta and it was so beautifully presented. The service was so lovely and they really took care of us. We loved this place for an elevated last dinner in Venice! It was beautiful, thank you!
label: Resident

name: Lindsay Duthie
review: We booked a table here for our 2nd Wedding Anniversary meal, whilst visiting Venice. The waiters were all very welcoming and friendly and the food was incredible. We had the King Fish Carpaccio and Filo Tortellini to start, Gnocchi and Crispy Sea Bass for mains and shared a Sorbet Trio for dessert. They gave us a complimentary lemoncello at the end of our meal which was awfully kind! The service started off very speedy but started to slow a little throughout the night, but I think that was due in part to the restaurant growing busier as the evening went on. Overall we had an amazing time and would definitely revisit if we came back to Venice again!
label: Resident

name: Jeanie Pearson-Wanek
review: Delicious - the waiter recommended the caprese with a local seasonal heirloom tomato. It was the best caprese salad I've ever had, by far. Incredibly delicious. The service was excellent. We enjoyed all of our dishes (forgot to take a photo of the main pasta dishes because we wanted to start eating them right away!)
label: Resident

name: Dickson Au
review:
A fantastic place for a meal. Foods are great. Staff services are excellent, especially Cristian. Cristian has giving the warmest feeling to us during our lunch time, he is also capable to speak Cantonese with us. Sincerely recommend foods and dessert as well to us. We do having a great time of lunch in this restaurant.

It is highly recommended to have the meal here and I m sure I will be here for dinner and lunch again when I have another visit in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Mark Wojciechowski
review: Hosted us without a reservation despite the busiest of afternoons due to the proximity to the marathon finish line.  Great staff and even better food.  The very best meal we had in Venice over the past several days. Good value too.
label: Resident

name: Jasper Vercammen
review: One of the best pizza¡¯s we ever had. And finally a decent limoncello spritz, not the chemical fluorescent stuff we were server regularly in other places.
label: Resident

name: Utkan Pazar
review:
--Service--

The staff is very friendly and they have the best sense of humor too. The service was swift and well regarding the food and drinks as well. Our food were brought to us in under 10 minutes and they were prepared very well.

--Cost--

Although the atmosphere of the restaurant was very well and the food was delicious, the cost was reasonable and standard. A carbonara was 15, the vien pizza was 12 and the prices are around this range if you don't get some pompous stuff like pasta with lobster.

--Atmosphere--

Great and looks like an expensive restaurant, yet somehow the prices are good.

--Food--

There are various foods with local ingredients. My friend got cuttlefish inked pasta and he said it was different and great. We also got fried calamari for the table and it was delicious as a side dish. The pizza that we ordered was also well done and it was filled generously with ingredients.
label: Resident

name: Lulu Ritchie
review: Lovely restaurant! Dalia looked after us so beautifully. Incredible sea food linguine. Sardines with onions and pine nuts delish. Unpretentious with good food in what felt like a traditional Venetian local restaurant
label: Resident

name: J-J Mao
review: Wonderful dinner with attentive service and delicious food. The portions are a good size to try several dishes. Dessert was very inventive too.
label: Resident

name: Evelynn Pena
review: We came here for my birthday. It was the best restaurant in Venice. From the customer service, to the ambiance, and the unique flavors/ textures of the food, we were ate awe every second while we were there. I will highly recommend this restaurant for all special occasions.
label: Resident

name: Claudine Super
review: From the service, the food, and the wine, it was incredible. It was our last evening in Italy, and it was perfect. Ivan our server was very knowledgeable and gave many good recommendations. The chef created food with love and thoughtful presentation. I would HIGHLY recommend La Caravella!
label: Resident

name: Quentin Mari¨¦thoz
review:
Just perfect.

All the waiters were amazing and helpful. It¡¯s was a delight to be able to share all antipasti/primi platti/secondi platti together with my husband.

The food was extraordinary, our best restaurant in Venice : the best discovery and quality versus price.

We have been amazed with the vegetarian options.
label: Resident

name: Mona Ansryan
review:
The food was delicious, the staff attentive, and the atmosphere peaceful and beautiful. Perfect for a luxurious & enjoyable dinner experience in San Marco!

+ They are very accommodating to children & special requests.
label: Resident

name: James
review: Everything was amazing with La caravella, from the decor to the complementary lemoncello at the end. The food was the best we have ever had in Venice. Was a bit pricey but if you want somewhere worth spending a bit of money on this is the place. Would definitely recommend!
label: Resident

name: Zinny Tiaon
review:
We had researched this restaurant as it had gluten-free options for our 15yr old coeliac son and it did not disappoint.
On the first day our family got separated, so 2 of us ate elsewhere (to our misfortune). My wife had Risotto with wonders of the sea and my son had a pizza. They both absolutely loved their meals. Our 11yr old who ate with me earlier then got a chocolate panna cotta which was divine. The friendly staff said to come back and try the pasta, which needed no arm twisting.
The next day 3 of us had pasta and the 11yr old had pizza.
My wife and son being gluten free meant pasta dishes were adapted to have gf penne only. Their carbonara and porchini mushrooms with speck were some of the best gf pasta dishes we've ever tasted.
I was able to have the regular spaghetti carbonara which was incredible. And most importantly they were incredibly careful in the kitchen to avoid any cross contamination.
We had 3 more panna cottas and the youngest had tiramisu.
Delicious food, friendly attentive (and patient) staff, lovely atmosphere.
Highly recommended
label: Resident

name: Philippe Reme
review:
I had the fish of the day baked in salt with herbs and seasonal vegetables.
Spaghettoni with clams.
My friend had the Tuna filet in a sesame and pistachio crust with a mixed salad.
The food was delicious. Whenever we are back in Venus, we will definitely come back to try more things on the menu. I would recommend Queen Restaurant.
label: Resident

review:
This was one of the most satisfying restaurant I've ever had in Italy. It felt like I'd discovered a hidden treasure. I highly recommend the Queen of Venice pasta, and the other dishes are all excellent value and delicious. My seafood-loving kids loved the clam and lobster pasta, and the noodles and cooking were always perfect. I've had scallops, lamb chops, seafood soup, tuna tartare, and tataki, and they were all excellent.

I VISITED TWICE!
label: Resident

name: xhulieta marku
review:
From the moment we walked in, this restaurant impressed us with its warm atmosphere and incredibly attentive service. The staff were not only friendly and professional, but they made us feel like honored guests throughout the entire evening.

The food? Absolutely exceptional.

We started with a beautifully presented antipasto of prosciutto, fresh burrata, cherry tomatoes, olives, and arugula¡ªsimple yet bursting with flavor. The ingredients were clearly fresh and high-quality.

The creamy risotto was a standout dish, rich and perfectly cooked, with just the right balance of flavors. It was comfort food elevated to fine dining status.

But the star of the show was undoubtedly the tomahawk steaks¡ªperfectly seared, juicy, and full of flavor. They were served dramatically at the table, a true feast for the senses.

Every dish was prepared with great attention to detail, and it¡¯s clear that the kitchen team takes real pride in their craft.

Overall, this was one of the best dining experiences we¡¯ve had in a long time. Outstanding food, impeccable service, and a welcoming ambiance¡ªwhat more could you ask for?

Highly recommended. We¡¯ll definitely be back!
label: Resident

name: Omi Jeanlouis
review: I was very impressed with the service and my meal. I had to Seabass and it was delicious. It was freshly deboned right in front of me. My friend had the lasagna and it was amazing. Customer service was awesome. They were very attentive to my group we had a great time.
label: Resident

name: Ric Reifel
review: Please believe me,  this is a fantastic restaurant.   The food quality is top notch.   I spoke with one of the owners,  this is his baby after running other restaurants in town.   Everything was exceptional,  best sea bass packed in salt,  and the best liver we have ever had.   I love that they recommend local dishes and wine!
label: Resident

name: Cecile Biezanowski
review: We booked a table for lunch. The place is nicely decorated and the food was good. BUT we had some big disappointment: at our arrival the waiter brought glasses of prosecco (which we did not order). We thought it was a welcoming drink, as the restaurant looks fancy. The waiter did not even asked if we wanted them. In other places I would have asked why and so on. But again the restaurant looks fancy, so we were full to think it was a welcoming drink (we are French and such things happin nice French restaurant). When we got the bills, they charged us 15  per glass !!! We were shocked. So be careful if you eat there, as they want to full you. The food was good but this attitude really disappointed us, therefore I would not recommend thus place!
label: Resident

name: Betty Bui
review:
Absolutely love this restaurant and its location right on the water! Although our reservation was 8:30, it took a little while longer because we wanted to wait for a table closest to the water for photos :)
There was bread and introductory bites that were absolutely delicious! I wish I could remember what they were but I can remember they are DELICIOUS!
The carpaccio was fresh and amazing - I definitely recommend. As well as the Maialino - so tender and flavorful. The only thing I dislike is the service speed. They¡¯re friendly and inform you on all plate items, but ga damn they are slow. Although, they close at 11pm, we ordered the tiramisu at 10:55pm. They said the kitchen was closed. It¡¯s unfortunate because a lot of reviews said it was good so we wanted to try it, but maybe next time.

name: Ashley Carswell
review: Food was delicious, service impeccable, beautiful interior, and gorgeous views of the water. What more can you ask for a Venice date out? Would recommend: chicken ravioli and vegetarian pasta.
label: Resident

name: M G
review: The interior design was exceptional, with a cozy atmosphere. The staff were incredibly friendly and attentive, adding a personal touch to our experience. Given the reputation of the restaurant and the premium pricing, we arrived with high expectations. While the setting and service were outstanding, we felt the food didn¡¯t quite match the same standard. With such a prime location and wonderful ambiance, there¡¯s incredible potential here, and we hope to see improvements in the menu that align with the overall experience.
label: Resident

name: Harun Feraidon
review:
The interior is gorgeous, great artwork selections.
The service was amazing, very attentive service and they bring out complimentary bread and amuse bouche. The bread was delicious and a little warm, however I think much more warm would be better. And the amuse bouche had various sea flavors which were fun to try. Not everything here was delicious but I don¡¯t think that was the goal, it was various flavors and I still enjoyed trying each.

The entrees are small proportion, beautifully plated, however taste mostly typical. I think the price is mostly fair considering the artful plating and ingredients used, but the taste isn¡¯t spectacular and food size is very small.

I had the lobster black ravioli, it was good, however not spectacular, and obviously 4 ravioli for 25 is not fun. I think  maybe throw in some non lobster ravioli to fill the plate more.

I would only really recommend this place if you¡¯re looking to spend a lot, given the small plates.
label: Resident

name: Giovanna Puntorieri
review:
Beautiful and welcoming restaurant.

The atmosphere is quite elegant and the style is definitely up to standards. We ordered a selection of plates which have definitely called our attention in their presentation and flavours profile. A quite heavy selection of fish dishes with a couple of meat choices.

The Aperol sprits was top.

I¡¯d definitely recommend to try it if you want to remember your stay in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Tar
review: Great place for lunch.  Menu was perfect and the location is great for a view and a chance to watch the water taxis!  Service was excellent and all servers spoke English perfectly.
label: Resident

review:
Let¡¯s start with the good stuff:
- The view is fantastic
- The bread and an appetizer (first picture) are complimentary
- Presentation was good

We ordered 2 Menu Tradizionale and the Menu Algiubagio (each menu was 78  + 4,80  cover charge) plus drinks.
You can see influences from Japanese cuisine and fine dining.
The food tasted very average in our opinion and did not live up to our expectations (especially given the price and the overwhelmingly positive reviews).
If you still plan on coming here for the view: only order what you like a la carte and be aware of mosquitoes at night.

review: The food here was extraordinary! We highly recommend the Gnocchi Bolognese and the Spaghetti al Nero di seppia. We got there as they opened and grabbed a great seat. The staff was welcoming and super helpful. Give this place a try.

review: Walked in off the street for lunch with my daughter and had the 'menu di mare' which was great: pickled sardines, a Venetian standard, along with a light, fresh tomato and prawn tagliatelle, and grilled king prawns with polenta. name: Ashley
review: A last minute stop looking for an authentic meal. It did not disappoint. Welcomed in and enjoyed a delicious meal. Started with a cheese plate appetizer and a litre of wine. Followed by gnocchi and pesto pasta. We were not rushed and thoroughly enjoyed our meal here.

review: A local friend recommended this spot to me. It¡¯s tucked away behind a fish market, so it can be a little tricky to find, but once I stepped inside, I instantly loved the atmosphere. I ordered the raw bar platter, it was fresh and beautifully presented, and the squid ink pasta was absolutely delicious.
label: Resident

name: suleewan sueram
review: There is one staff, the food is delicious, and they also have a welcome drink service. They are very kind. The atmosphere in the restaurant is very good, but the entrance is a bit difficult to find because if you are not a local, you probably won't know this restaurant because you have to pass the fish market.
label: Resident

name: Maria Norton
review: The food was not great - expected fresh Italian seafood and what we got was overpriced chewy lobster. Sat to a really loud table and were moved when complained about it. If you¡¯re thinking about coming here to taste Italian food? I recommend you pick elsewhere! Even after leaving most of our meal, we were still charged full price. At 150, I¡¯d expect much better!
label: Resident

name: Leanne Morrell
review:
Fabulous service and kept us warm with heaters and blankets during our evening meal!

Risotto was to die for and the whole meal for all was incredible! A must to visit if you come out here!
label: Resident

name: Hannah M
review: Food is amazing and reasonably priced. Drinks are fantastic, and the service is incredible. Everyone here is so lovely and make you feel so welcome and they make sure you are having a brilliant time. 10/10 I would definitely recommend.
label: Resident

name: Amy Childress
review:
Delicious food, amazing staff, and they made our family celebration a perfect night to remember!

I can¡¯t say enough good things about my experience at Trattoria Rialto Novo. I was visiting Venice to celebrate my daughter¡¯s engagement and was searching for a good restaurant for the celebration dinner for the whole family. I stopped in at Rialto Novo for lunch to check out the menu and atmosphere.  The first thing I appreciated was that the restaurant is just a block away from the crowds - making it quieter and easier to connect and hear each other for a family dinner. When I shared  the situation, Tani and his team jumped in with their ideas  and options and I could see they were committed to make the night special. I visited the restaurant several times before the event to ask more questions and finalize plans and every time I arrived they made me feel welcomed and comfortable.

In the end, they worked within my budget to put together an incredible menu - I think maybe 6 courses?! - consisting of some dishes that are not on the regular restaurant menu that were very special and delicious. Our menu centered around the fresh seafood that they get in each day. But they also accommodated people in our group who were vegetarian or did not like seafood. They helped me work with the flower store just across from the restaurant to create gorgeous flower arrangements for our table.

The main course of the Mediterranean Sea bass might be the most delicious dish I¡¯ve ever had. And the dessert was incredible - I wish I had the recipe. I forgot to take pictures of every course but they were all presented beautifully!

Everything they did exceeded my expectations and I feel so lucky and grateful that I found them.
label: Resident

name: Mark Johannes
review:
Food (pizza and salad with house wine) was good. Not exceptional or bad. Service was ok. Not great, and not bad. Atmosphere, could be better(sat in courtyard and had leaves and tree parts all over the table and waiter did not clean or brush off the table when we sat).
I was surprised at the cover charge per person. It is shown on the menu (tiny letters at the bottom). To me, this is a hidden fee to get extra money. I definitely don¡¯t feel like the place deserved an extra 5 euros for the quality and service. If they need the money, put an extra euro on all items.

name: Arsen Barbullushi
review:
Nestled in a quiet corner of the city, is the kind of place that instantly transports you to the heart of Italy. From the warm welcome at the door to the last sip of espresso.

The menu is a love letter to classic Italian cuisine.

The wine list features several regional Italian bottles, and the staff are happy to recommend pairings. Our server, Argent, was knowledgeable and attentive without being overbearing ¡ª the kind of service that elevates a good meal into a memorable one.
I¡¯ll be back again ??

review: We had an awesome meal at La Lista! The Diavolissima pizza was delicious ¡ª just the right amount of spiciness with amazing flavor. label: Resident

name: Nat K
review:
A must try when you are in venice , worth every penny you are going to pay , the food quality is amazing , it is so delicious and very well cooked .
The place is cozy and the people there are very hospitable and professional.
I had my birthday celebration there and it is an unforgettable one .
label: Resident

name: Ben King
review: Wonderful food, decent price, attentive service. Really enjoyed our dining experience. The mixed fried seafood and the ravioli were the highlights.
label: Resident

name: Anna Askins-Dunn
review: My first sip of wine in Italy! The service was fantastic. We ordered the seafood risotto (a must in Venice) and the spinach ravioli. Both were delicious- large portions appropriate for the price! We went for an early dinner and was very easy to find a table outside.
label: Resident

name: Antlers McDoogle
review:
Alboretti was highly recommended by our boutique hotel manager as one of the few authentic Italian restaurants left in Venice.  He was not wrong!  The experience was an absolute delight!  The dining patio garden in the back is very romantic and a wonderful place for a very memorable dinning experience.  Our dinner concierge was a true delight with excellent recommendations, especially on the vino ??
We had the seafood risotto and everything was so exquisite we bought a bottle of the wine to enjoy at our hotel and came back our last night to end our stay in Venice on a definite high note ??
And yes, my shrimp was eyeing my wine and enjoying the atmosphere ??
label: Resident

name: Dane Brough
review:
The experience here has been absolutely phenomenal.

Our waiter was incredibly friendly and very accommodating of my limited Italian. Our food was served promptly and delicious. We opted for a traditional dinner where we had entre¨¦s, first and seconds. However we were full by the time we came around to dessert so we decided to just have some wine and relax. Our waiter brought us some lovely cheeses from the local area to have with our wine! Absolutely delicious. We finished with an espresso and some limoncello.

Absolutely fantastic meals, fresh food and pasta al dente. Cannot recommend this enough for someone looking for a quieter evening away from the main streets of Venice!
label: Resident

name: N RDBS
review: This is a cozy restaurant in Venice. They have outdoor seating but it was raining while we were there so we sat inside. I did make a reservation but I'm not sure if it was needed. They weren't busy when we arrived but slowly more people arrived. We ordered several dishes to share with the table. Please see photos to see the different dishes we tried. We were able to walk to the restaurant from our hotel, it was a beautiful walk through the walkways of Venice. FYI, the risotto is served as two orders and is meant for more than one person. We could barely finish it though since we ordered several other dishes.
label: Resident

name: Heidi Hany
review: Far beyond amazing ...very warm and cozy ambiance and the food was something else ???? highly recommended ????
label: Resident

name: Andy Britner
review:
A great experience at this restaurant.
Very attentive and friendly staff, such a nice visit.
Our table had a whole selection of food, but we didn't take pictures of everything, just 2 dishes as the others were dived in to right away!
All pastas were perfect, sauces delicious and fresh and plentiful.
If back in Venice, we would visit here again.
label: Resident

name: Lou
review:
We were looking for this place for two days after accidentally passing it during day walks around town. Found by by a chance - Thank you Universe! ???? really wanted to try cuttlefish ink risotto and yes it was very much worth the try. Fantastic bruschetta¡¯s for a starter sealed the deal.
A big plus - Italian music in the background which was so rare to find everywhere else!
label: Resident

name: Ayshea
review: We visited Gheni after the most romantic moment of my life (a gondola proposal) and it couldn¡¯t have been a more perfect choice. Maurizzi was absolutely fantastic: warm, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about what he does. The wine and champagne selection was exceptional, and the food¡­ truly unforgettable. Probably the best steak and risotto we¡¯ve ever had. A beautiful restaurant with impeccable service. Definitely an evening we¡¯ll remember forever.
label: Resident

name: Renata Sadekova
review:
That was amazing very high end dinner. Service is on the top of expectations. Luckily we had a chance to taste a new menu. The dish of the evening for us - Ravioi with figs and covered with cacao beans sauce. The classy food as beef tar-tar was also good. If I would be in Venice one more time definitely will come back to Gheni.

Thank you for the soulful and memorable evening!
label: Resident

name: Chris Vargas
review: Do not miss this place!!  We discovered this on our first night in Venice and could not have been happier.  Every dish was a piece of art - and delicious!  Servers were super friendly and fun to chat with.  Good english.  And an amazing wine list.  5-stars-plus.
label: Resident

name: seth rawlins
review: This restaurant is simply incredible. The owner has created an amazing environment ¡ª he even offered to take a group photo for us (which we gladly accepted). The food was excellent ¡ª I had the crab appetizer and the John Dory as my main course, both of which were delicious and expertly prepared. The presentation was top notch as well. Beyond the food, the service and atmosphere were outstanding. Maurizio and Nicolo took care of our table (and the others) with professionalism and genuine hospitality. I rarely write reviews, but I had to leave one for this spot. 10/10!
label: Resident

name: Katharina Bergerhoff
review: We had such a lovely evening at Gheni, excellent service from Maurizio and inventive and fresh dishes ?? A little table at the canal side, sunset and a delicious Veneto red wine¡­ don¡¯t miss it!
label: Resident

name: Lisa Cantaert
review:
To be fair, we had very high expectations due to the many good reviews and as Belgians we are spoiled when it comes to food. Sadly, the expectations were not met when the food was served. We ordered the tasting menu. The starters were nice and so was the cloud egg, but the other dishes lacked seasoning and cohesion.
The man serving our dinner was very friendly and cheerful so he made up for a lot of it.

However, some mistakes were made regarding the wine (we were served a bottle almost twice as expensive as the one we ordered), a bottle of water was charged extra and empty glasses weren¡¯t taken away. We did pay the more expensive bottle of wine but were each offered an extra glass of digestif to compensate, which was a nice thing to do.

Nice setting, there¡¯s definitely potential as the chef is displaying some nice techniques in his dishes and I¡¯m not saying you shouldn¡¯t go, but stating they¡¯re close to a Michelin Star is to my opinion overselling it. At least for the situation today. I do believe they can grow.
label: Resident

name: xhoena prenga
review:
I visited Restaurant Gheni and I was really impressed. All the dishes I tried were made of high quality ingredients. The combinations were flawless! Everything was perfectly cooked and full of flavour! The service was excellent. Everyone was super friendly, attentive and really professional.
I definitely recommend!
label: Resident

name: Philine Cloet
review: I had an amazing dinner at this restaurant! The food was really amazing, especially the ravioli with shrimps. The staff is beyond friendly and welcoming. I would definitely recommend this place & come back the next time I visite Venice.
label: Resident

name: Zo? Lamming
review: Absolutely beautiful seafood! Very small and cosy, staff were excellent- attentive and knew the menu very thoroughly. The menu is small and there are daily dishes based on what seafood they can get locally. I would thoroughly recommend for some delicious local and inventive seafood.
label: Resident

name: Colin Smith
review: The whole menu is Venetian style seafood and the seafood here was quite good.   The branzino and sea bass both tasted very fresh and had very nice cream/butter/herbacious sauces to go with them. The tiramisu was delicious. The service was friendly and helpful. The location outside was right on one of the waterways. My wife and I thoroughly enjoyed the meal, I'd recommend to anyone.
label: Resident

name: Julie Snyder
review: Best food we had in Venice by far. Quiet and beautiful setting with just 10 tables inside and ac! You could also sit outside on the canal. The waiter, Diego was incredible. We started our meal with a complimentary glass of presseco. Every dish was fresh and delicious and they were very accommodating of my son¡¯s allergies. Highly suggest going here. Made a reservation in advance although was probably not necessary.
label: Resident

name: Vlad Ivancu
review: I had a glass of Prosecco and the pineapple & lime cheesecake at Moro, and it was a truly delightful experience. The dessert was beautifully presented, topped with confit pineapple and even a touch of gold leaf ¨C because clearly, I deserve a little treasure on my plate. The flavors were fresh, light, and perfectly balanced, pairing wonderfully with the sparkling Prosecco. The elegant atmosphere and attention to detail made it feel like a special treat. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a refined and memorable dining experience in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Victoria K
review:
A Hidden Gem in Venice ¨C Exceptional Service!

My friend and I stumbled upon Moro while exploring Venice, and we couldn¡¯t have been more pleasantly surprised. From the moment we walked in, the staff went above and beyond ¡ª incredibly kind, attentive, and thoughtful. When mosquitos started flying in, they even offered us bug spray without hesitation! The food was beautifully presented and delicious, and the view added a perfect touch to the whole experience. Truly standout service ¡ª highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Daniel Calamonte
review:
Maybe ¡­ one of the most beautiful restaurants in the world

It is quite impossible to eat bad in Italy ¡­ if you are not going to. 3 stars Michelin where the food is not more recognizable because it is pure chemistry. This is not the case . Short list is plate, all of them delicious¡­ wine list something readable and  comprehensible. It is not cheap but it is impossible to have better views .
label: Resident

name: Dave
review:
After a very hot morning sightseeing in Venice, we were really pleased to come across this attractive restaurant with a shady, cool garden ¡ª a perfect place to escape the heat.

The lunch menu offered a good selection of lighter dishes, and I chose the Pinsa Romana with datterino, Stracciatella, guanciale, and pesto. It was a very tasty crusty focaccia topped with sweet yellow tomato pur¨¦e, rich guanciale, and a vibrant pesto ¡ª very nice and well-prepared.

The setting was lovely and the food was genuinely impressive. Unfortunately, the service let the experience down. Despite having around five members of waiting staff, there seemed to be no coordination at all ¡ª the food took a very long time to arrive, and we even saw other tables leave after waiting too long just to get a menu.

Such a shame, as this place has great potential, but the chaotic service really detracted from the overall experience.
label: Resident

name: Emily Betzler
review: Bravissim!!  Best lunch in Venice!!  You can¡¯t beat the scenery, the food, or the service!! You will not regret dining here! The Saor was absolutely fantastic one of the best dishes we have had.
label: Resident

name: hessa I
review: The food was absolutely amazing the best breakfast in Venice. It was full of flavor and beautifully prepared. The staff made the place feel so welcoming and friendly. A special thanks Imran for his outstanding hospitality and warm attitude, and to Mahmoud for his wonderful care. They both made the experience truly unforgettable! Highly recommended
label: Resident

name: ¨¢nh Ti¨ºn
review:
Soo relaxing, beautiful and chill.
PERFECT cafe and cakery , alll the cakes are soo delicious and well-made.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review:
We got to Venice very late and decided to checkout this Panini place. Unfortunately at 8:00 PM they were all out of cicchetti which we were looking forward to trying with the sandwich. I saw many other places still had them, but it is more of a early day food.

With that said, we ended up getting a panini and it tasted really good, I asked for it to be toasted and the server ended up having a conversation with locals while the food was cooking, so as you can see, it got burnt. Overall, it tasted good, but the service left a lot to be desired.
label: Resident

name: Eric Witiw
review: The cicchetti were awesome and the staff are so friendly and kind. Great place to stop for a coffee and some food!
label: Resident

name: Emily Jarrard
review: Best cappuccino and croissant I¡¯ve had in Italy so far! The two women working were lovely and the vibes sitting outside were perfect :)
label: Resident

name: Jordi E
review:
We had a superb evening at Wist¨¨ria. If you¡¯ve read a few online takes suggesting the food is ¡°whacky¡± or veers into shock value, rest easy: this is imaginative Venetian cooking that stays coherent and delicious. The tasting menu riffs on local tradition with contemporary technique, aiming for contrast and harmony rather than gimmicks¡ªand that¡¯s exactly how it eats.

Two dishes deserve special mention. First, the red mullet¡ªbeautifully handled and deeply flavorful¡ªwas the standout of the night. Second, the truffle/hazelnut/mushroom dessert was one of the most unique (and successful) endings I¡¯ve had anywhere: flavors you wouldn¡¯t normally expect in dessert, woven into something elegant rather than odd for odd¡¯s sake.

Service was top-notch: attentive, friendly, and genuinely funny, with every course and technique clearly explained in English. The sommelier guided us to an excellent bottle that paired across the full Serendipity menu; if you prefer, there are curated wine pairings for the 6- or 8-course menus, and wines by the glass as well.

The setting matches the cooking. Wist¨¨ria sits on a quiet canal in central San Polo, an easy walk from major sights yet blissfully calm; the room includes thoughtful Venetian touches (think Murano glass accents) and feels distinctive to place rather than generic ¡°fine dining.¡±

In short: a Michelin-starred restaurant that earns the star not with theatrics but with clarity of flavor, craft, and hospitality. Book it for a special night in Venice¡ªI¡¯d happily return.
label: Resident

name: Cole L
review:
My second year coming. It was the best meal of the year 2024 and they knocked it out of the park again. This year I came with 2 kids 8/12 and the team did better than my best hopes. They fed the kids quickly and with a care most restaurants can¡¯t do for even there regulars. Out of 10 it was a 15. Things they didn¡¯t have on the menu were crafted and brought for them

Now for the adults meals amazing every bit was carefully crafted to be a complete dish in ways few can do for a single dish
label: Resident

name: Dolim Lee
review: Wonderful dining experience.  It was a great choice to celebrate a birthday. The restaurant felt elegant, and the indoor dining space felt intimate.  Staff were friendly and welcoming. We had Serendipity 8 instead of 6, as we felt the former had more interesting dishes included, although an 8-course meal usually is a bit too much food. Dishes were creative and beautifully presented. If I have to choose, fruits in the sea and autumn lasagna were my favorites.
label: Resident

name: Yogisan
review: Amazingly unique and creative blend of local and exotic ingredients.  We had the 8 course menu with a superior wine pairing.  If you are in Venice and want to try something amazing, go to Wisteria.
label: Resident

name: Bill Alber
review: This a great local restaurant serving local specialties. If you're staying at the Hyatt Centric or another hotel nearby, don't miss this. The food is creative and fabulous. The staff was friendly, knowledgeable, and just the right amount of attentive. We had a raw seafood platter to start followed by wonderful mushroom and scallop risotto. Slow roasted beef and stuffed sea bass for entrees. The wine they recommended was world class delicious. This was a reasonably priced, world class dining experience.
label: Resident

name: Patrick Morgan
review:
Could not recommend enough. The food was unreal, I would strongly suggest the seafood platter, or the risotto.

A table right on the canal surrounded by Murano glass, and drinking from it. Couldn't be better.

Service was perfect. As a hospitality manager in the UK I was very impressed, particularly  how they dealt with a rude countrymen of mine who didn't realise closed means closed. They were very courteous and polite despite the described people being a pain.

Would recommend to anyone coming to Murano.
label: Resident

name: Tyronne Toh
review: Really enjoyed the food - eggplant pie was amazing and so was the fettuccine with salmon. The friendly staff were the cherry on top; made us feel welcome and even got to try some sweet wine on the house. Will definitely swing by again if back in the area!
label: Resident

name: lili dodokova
review:
The cutest little restaurant hidden in the streets of Venice.. we just stumbled upon it while walking around on our first night in Venice and we just had to come back for a second time..

The service was amazing! The restaurant itself is very nice and cozy ..and the food?? i was blown away.. especially the tiramisu! - i would say it was the best dessert i¡¯ve EVER had -( if one day i come back to Venice, this would definitely be one of the reasons.. )
Overall very fresh, homemade and amazingly delicious food ?? thank you!!
label: Resident

name: Joseph Paparella
review:
After a long day of exploring Venice we had dinner at L¡¯Osteria San Barnaba. We arrived late for our reservation, but the staff welcomed us warmly and never rushed us.

I ordered the cuttlefish ink pasta, and it was extraordinary. The sauce clung to the noodles like velvet, rich and briny with the taste of the sea, faintly metallic in the best way. It even stained my tongue and fingers as I used bread to wipe up the last drops. My wife chose the homemade gnocchi in spider crab sauce, pillowy-soft dumplings bathed in a silky, flavorful sauce that she called some of the best gnocchi she¡¯s ever had.

We were there late into the night with glasses of Fratelli Brunello grappa, comparing tasting notes and enjoying the staff¡¯s patience with my horrible Italian. Our server even smiled through my slow, deliberate attempt at a full sentence, encouraging me as if to say, ¡°You¡¯ve got this.¡± We laughed and made friends with a French couple at the next table, clinked glasses, and ended the night with a shared toast of limoncello.

L¡¯Osteria San Barnaba is the kind of place where food, wine, and warmth all come together. From the extraordinary pasta to the genuine hospitality, it¡¯s a dinner we¡¯ll be talking about for years.
label: Resident

name: Matthew Ho
review: We had a wonderful experience tonight. The waiter out the front was welcoming. We booked through the fork. There is a cover charge, but they brought out bread which was part of this, and also gave a spirit ?limoncello at the end on the house. We had the squid ink pasta and the Vongole which were both exquisite, as well as the cod polenta entree. Tiramisu was the best we had. Highly recommend.
label: Resident

name: Sage Bujold
review: The house wine was delicious (5/glass) and I tried new dishes I never saw before and was hoping to find in Venice (as the skid ink pasta, which was great). The eggplant apetizer was twice as big, but I only took a picture once I had my share. They charge water, but they offered bread basket and sweet wine, and the service was perfect. We had a great time.
label: Resident

name: Marcus
review: The restaurant was very traditional and loaded with fine dining aspects to modern vendig culture. The true feeling of Italy is felt through the nice Italian wine and traditional serving of salads with bread and olive oil with some cracker bread to go with it.  Serving time with just us alone in the restaurant was about 20 minutes. Seems that even noodles are on, made to order basis, not bad, that's good. We had spaghetti carbonara and spaghetti with clams. Both tasted very good and was made the traditional way. In other words it was worth the wait!  I would recommend this restaurant. The waiter is very nice too, funny as well.
label: Resident

name: Freddy Heng
review:
We walked over to this area 1 night for late dinner. Did not know where to eat.

Was looking at the menu and price when owner came out and greet us. We decided to give it a shot since it's not expensive.

Food was great, bread and olive oil was the nicest we ever had. We are in Rome now and before that we were in Florence but we still miss the food here in Venice.

Can't say it's the best in Venice but 100% sure it's 1 of the best and really worth the price.

We only ordered 1 mains but we went in at 9pm still feeling very full from a late lunch and we still feel the food was so nice. Imagine if we were super hungry.. Total we paid 58 for the food in the pictures.
label: Resident

name: Greg
review:
Acquerello was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The beef tartare amuse-bouche was a perfect opener and the pasta dishes were spot on. All our dishes were cooked beautifully and full of flavour.

What makes it extra special is the setting, right on the edge of the lagoon with incredible views across to Venice. A magical dining experience at the wonderful San Clemente Palace.
label: Resident

name: Hasan Karada?
review: The best view in Venice!
label: Resident

name: Laureline Desabres
review:
Diner devant la mer et le coucher de soleil, splendide vue sur Venise. Il y a une navette gratuite pour acc¨¦der au restorant, les horaires sont envoy¨¦es par Whatsapp au moment de la r¨¦servation. Le personnel ¨¦tait tres gentil, et nous nous sommes r¨¦gal¨¦s. Si vous voulez un diner romantique au coucher de soleil avec une petite ballade en bateau avec, allez-y, on a ador¨¦ !!
Attention, prenez de l¡¯anti-moustique :)
label: Resident

name: martina vettorello
review:
Volete vivere una serata da favola a Venezia? Lasciatevi trasportare nell'isola di San Clemente  dal motoscavo privato al ristorante Acquerello. Vista sulla laguna e fuori dal caos della citt¨¤.
Cena di livello molto elevato, il maitre che ci ha descritto tutti i piatti e ci ha raccontato la storia dell'isola, il pane appena sfornato.. tutto ha contribuito a creare un'atmosfera magica.
label: Resident

name: Helmut Pfeiffer
review:
Wenn es was Besonderes sein soll. "Ein wahres Juwel in der Lagune"
Das Kempinski Hotel auf der kleinen Privatinsel bietet h?chsten Erholungswert in einzigartiger Lage. Besonders hervorzuheben ist die au?ergew?hnliche Freundlichkeit des Personals ¨C aufmerksam, herzlich und stets hilfsbereit. Der kostenlose Motorbootshuttle verbindet die Insel komfortabel mit dem MArkusplatz. Das Fr¨¹hst¨¹ck ist ?u?erst vielf?ltig und von hervorragender Qualit?t, ebenso wie das kulinarische Angebot am Abend ¨C geschmacklich ein Highlight! Der gro?z¨¹gige Poolbereich und der erstklassige Service machen jeden Aufenthalt zu einem unvergesslichen Erlebnis. Uneingeschr?nkt empfehlenswert!
label: Resident

name: Ran Levari
review:
Great restaurant ! Apparently this place has been around for 10 years which is very impressive for Venice, this being the ONLY completely Vegan restaurant in town.
As usual, we were so quick with the tasty food that we forgot to take photos of some of the dishes¡­ we got the lasagna and ravioli plus both deserts - all were great but missed taking a picture of the special home made pasta in onions, capers and sea weed - a vegan version of a local venetian dish, which was excellent!

Great service - nice and patient, located quiet and relaxed part of town near a water-bus station.
Highly recommended ??
label: Resident

name: Virginia Lin
review: Friendly staff and great food. We came after recommendations from some local friends. Got lots of different starters and mains as well as the cheese plate.
label: Resident

name: Denny J
review: Absolutely wonderful. Local ingredients which mentions on the menu where they are sourced from.  Not your typical recipes yet sticking to Venetian ingredients.  Want something that isn¡¯t served at every restaurant in Venice yet tastes like Venice?  This is your place.
label: Resident

name: Kero Chan
review:
Cover charge EUR2.5 per person.

Walked in on a weekday evening without reservation. Only 2 staff were serving so service was slow and it was quite difficult to get help or attention from staff.

The Fiorentina Burger used 100% beef patty but was lacking some seasonings.

The Fish Burger was not how I pictured it to be based on the menu description. There should have been a 'fried cod fillet' and 'tartar sauce' placed on a cuttlefish ink bread. Instead, the 'fillet' was a minced fish patty which had no texture at all. I did not enjoy the taste especially when it was mixed with the bitterness of the rocket.

The chips were freshly fried and served hot.

If you desperately need a break from Italian pasta and pizza this may be a place to go but I certainly would recommend trying other items on the menu.
label: Resident

name: Mikhail Yevdokimov
review:
We visited Zoja for dinner during our stay in Venice. Booking through TheFork provided a pleasant 20% discount, which made the final bill more reasonable for this type of venue.

The food was well-prepared, with fresh ingredients and balanced flavors. Presentation was neat and thoughtful. Service was attentive without being intrusive ¡ª the staff deserves credit for maintaining a polite and professional tone throughout the evening.

On the downside, the restaurant lacks any notable view ¡ª no canals or scenic surroundings. The interior is understated, and being located inside a hotel, the atmosphere feels more private and quiet rather than distinctly Venetian.

Overall, a solid choice for a relaxed dinner with good service and well-executed dishes.
label: Resident

name: Francisco Marin
review: We had a wonderful Sunday lunch at the Radisson Collection Hotel. Malia and Celeste took great care of us and delighted us with their charm and yummy cocktails. With The Fork offer, I would say it is a bargain for Venice, since the location is one of a kind and they have a marvelous garden to chill. Keep up the great work! A++
label: Resident

name: Masoud Tavazoei
review: Got our dinner here and Alessandro was our server. The food tasted really well and Alessandro was very attentive and accommodating!
label: Resident

name: Almendado
review: the night we went it was a nice escape of the business of the city. the service was exceptional, i could compare it to that of top resorts. the food was also great. overall, i would recommend for those searching a more secluded and quiet dinner
label: Resident

name: Andrei Pupaza
review: Great place, very good food & excellent service (special thanks to the 2 Annas, Alina & Larisa)!
label: Resident

name: ralph hogg
review: We noticed the Zoha Bar with its cool decor as we passed one day and returned for a cocktail  . Alessandro really looked after us , served fabulous classic cocktails and provided 5 star service . We will return to stay and enjoy dinner !
label: Resident

name: Marc
review: We enjoyed a delicious regular dinner during our stay, and the following day we indulged in an exceptional New Year's Eve menu. We are especially grateful to Bobo, who convinced us to join the taxi boat excursion to witness the fireworks ¨C a decision that proved to be the highlight of our time in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Ivy Zhu
review: Hotel staff rude and most importantly the restaurant charged higher prices than what was listed on the menu! Didn¡¯t bother going back to argue as we had already left and it was only 5-10 but very dishonest practices.
label: Resident

name: qlv assoc
review: Located on the ground floor of JW Marriott Venice. It offers continental breakfast with buffet style for guests. Their expresso is among the best. The freshly squeezed orange juice is so good. Like the pickles - perfect pairing with other items. There is a section of freshly baked pastries. During peak hours, the place is pretty busy as expected.
label: Resident

name: Aimee F
review:
We had no idea this was the same place as breakfast. It is closed random days so every day we tried to make a reservation, they would then call us back and say not open.

It would be nice if this had outdoor seating which it did not when we were there.

The pizza was ok. The burrata appetizer was amazing. The service was ok. We had a reservation and waited in line and got to the front and the guy disappeared and we just stood there. For like 10 mins or more with no one coming. We finally sat down and no one brought water etc for at least 10-15 mins.

Then we realized, there was an awful table sat near us. I am guessing they were from New Jersey. They kept changing their minds on what they wanted for their kid and then she was snapping her fingers and demanding it be made quickly. Then, they hated the pasta and told the Italians how to make pasta!!! Meanwhile, their kids were horrible and obnoxious and screaming and they ran off about 5 tables. The wait staff was beside themselves and trying to placate them. They offered free food etc and they were screaming and crying it wasn¡¯t enough. Our service was terrible but we felt so bad for them bc this family (it seemed to be two families) were so so awful. And the lady had the nerve to say they had bit their tongue long enough..for what?? She was horrific and so were her kids. So bless them for trying to handle that situation and they were very professional about it.
label: Resident

name: Ngo Limit
review: This place is lovely! Service was excellent and accomodating... Just simply wonderful overall!!
label: Resident

name: Orchid Dew (JP)
