label: Resident

name: Christopher Chung
review: Exceptional menu offerings: raw fish trio appetizer and duck tortello were flavorful and modern. Smartly decorated restaurant interior evokes a sense of celebrity, but refined taste. Beautiful bar. A great place to enjoy a special occasion or an elevated experience.
label: Resident

name: Monserrath Flores
review:
So good!! We got the beef samosas, chicken tikka masala, rice, garlic + cheese naan and salad (which is more like raw veggies)

Everything was so good! The cheese naan so yummy and the chicken tikka masala. I will definitely return if I come back to Venice!
label: Resident

name: Dominique Dirks
review:
What an absolute gem of a restaurant! The flavours were out of this world ¨C every bite was rich, authentic, and perfectly balanced. The service was just as impressive: warm, attentive, and genuinely friendly, making the whole experience even better. And the portions? Generous without compromising on quality ¨C you¡¯ll definitely leave satisfied!

This place is a must-visit if you love Indian food. I can¡¯t wait to come back!
label: Resident

name: pradstar P
review: The food at Ital India was delicious.  The flavours of the spices was authentic.  And one of the best Indian restaurant I've eaten at.  The decor of the restaurant is very nice. Staff are also friendly.  I would recommend eating here if your into Indian food.
label: Resident

name: Helene Rosalie Jordan
review: Amazing  culinary experience.  Food was TOPS, service and atmosphere excellent.  Abundant portions, all delicious.  Definitely would return!
label: Resident

name: Sravya Garimella
review: Great food! Very authentic and yummy! Portion sizes and prices are very reasonable. Great service and good atmosphere - seating available inside and outside. Definitely recommend !
label: Resident

name: Aitor Godoy
review:
One of the best Indian restaurants I have ever been to, not only in Italy, but also in India. The butter chicken was our favorite, but everything was so great!
I would recommend it to anyone!
label: Resident

name: Ram Prakash Palaniswamy
review:
We started with the Aloo Tikki (3 small pieces) but too much of chat masala.
For mains, the Karahi Paneer with naan was good;
the naan bread was especially soft and puffy ¨C a solid 5-star!

However, I wouldn't recommend the Vegetable Biryani ¨C the inclusion of what seemed like butter beans was quite unexpected for a biryani.
We finished with a refreshing lime soda.
label: Resident

name: Erin Lowe
review:
We had such a wonderful experience at Conca D¡¯Oro! The food was absolutely delicious ¡ª the cacio e pepe was creamy and perfectly seasoned, the spaghetti bolognese was rich and comforting, and the tiramisu was the perfect finish to the meal.

Our server Anna was excellent ¡ª attentive, kind, and made us feel right at home. The host Sean greeted us with great energy and a sharp sense of humor, and he might just be one of the best salespeople in the business!

Highly recommend this spot for anyone who loves authentic Italian food and genuine hospitality.
label: Resident

name: Kyle O'Neill
review:
Best meal we had in Italy!  I know, I know, but trust me, it¡¯s true.  We had the Greek Salad (Insert Italian finger/hand chefs kiss emoji), the Pork Chop/Rib (it¡¯s really an amazingly giant rib¡­again with the chefs kiss emoji), the parpadelle with bolognese (yet another chefs kiss emoji), and the pizza with mushrooms, sausage, and egg (and yes, another chefs kiss), and to wash it all down, a bottle of Amarone Classico (this is getting silly, but ANOTHER chefs kiss).  Best of all, every dish came recommended by Sean (a local Venetian who grew up just around the corner).

We¡¯ve had a lot of great meals, and great wines, and meals with great wines, but no meal compared to this one.  If you¡¯re looking for a great meal in Venice, go talk to Sean¡­He¡¯ll definitely take care of you!  I¡¯m jealous of your meal already.
label: Resident

name: No?mie Gala
review:
An absolute disgrace. We were ¡°welcomed¡± by the so-called manager in jeans, a baseball cap, and sunglasses ¡ª even though the weather was dull and gloomy. Zero professionalism, zero respect. This man spends his time yelling at passersby, telling them not to eat at the restaurant next door because they¡¯re ¡°cheap,¡± while boasting that his place is ¡°the first pizzeria in Venice¡± and ¡°famous.¡± What a joke.
I ordered two salads and truffle ravioli. The salads? Empty, tasteless, a complete insult. The ravioli? Not even a real dish, just a pathetic starter: 6 pieces of pasta (yes, SIX!) on the plate. And all of this for 70 ! Outrageous and completely unacceptable.
And don¡¯t even get me started on the bathrooms: catastrophic. The doors don¡¯t close properly, and the men¡¯s and women¡¯s toilets are directly opposite each other. If you are a woman and need to go while a man is there, he can see you. Totally disrespectful and shocking.
Do not go here if you¡¯re hungry, do not go if you enjoy real food, and above all, do not go if you expect to be treated with a minimum of respect.
label: Resident

name: Doris Kole
review: This was the most peaceful and enjoyable aperitivo I have ever had. The place is absolutely gorgeous and calming, and the staff is exceptional. You can enjoy incredible views of Venice¡ªaway from the busy streets and fast pace of the city. I absolutely loved my time here.
label: Resident

name: Marco Duarte
review:
I had high expectations dining at a restaurant in a 5-star hotel like JW Marriott, but the service was far below what one would expect¡ªespecially at the price point. Basic attention to detail was completely lacking.

We were a table of seven, including a child, yet the staff didn¡¯t acknowledge the need to serve the child first¡ªsomething any quality establishment should instinctively recognize. Starters were brought out and placed randomly wherever there was space, with no consideration for presentation or explanation. No one even bothered to ask if the starters were meant to be shared between all and help to do it. Also chairs very uncomfortable

For a hotel that carries the JW Marriott name, this level of service is simply unacceptable. I hope the management seriously reviews the training and standards expected of their staff, because this experience was far from the premium service they should have.
label: Resident

name: leila abaab
review: Excellent service and food! Everyone was so attentive. I¡¯m a platinum elite member so we were offered free cicchettis too. We were there for dinner and the view was breathtaking. They offered blankets because it was a little chilly. Expect many bugs as this is normal in Venice, so make sure to use some OFF before going ?? otherwise everything was perfect !
label: Resident

name: Amaury S
review:
We were really excited for Sagra and truly were expecting more given its exclusive location and price point. Unfortunately, it wasn¡¯t one of the best meals we had in Venice. We felt that the menu was limited and although I appreciated its creativity, it felt very expensive for what we got. It was the first meal in Venice where we left a bit hungry after paying a pretty penny!

We had the torta as an appetizer and it was just too small. For entrees, I had the pasta with octopus and my partner had bucatini with meatballs. My pasta was delicious and so was my partner¡¯s, but portions were more on the smaller side. Thankfully mine was a bit bigger, but his had more meatballs than pasta. Come on, Sagra, no need to be stingy with the pasta.

Service was really good but the whole experience felt rushed. We were done with dinner in no time. One of our favorite things about Europe is being able to take our time dining and not be rushed, but it felt quite opposite here. Also ¡ª it¡¯s ridiculous that they charge per person for the table/to make a reservation. The views are great but at night you can¡¯t see much.
label: Resident

name: Francis
review:
Came here for lunch once in the inside part and we both had the burger. It was delicious and the fries were also super good. Service was great and yes overall it was pricey but the convenience of not having to take the shuttle was worth it since the burgers were so good.

We came back later in our stay for dinner after a long day of sightseeing but this time we sat on the outside patio. The service again was fantastic but this time we got a welcome drink which was delicious and an arancini amuse bouche which again was fantastic. The bread provided was just ok since we thought the crust was too hard but the basket came with some amazing focaccia bread.

For the mains we had the pappardelle al ragu and the seafood spaghetti. Both were fantastic! Portions were on the smaller side but with the bread it wasn't an issue. For dessert we tried the tiramisu experience and the lemon tart. The lemon tart filling was good but the shell was way too hard. The tiramisu was definitely the highlight though and was one of the best I've ever had. The fact that it's made tableside was also neat so I would highly recommend it.
label: Resident

name: Olga van der Vlist
review:
Delicious food! Great service!
One thing we didn¡¯t like it¡¯s super bright for dinner setting. It would be nice, more romantic and cozy with light dimmer.
label: Resident

name: Rachel S
review:
This is honestly the best gluten free pizza I've had in Venice! No way could I tell the difference in pizza base.
The service was attentive, the food came out quickly and was heavenly.
If I had more time in Venice, I would be going back.
The location is perfect. The outside seating was very picturesque and not on the main thoroughfare.
5 stars all round.
label: Resident

name: Mikhail Kazansev
review:
It¡¯s probably the most ¡°italiano vero¡± place I¡¯ve ever visited. Many thanks to the wonderful Marco, who gave us great advice and even helped us improve our Italian. It was a truly fabulous experience.
The food was absolutely delicious, and we had never seen desserts like this before. It was a truly fabulous experience.
label: Resident

name: William Gray
review:
An incredibly-pleasant restaurant that's very close to the main thoroughfare linking the train station with Rialto, yet offers better quality fare and atmosphere compared to the myriad of eateries directly lining the street.

Spent several dinners here, enjoying the excellent service by the staff and the delicious food. Fare is a good mixture of local and national cuisine, with thoughtful portion sizes for the primi and secondi dishes.

The interior dining room is calm and cosy, allowing for a relaxed dining experience. A reliable, quality eatery that you don't need to trek too far off the beaten path for.
label: Resident

name: Robert Artac
review: Cuttlefish ink pasta was good. I wouldn't do it again but has nothing to do with how it was prepared. The pasta was bang-on al dente. Perfectly prepared. Wine selection was excellent. Very kind waiter and loved his impromptu whistling. Overall would definitely go again.
label: Resident

name: Margaret G
review:
Wow!!!

We stopped in for dinner because this was the only restaurant near our hotel, and we arrived late - but what a treat we were in for! So, so good.

The chef makes everything from scratch daily, so quantities of each dish are limited.  Francesco took the time to explain all the dishes on the menu to us, not just list what was or wasn¡¯t left available at the late hour we arrived. He also offered wine pairing recommendations; we ordered what he suggested, and each was great with the accompanying dish.

Francesco mentioned that the squid ink risotto is a specialty, and I love risotto, so I had to give that a try despite being skeptical about the squid. This is the only time I¡¯ve ever had squid that wasn¡¯t rubbery. It was delicious! 100% worth taking a chance on. My husband had the shrimp & crab ravioli, which he also thoroughly enjoyed. We shared a cheesecake for dessert. It was so good we forgot to take a picture!

The restaurant has not been open long, so some of the signage from the previous restaurant is still on the building. We were confused at first to see the name of a different restaurant on two sides of the building and a menu for that previous restaurant still hanging on the wall by the entrance. There is signage with the new restaurant¡¯s name, and I¡¯m sure that with time, the inconsistency can be addressed. In the meantime, don¡¯t let confusion over the name keep you away from this gem.
label: Resident

name: Jefferson Daniel
review: Stopped in for a quick bite. Vibe and area nice. Service however wasn't very great, and we were like the only ones there. So not sure if it was a culture thing or what. But we were pressed for a little time. So had to constantly urge them along. We had pot stickers, and something else, and those alone were amazing. Id imagine the other food probable just as good.
label: Resident

name: Dan Price
review:
Tried to find it using Google maps
Took us to another restaurant. Doors on two floors both locked at 8 pm on Wednesday. Website error. Called no answer. Must have  closed.
label: Resident

name: Tim
review:
The menu for 15,90 is priced quite fair if you take the right dishes - pasta with clams, grilled salmon or fried potatoes for instance.
The schnitzel on the other side is very triste and the salad also looks quite depressing.
Overall quite fair though if you chose the right food and the great service and included service fee taken into consideration.
label: Resident

name: Ben Gartland
review:
We ate here after finding it on Google maps, we were looking for somewhere to eat that was local to where we were staying.
It's in a lovely area with a vibrant atmosphere at night, surrounded by other bars, cafes, restaurants.
The menu had plenty of choices, and the service was good.
The food was good (not great hence 4 stars), we've had better at other local places, and as others have said the salad was pretty basic (mostly lettuce and carrot).

My spaghetti bolognese was really tasty and a good size dish, but my wife's lasagne lacked the same punch.

There was no cover charge, so what you see on the menu is what you pay.

Yes, we would eat here again without a doubt and are happy to recommend it.

Evening meal for 2 with drinks and sides, about 55 in total.
label: Resident

name: Chew W. C
review: I had an amazing dining experience at this restaurant. I would say it is a hidden gem in Venice City Centre. For just 13.90, I enjoyed a delightful 3-course meal that left me more than satisfied. The portions were generous, especially the homemade pasta and fresh clams. The lasagne was a standout, showcasing delicious homemade flavours. The Margarita pizza impressed with its thin crust and generous cheeses. The attentive service added to the overall enjoyment, and it was popular among the locals. A definite recommendation for anyone visiting Venice!
label: Resident

name: Diana Restrepo Copher
review: Just the place o was dreaming of while in Italy: with a sweet, smooth, tranquil vibe, and food that is made from scratch and with fresh ingredients. We had the best experience with the ¡°menu of the day¡± (affordable and delicious), but their regular menu also looks great. That day, the had the Mediterranean fish, with potatoes and olives, grilled chicken, spaghetti bolognese and penne al pesto with pistachios. Service was excellent, not rushed at all, which we loved. Please note that this is based on Italian culture; they take their time to serve you and strive to not to disturb your experience. I¡¯d return without hesitation!
label: Resident

name: Hannah
review: We were lucky enough to have eaten here for their NYE dinner, the food was amazing! Some of the best food we have had in Italy so far! The restaurant was comfortable and charming and the service was great! Would 100% recommend to anyone looking for a good affordable meal!
label: Resident

name: esther b?dker
review:
The place is nice, good drinks and fast service.
But the pizzas are a joke. A Diavola that isn¡¯t the slightest bit spicy and a potato pizza that in reality is a pizza with fries on top??
label: Resident

name: Angela Boyd
review: The food was AMAZING! We ate there three times in the 5 days we stayed in Venice. The homemade ravioli was mouth-watering and DELICIOUS! The ribs fell off the bone and the prime rib was so tender. Haque and Alvise were the best servers, so friendly and helpful. They spoke great English and were able to give us recommendations that were perfect. Marina, the owner, was so welcoming. I highly recommend this restaurant when you are in Venice. The food, atmosphere, and staff were the best.
label: Resident

name: Simon Thompson
review:
Excellent range to choose from. We had first course only as we weren¡¯t particularly hungry. The Duck, the Ravioli and the Gnocchi. All were delicious. Drinks and food served in a timely manner. Not rushed for table turnover. Good atmosphere
We also had Tiramisu which was very good and I had a great Espresso. Most enjoyable. Grazie mille.
label: Resident

name: guy simhon
review: There were three of us. We ordered a variety of dishes such as Black Angus carpaccio which was creatively laid out and delicately seasoned, tuna tartare with wasabi cracker, a creative, balanced and special dish. Potatoes that were soft but perfect, ravioli with a dreamy pecorino sauce and finally we added a carbonara dish. A family restaurant that serves meticulous but not sophisticated food, balanced and incredibly tasty
label: Resident

name: Rogatina Jane
review: Coperto 3.50 per person which includes nothing, even bread. 63 for 2 mediocre pizzas and 3 drinks sounds like a lot. The pizzas looked pre-cooked (if not heated from frozen). Very poor experience and canteen ambience.
label: Resident

name: Marcus W
review: Great food. Great atmosphere. Fantastic staff. Gluten free pizza was excellent. My wife said it was way better than the regular pizza she had elsewhere. Gnocchi carbonara was delicious (gnocchi is the GF paster option). The upstairs waiter was amazing.. Racing around making everyone happy, speaking in English, French, Italian.. Then broke out a little Japanese for a young Japanese couple.
label: Resident

name: Dakshan Krishnapalan
review: Stumbled upon this restaurant for breakfast and noticed it had great deals so we gave it a try. They have some of the best deals that I¡¯ve seen, I got a fresh orange juice, croissant sandwich with salami and cheese as well as a cappuccino for 8.50 euro. We were served relatively quickly as well and there was no cover charge! Would go here again for sure!
label: Resident

name: Matthew Urbas
review: We had a nice, and most importantly, cheap (for Venice) dinner here. Whilst it's far from a gastronomical master class, it was quite enjoyable. What more, the server took care and attention to my partners milk allergy, and made sure her food wouldn't me contaminated.
label: Resident

name: Ivette Alejandra
review: Delicious and affordable breakfast. We ate here every day of our stay and had the best waiter - I wish I caught his name but he was super friendly and spoke perfect Spanish. Highly recommend for breakfast.
label: Resident

name: Tammi Van Horn
review: Enjoyed breakfast both days of our stay. Service was very friendly. Food was good, prices are on the menu so definitely not a trap as some have stated. We will definitely enjoy again on our next visit.
label: Resident

name: Wisanu Tuntawiroon
review:
I had the pleasure of visiting **bArCollo** in Venice and was impressed by the warm and friendly atmosphere. The staff were welcoming and attentive, making it an enjoyable experience from the start. The breakfast is simple yet satisfying, with a nice mix of options that suit both traditional tastes and those looking for something more familiar.

I particularly enjoyed the omelette, which was cooked to perfection, along with crispy bacon that added just the right amount of flavor. The fresh orange juice and a good cup of coffee rounded out the meal nicely. If you're looking for a cozy spot with a typical yet delicious breakfast, bArCollo is a great choice.
label: Resident

name: Canice Hong
review: Good and cheap food for the area. I got a carbonara for under 10 which felt insane considering everything nearby was 12-15+. It was delish and they bring a a whole jar of parmesan so you can add as much as you like. The rest of the food also looked good. Nice area by the canals but tucked away in a little court of restaurants so it¡¯s not overlooking a canal. Great spot to people watch. They have only a couple bar spots for indoor seating and the rest is outdoors which was nice on a cool May day.
label: Resident

name: Sankha Subhra Ghosh
review: Good place to try Korean and Chinese food. We needed to eat something spicy, affordable and near our hotel where we were staying. Found this place from Google Map. We ordered Grilled Pork Spicy, Lemon chicken and Prawn Fried Rice. Though we specifically asked to make the dishes extra spicy, they didn't do that. But still the taste was above average. I'd definitely recommend the place anyone who is bored with Italian food and want to try something spicy.
label: Resident

name: Jing Huang
review:
I tried this Korean-Chinese restaurant, it was a really good experience. The place was super clean, and the food came out pretty fast, which I appreciated.

The best thing we had was the spicy pork ¡ª I think it¡¯s called jeyuk bokkeum. It came out sizzling on a hot stone plate with onions, plus lettuce leaves and a bowl of rice on the side. The flavor was amazing ¡ª spicy, savory, and satisfying.

We ordered a bunch of different dishes, and honestly, everything tasted good. There wasn¡¯t a single thing I¡¯d tell you to skip.

Clean space, quick service, very reasonable price.
label: Resident

name: Alexandra Polisevschi
review:
The food here is really good, a nice variety after eating everyday classic Italian food.

We stopped for a quick and light dinner (my son loves plain rice, which is difficult to find in European restaurants). I took the dumpling soup, which was also very good.

The restaurant is small and cozy, with a pleasant atmosphere. I appreciated that they had real, reusable chopsticks and even had a special holder for them ¡ª a nice touch you don¡¯t see often.

The staff is kind and helpful. We had small difficulties ordering, however the owner speaks very good English and quickly helped out.
label: Resident

name: Evgenii
review:
Came here with zero expectations, but with a huge hope to eat something different from pasta & pizza. As a fan of asian cuisine, and korean in particular, I can say that this place is a pearl in the ocean of mediocre asian-style restaurants adapted to EU market. I strongly recommend this place for its quality and true korean cuisine taste.
Thank you, it was a real pleasure.
P.S.: my bowl is empty because the bibimbap was too good to wait until I'm done with taking pictures.
label: Resident

name: Jason Yoo
review: I was pretty hungover from last night and the ???? healed me. The ???? came with ?? and ??. Considering this is Korean food in Europe the food was great. Korean approved lol.
label: Resident

name: G
review: One of the best pasta i had! So good. Waiters were all very nice.
label: Resident

name: Maurice Sheehan
review:
Hidden down a little sidestreet this restaurant was lovely.It has a full menu of local dishes and more internationally know Italian cuisine. The staff are more than friendly and it's reasonably priced compared to the main Venice restaurants.
We had a cosy lunch here before leaving Murano and we were really disappointed that we had only discovered this place on our final day in Venice.
The spaghetti bolognese was delicious and there's a vegetarian version also. For dessert the pastry with custard and raspberry was divine.
If your in Venice and are planning on a visit to Murano this is a good place to eat.
label: Resident

name: Mark K
review: Best lunch we had in Venice. Great service, food, and prices. A godsend on a hot hot day. Try the special pasta. You know the place is great when next to you is a table full of elderly Italian people having a great big meal.
label: Resident

name: Vanessa Ip
review:
This restaurant is just off the main street in Murano. The outdoor seating is in a pleasant little courtyard and they also have indoor seating available if you want.

There is a good selection of food options and the portions are quite large. Everyone should be able to find a dish they enjoy! Everything is reasonably priced, so this is a good stop if you're visiting Murano.
label: Resident

name: Prapti Patel
review: What a wonderful find while visiting Murano! The owner is welcoming, hospitable, and the food is amazing. Easily the best pasta dishes we've had in Italy so far! Don't miss stopping here if you are in Murano.
label: Resident

name: Erin Lowe
review:
We had such a wonderful experience at Conca D¡¯Oro! The food was absolutely delicious ¡ª the cacio e pepe was creamy and perfectly seasoned, the spaghetti bolognese was rich and comforting, and the tiramisu was the perfect finish to the meal.

Our server Anna was excellent ¡ª attentive, kind, and made us feel right at home. The host Sean greeted us with great energy and a sharp sense of humor, and he might just be one of the best salespeople in the business!

Highly recommend this spot for anyone who loves authentic Italian food and genuine hospitality.
label: Resident

name: Kyle O'Neill
review:
Best meal we had in Italy!  I know, I know, but trust me, it¡¯s true.  We had the Greek Salad (Insert Italian finger/hand chefs kiss emoji), the Pork Chop/Rib (it¡¯s really an amazingly giant rib¡­again with the chefs kiss emoji), the parpadelle with bolognese (yet another chefs kiss emoji), and the pizza with mushrooms, sausage, and egg (and yes, another chefs kiss), and to wash it all down, a bottle of Amarone Classico (this is getting silly, but ANOTHER chefs kiss).  Best of all, every dish came recommended by Sean (a local Venetian who grew up just around the corner).

We¡¯ve had a lot of great meals, and great wines, and meals with great wines, but no meal compared to this one.  If you¡¯re looking for a great meal in Venice, go talk to Sean¡­He¡¯ll definitely take care of you!  I¡¯m jealous of your meal already.
label: Resident

name: No?mie Gala
review:
An absolute disgrace. We were ¡°welcomed¡± by the so-called manager in jeans, a baseball cap, and sunglasses ¡ª even though the weather was dull and gloomy. Zero professionalism, zero respect. This man spends his time yelling at passersby, telling them not to eat at the restaurant next door because they¡¯re ¡°cheap,¡± while boasting that his place is ¡°the first pizzeria in Venice¡± and ¡°famous.¡± What a joke.
I ordered two salads and truffle ravioli. The salads? Empty, tasteless, a complete insult. The ravioli? Not even a real dish, just a pathetic starter: 6 pieces of pasta (yes, SIX!) on the plate. And all of this for 70 ! Outrageous and completely unacceptable.
And don¡¯t even get me started on the bathrooms: catastrophic. The doors don¡¯t close properly, and the men¡¯s and women¡¯s toilets are directly opposite each other. If you are a woman and need to go while a man is there, he can see you. Totally disrespectful and shocking.
Do not go here if you¡¯re hungry, do not go if you enjoy real food, and above all, do not go if you expect to be treated with a minimum of respect.
label: Resident

name: Shannon Renfro
review: We stopped for smoothies while we were out walking and then I ordered delivery that night since the food looked so fresh and tasty. After so much pasta and pizza this was such a nice light and fresh meal to enjoy a rice bowl with chicken and veggies. The website was easy enough to order from and it arrived on time in the delivery window.
label: Resident

name: F Whiteley
review: Popped in for lunch for a pleasant change from pizza and pasta and had a good experience. The service was quick and the lady spoke good English as my Italian is very limited. The food is nice and seems fresh. I paid 10.50 for a regular bowl which was enough for me, I didn¡¯t get a drink as they were quite expensive. Only downside was they had tables but no charge to be able to eat in.
label: Resident

name: Joshua Acosta
review:
Overall this wasn't a bad experience but it wasn't a star worthy experience, in my opinion.

The service was excellent.  The room was partitioned so the waiter had to keep popping in which gave the illusion of feeling rushed, but it wasn't. The 5 glass wine pairing was excellent, albeit the pours were light considering the 70 euro price point.

The food was mostly uninspiring and safe.  The most thought provoking dishes were the fowl ravioli & the ice cream.  The potatoes on the rabbit & potato course lacked seasoning.

All in all, it was just shy of a satisfactory experience.  I left questioning my choice to come here.
label: Resident

name: Mert EMRE
review:
Il Ridotto has an intimate setting and delicate plates, but my experience was mixed.

The starters and desserts were the highlights, innovative such as the Negroni with a shark fin garnish stood out as the most inventive touch of the evening. Unfortunately, the risotto was undercooked, with grains still chalky at the core, which took away from the balance of the dish.

Dessert was pleasant and well executed a bit Icelandic frozen yogurt inspired

The real issue was the service: our dinner stretched to nearly four hours, and I¡¯m not exaggerating. Courses arrived painfully slowly, to the point where other tables finished their meals entirely while we were still waiting. The staff remained polite, but the pacing ruined the flow of what could have been a memorable night. I know the restaurant is run by a father-and-son duo, and while the culinary ideas are promising, the service side still needs serious attention.

For me, its a pass in Venice unless they resolve this.
label: Resident

name: Nicolas Arauz
review:
Had a fantastic meal here, highly recommended. It¡¯s expensive, but very much worth the price - go for lunch if you want the best value.

Each course was beautifully presented and delicious. Expect portions to be smaller (plenty!) and the quality to be far above average, with attentive service. Their wine suggestions for local wines were perfectly matched to the meal - I had the Rabbit, my wife enjoyed the fish of the day, and we shared a very nice seafood pasta and a wonderfully simple dessert. Also had a very nice regional Vermouth.
label: Resident

name: Jillian Crisp
review: We always go to Il Ridotto when we visit Venice from Australia. Last Tuesday we very much enjoyed the flavour of the lobster and rabbit dishes - such surprising and delicious tastes. I loved the white wine the waiter chose for me, to go with the lobster. It is fun to try the amuse bouche and small sweet things they give you free at the beginning of the meal and with a hot drink at the end. They found some real mint to make  tea for my daughter and me. They were so kind. Il Ridotto felt like a peaceful haven at the heart of a very busy city.
label: Resident

name: Owen
review:
Il Ridotto provided one of the most extraordinary dining experiences we¡¯ve ever had! We chose the seven-course dinner, and every dish was an absolute masterpiece, beautifully crafted and bursting with flavor. From the delicate starters to the decadent dessert, each plate was a perfect balance of tradition and innovation.

What truly elevated our evening, however, was the exceptional service from our waitress, Sonia. She was warm, attentive, and incredibly knowledgeable, guiding us through the meal with enthusiasm and a genuine passion for the food. Her attention to detail made us feel completely cared for throughout the evening.

The intimate and welcoming ambiance of the restaurant, paired with Sonia¡¯s outstanding hospitality and the impeccable quality of every dish, made this a night to remember. Il Ridotto is a must-visit for anyone in Venice seeking an unforgettable culinary adventure!
label: Resident

name: Andy Nicholls
review:
A really lovely dining experience. I would give it a 4.5 out of 5. We ate from the a la carte menu and it was very tasty. I also had one of the wine trails which was also a nice mix of different white and red wines.

This was a really great meal and I would definitely recommend it. When you go to fine dining restaurants you obviously hold it to a very high standard so 4.5 out of 5 should not be seen as a negative at all. The young man who served us was great and very polite. The only negative in this regard was the service slowed a bit as the restaurant got busier but this is no reflection on how good he was at explaining the food and wines etc.
label: Resident

name: Perspectas
review: We are so grateful to the whole staff for their hospitality. Everything was thought of to the last detail. Can't wait to visit again!
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: Lexie McClellan
review: The food here was excellent! My husband ordered the beef shoulder and I had the pumpkin gnocchi. The plates were tasty and unique compared to some of the other places we¡¯ve eaten at here. The service was top notch with great attention to detail and hospitality. Thank you!
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: UnDiplomatic Wife
review:
I wish I could give the hotel restaurant five stars, but there was some confusion or a mistake by the staff after we ordered because our order arrived about 50 minutes after we were seated. They knew they messed up because the wait staff came to us to say that the food is coming. But for sure it is not normal to eat 1 hour after being seated especially when we had a hungry child. The people who arrived after us were already having their dessert before we were served so I know that this is not the usual quality of service. I think for the delay they should have at least comped or discounted something.

My son & husband shared the 700g steak. They thoroughly enjoyed it and the price was not bad for the amount of food.

I ordered the grilled fish. But I was served pan fried salmon.

Both were almost overcooked¡­ almost but not quite. On the dryer side. So I¡¯ll only remove 1 star for the food.

The gelato especially the hazelnut was great.
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: Parth Thakker
review:
This is the greatest eggplant Parm I've ever had.

Paired with the best whiskey sour I've ever had.

This meal was easily the best I've had in Venice so far. An absolute must.

Zero complaints. 15/10.
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: Patr¨ªcia Valente
review: 5* ! the best restaurant I¡¯ve been to in Venice . Good people & Good food, couldn¡¯t ask for more ?
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: Derrick Ruiz
review: Everything from the food to the drinks were exquisite. The service was excellent, highlighted by the very friendly, Ali! He made us a dessert liqueur, a limoncello and elderflower mix, which topped off a delightful meal for my wife and I. Ali, you¡¯re the best! Grazie!
label: Resident

place_title: Antica Fornace De Majo Restaurant & Terrace
name: Saba S
review: Food and service both were great!
label: Resident

name: Ashley Holmes Millett
review:
Everything was delicious! It¡¯s hard to find truly good food in Venice. This was a find! The street isn¡¯t much to look at, but the table settings and colorful chairs a make up for any lack of ambiance. Perfect service as well.
Watermelon gazpacho, spider crab tagliatelle, a nut crusted fish and strawberry semifreddo for dessert.
label: Resident

name: Diana Cecilia Barahona Rivera
review: Stopped by just for an aperol and spider crab tagliatelle. It was really delicious and I definitely recommend. It¡¯s located in a very calm and colorful street behind the Accademia. Besides, copperto is only 2 so that¡¯s a plus!
label: Resident

name: Martha Bledsoe
review: We stayed at the Hotel Ca¡¯ Pisani late October,¡¯25 and had lunch the first day while we waited to check in. We loved the food and service so much that we went back and ate dinner the last night. They had a nice breakfast buffet each morning. We were specially , especially intrigued with one dish on the starter menu, pumpkin Brul¨¦ ! Nick and Nick were our bartender and server and we had such a fun time with them both.
label: Resident

name: Jeremy Olimb
review: Bartender was awesome. So friendly and made us feel like it was a joy to serve us and talk with us. We will be back.
label: Resident

name: Steve Ralph
review:
I only stopped to enjoy a coffee on the terraced seating at the  front of the restaurant.
So I can not give a rating for the food, but the service was prompt and the coffee delicious.
It was very reasonably priced as well at 3.
label: Resident

name: Byron Pop
review:
Overpriced food and mid quality.
16 for a tiny serving of pasta and 17 for a napoleon pizza! Both ehh and the salads as well. Much better spots elsewhere.
label: Resident

name: Andi Orlowski
review: A little Italian not far from the Acadamia boat stop. A relatively small section of dishes including some authentic local dishes. The food and service was ok and it cost 130 to feed 4 adults and 3 kids (1 main dish and 1 drink per person).
label: Resident

name: Michelle Fish
review:
I have been here twice! Besides a very helpful and funny waiter (Fabio, thank you)..they have an excellent wine list, and the wines by the glass are excellent. The food is a good price quality ratio. I had my first spinach risotto and it was excellent. Pulpo salad was fresh and paired well with its partner ingredients.

We sat outside..its quiet street with excellent people watching. My only recommendation to the restaurant is to update the inside decor....I can see how customers lose the feel of venice in the 80s style decor.
label: Resident

name: Candi Janssen
review:
We stopped by for a quick dinner based on reviews- we greatly enjoyed our meal.

Service was great, very friendly and attentive.

Food was great- I had the vegetable soup for a starter, a nice wholesome meal that I was craving after 11 days in Italy. It was enough for a meal so my husband shared it with me.  My husband had the Sea Bass- it came out whole and the owner/manager cleaned, prepared it for him. It was fresh and very tasty.  He also had the potato chips (French fries), which were fantastic- nice crunch but soft inside. For my meal I had the gnocchi with four cheese- it was spectacular. (I forgot to take a photo until I was half done)

We left - crazy full and very satisfied!
label: Resident

name: Virginia Holcomb
review: My mom and I had a lovely dinner at Anonimo Veneziano on our vacation! We passed it walking around the winding streets and are so glad we came back for dinner. The waiter was very kind and helpful in explaining some of the dishes we had questions about. We enjoyed the scampi with prawns and the spaghetti alla carbonara, both were delicious and fresh! I had a glass of local red wine and like it a lot! And a fun treat was that, he brought us shots of limoncello at the end of our meal! The ambiance was very cozy and nice. Not too fancy, but not casual either. The waiter was prompt but gave us plenty of time to look over the menu and enjoy our meal. If you¡¯re in Venice, go have a meal here, you won¡¯t be disappointed!
label: Resident

name: Lena Lisovsky
review:
We had a late dinner in this restaurant  because it located to next our apartment
It was best branzino i every ate!
Entire fish for 3 people. Carpaccio was basic, lack of  test, we added salt, pepper and lemon , and it was good
.
label: Resident

name: Cinee C.
review: We had a late check-in and was hungry. Found this restaurant nearby our hotel and it opened late. It's a charming little place. Food was good but it didn't have wow factor. Tasted like any Italian restaurant back home. Expect to pay a service fee of 12%.
label: Resident

name: Marin H
review:
We chose here for dinner, only because it was near our hotel and seems lively. We came here by chance, but we had a great night here.

The waiters are very friendly and it was fascinating to see them having fun at work.

We ordered Tagliatelle, Bolognese, and Grilled Sea Bass. Especially the sea bass was so good, which was a recommendation of them!!

Thank you, we¡¯ll come back someday!!
label: Resident

name: Dev W
review: Excellent food. A small alley restaurant but really really good food.   Much better service than La Piazza.  Price is reasonable. Friendly staffs.
label: Resident

name: ymer
review: The food was good but a little expensive and the toilets were clean. The staff did however make us a bit uncomfortable. For example it felt as if they expected us to say something more after we had ordered, it made is feel as if we had said something wrong. Maybe that is on us. The music did also not fit the atmosphere and the person who was connected to the speaker switched the song very often.
label: Resident

name: Margaret G
review:
Wow!!!

We stopped in for dinner because this was the only restaurant near our hotel, and we arrived late - but what a treat we were in for! So, so good.

The chef makes everything from scratch daily, so quantities of each dish are limited.  Francesco took the time to explain all the dishes on the menu to us, not just list what was or wasn¡¯t left available at the late hour we arrived. He also offered wine pairing recommendations; we ordered what he suggested, and each was great with the accompanying dish.

Francesco mentioned that the squid ink risotto is a specialty, and I love risotto, so I had to give that a try despite being skeptical about the squid. This is the only time I¡¯ve ever had squid that wasn¡¯t rubbery. It was delicious! 100% worth taking a chance on. My husband had the shrimp & crab ravioli, which he also thoroughly enjoyed. We shared a cheesecake for dessert. It was so good we forgot to take a picture!

The restaurant has not been open long, so some of the signage from the previous restaurant is still on the building. We were confused at first to see the name of a different restaurant on two sides of the building and a menu for that previous restaurant still hanging on the wall by the entrance. There is signage with the new restaurant¡¯s name, and I¡¯m sure that with time, the inconsistency can be addressed. In the meantime, don¡¯t let confusion over the name keep you away from this gem.
label: Resident

name: Jefferson Daniel
review: Stopped in for a quick bite. Vibe and area nice. Service however wasn't very great, and we were like the only ones there. So not sure if it was a culture thing or what. But we were pressed for a little time. So had to constantly urge them along. We had pot stickers, and something else, and those alone were amazing. Id imagine the other food probable just as good.
label: Resident

name: Dan Price
review:
Tried to find it using Google maps
Took us to another restaurant. Doors on two floors both locked at 8 pm on Wednesday. Website error. Called no answer. Must have  closed.
label: Resident

name: Doris Kole
review: This was the most peaceful and enjoyable aperitivo I have ever had. The place is absolutely gorgeous and calming, and the staff is exceptional. You can enjoy incredible views of Venice¡ªaway from the busy streets and fast pace of the city. I absolutely loved my time here.
label: Resident

name: Marco Duarte
review:
I had high expectations dining at a restaurant in a 5-star hotel like JW Marriott, but the service was far below what one would expect¡ªespecially at the price point. Basic attention to detail was completely lacking.

We were a table of seven, including a child, yet the staff didn¡¯t acknowledge the need to serve the child first¡ªsomething any quality establishment should instinctively recognize. Starters were brought out and placed randomly wherever there was space, with no consideration for presentation or explanation. No one even bothered to ask if the starters were meant to be shared between all and help to do it. Also chairs very uncomfortable

For a hotel that carries the JW Marriott name, this level of service is simply unacceptable. I hope the management seriously reviews the training and standards expected of their staff, because this experience was far from the premium service they should have.
label: Resident

name: leila abaab
review: Excellent service and food! Everyone was so attentive. I¡¯m a platinum elite member so we were offered free cicchettis too. We were there for dinner and the view was breathtaking. They offered blankets because it was a little chilly. Expect many bugs as this is normal in Venice, so make sure to use some OFF before going ?? otherwise everything was perfect !
label: Resident

name: Amaury S
review:
We were really excited for Sagra and truly were expecting more given its exclusive location and price point. Unfortunately, it wasn¡¯t one of the best meals we had in Venice. We felt that the menu was limited and although I appreciated its creativity, it felt very expensive for what we got. It was the first meal in Venice where we left a bit hungry after paying a pretty penny!

We had the torta as an appetizer and it was just too small. For entrees, I had the pasta with octopus and my partner had bucatini with meatballs. My pasta was delicious and so was my partner¡¯s, but portions were more on the smaller side. Thankfully mine was a bit bigger, but his had more meatballs than pasta. Come on, Sagra, no need to be stingy with the pasta.

Service was really good but the whole experience felt rushed. We were done with dinner in no time. One of our favorite things about Europe is being able to take our time dining and not be rushed, but it felt quite opposite here. Also ¡ª it¡¯s ridiculous that they charge per person for the table/to make a reservation. The views are great but at night you can¡¯t see much.
label: Resident

name: Francis
review:
Came here for lunch once in the inside part and we both had the burger. It was delicious and the fries were also super good. Service was great and yes overall it was pricey but the convenience of not having to take the shuttle was worth it since the burgers were so good.

We came back later in our stay for dinner after a long day of sightseeing but this time we sat on the outside patio. The service again was fantastic but this time we got a welcome drink which was delicious and an arancini amuse bouche which again was fantastic. The bread provided was just ok since we thought the crust was too hard but the basket came with some amazing focaccia bread.

For the mains we had the pappardelle al ragu and the seafood spaghetti. Both were fantastic! Portions were on the smaller side but with the bread it wasn't an issue. For dessert we tried the tiramisu experience and the lemon tart. The lemon tart filling was good but the shell was way too hard. The tiramisu was definitely the highlight though and was one of the best I've ever had. The fact that it's made tableside was also neat so I would highly recommend it.
label: Resident

name: Olga van der Vlist
review:
Delicious food! Great service!
One thing we didn¡¯t like it¡¯s super bright for dinner setting. It would be nice, more romantic and cozy with light dimmer.
label: Resident

name: Andrew
review: Great view and artichokes
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Anu Roverato
review:
The food was tasty and the dishes were beautiful. I'm happy that as a plant-based eater, there was a vegan menu too. Points of that! However, the vegan options should have more protein; now they were basically just vegetables in different forms. If there would be added tofu, beans, lentils etc. the meals would become more nutritious.
The restaurant is very beautiful and the staff was friendly.
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Steve
review: Spectacular food and amazing service at Patachic! The whole staff was great, but Adrian was truly standout ¡ª attentive without ever being overbearing. One of the best meals we¡¯ve had in Italy. And be sure to try the Steak Tartare ¡ª absolutely incredible!
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Seahn Finn
review: Unreal food. Our hotel recommended and it was such a good recommendation. I had the seafood pasta & the scallops to start and both were insane! Best pasta I¡¯ve ever had in my life, the chef is VERY talented. 10/10 would go back, a MUST try in Venice!
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Aiman Haziq
review: The food was absolutely bussin with authentic Italian cuisine at its best. Loved the atmosphere, especially the cozy back garden. Service was excellent and our waiter was fantastic. Highly recommend the linguine pistachio pesto as it is a must-try.
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Nicolas Nemeth
review:
Absolutely fantastic experience at this charming Italian spot! The food was amazing¡ªbeautifully presented and incredibly flavorful. The service was outstanding, going above and beyond to accommodate our every request, even some creative ones for the kids. The staff was incredibly friendly and flexible, making us feel right at home.

The wine selection was excellent, perfectly complementing the dishes. The ambiance is cozy and precious, tucked away in a lovely little spot that adds to the charm of the place.

From start to finish, everything about our meal was delightful. Highly recommended for anyone looking for authentic Italian cuisine with a touch of personalized care!
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Joe
review: Very nice trattoria in a cute little Campo. Great atmosphere, whether dining outside in front, inside, or in the outside garden in back. Excellent service. The food was very good, with nice presentation.
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Joey Tennutti
review: This was the best meal in Venice! The staff and their service was so amazing. Very attentive, friendly and our server was charming and funny. He took the time to make sure everything was excellent! I wish I remembered his name because he was so wonderful. Definitely would come back here. The seabass was the fish of the day and it was incredible!
label: Resident

place_title: Restaurant Patachic(ex la patatina di san giacomo)
name: Purvang Gandhi
review:
It is a true Venetian culinary delight! From the moment we walked in, we were welcomed with warm hospitality and an inviting atmosphere that made us feel amazing.

The menu offers a fantastic selection of traditional Venetian dishes, all made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. They also had vegetarian options and made our experience delightful.The spaghetti and gnocchi were absolutely exquisite. It was perfect spicy and bursting with flavors.

The wine list is impressive, featuring a great selection of local and regional wines that perfectly complement the dishes. Our server was knowledgeable and provided excellent recommendations.

The ambiance of the restaurant is charming, with a cozy interior with lot of wine bottles and with option to seat outside. It¡¯s the perfect setting for a romantic dinner or a special evening with friends and family.

It was an authentic Venetian dining experience with the combination of delicious food, excellent service, and a wonderful atmosphere. Highly Recommended!
label: Resident

name: Sankha Subhra Ghosh
review: Good place to try Korean and Chinese food. We needed to eat something spicy, affordable and near our hotel where we were staying. Found this place from Google Map. We ordered Grilled Pork Spicy, Lemon chicken and Prawn Fried Rice. Though we specifically asked to make the dishes extra spicy, they didn't do that. But still the taste was above average. I'd definitely recommend the place anyone who is bored with Italian food and want to try something spicy.
label: Resident

name: Jing Huang
review:
I tried this Korean-Chinese restaurant, it was a really good experience. The place was super clean, and the food came out pretty fast, which I appreciated.

The best thing we had was the spicy pork ¡ª I think it¡¯s called jeyuk bokkeum. It came out sizzling on a hot stone plate with onions, plus lettuce leaves and a bowl of rice on the side. The flavor was amazing ¡ª spicy, savory, and satisfying.

We ordered a bunch of different dishes, and honestly, everything tasted good. There wasn¡¯t a single thing I¡¯d tell you to skip.

Clean space, quick service, very reasonable price.
label: Resident

name: Alexandra Polisevschi
review:
The food here is really good, a nice variety after eating everyday classic Italian food.

We stopped for a quick and light dinner (my son loves plain rice, which is difficult to find in European restaurants). I took the dumpling soup, which was also very good.

The restaurant is small and cozy, with a pleasant atmosphere. I appreciated that they had real, reusable chopsticks and even had a special holder for them ¡ª a nice touch you don¡¯t see often.

The staff is kind and helpful. We had small difficulties ordering, however the owner speaks very good English and quickly helped out.
label: Resident

name: Evgenii
review:
Came here with zero expectations, but with a huge hope to eat something different from pasta & pizza. As a fan of asian cuisine, and korean in particular, I can say that this place is a pearl in the ocean of mediocre asian-style restaurants adapted to EU market. I strongly recommend this place for its quality and true korean cuisine taste.
Thank you, it was a real pleasure.
P.S.: my bowl is empty because the bibimbap was too good to wait until I'm done with taking pictures.

name: Jason Yoo
review: I was pretty hungover from last night and the ???? healed me. The ???? came with ?? and ??. Considering this is Korean food in Europe the food was great. Korean approved lol.

name: Ilyasse Messiaen
review: Worst carbona I¡¯ve ever had in my life. There was literally almost no sauce and instead of bacon they used regular ham. 8 euros in the trash can.

name: Anisa
review: Amazing pasta in Venice with super reasonable prices! The girl who took our order was so friendly, spoke great English, and even knew a couple of other languages. The atmosphere was cozy, they played great music, and the service was top-notch. The flavors were fresh and authentic, making it a truly memorable meal.
label: Resident

name: M *
review:
The area is so inviting for outdoor sitting and the weather is perfect. Enjoy the coffee, which is always delicious. The hot chocolate is also a great choice.

This is the perfect place to relax and unwind. The square outside offers a refreshing breeze and nice ambience on the island. Service is friendly
label: Resident

name: Steph
review: Lovely place for a lunch break while exploring the streets of Venice on foot! The pizza was a thin crust style and well flavoured. The lasagna was the best we have had so far in Italy¡­ SO GOOD. The scampi was soft and lovely and the bruschetta a good starter! Service was good. Recommend :)
label: Resident

name: Windy Ezelle
review: We ate here 4 times during a 3 day stay in Venice. We had exceptional food and service each visit and couldn¡¯t recommend it more!! Drinks are great, food is great, service is top notch!! Gluten free options were just as good as regular options!
label: Resident

name: Prathmesh Bhor
review:
Delicious Food and Great Service

The food was amazing! I had the mushroom ravioli, pizza, and spaghetti ¡ª all were full of flavor and beautifully prepared. The staff was also very polite and attentive. One of the servers even recommended a spaghetti dish that perfectly matched our taste preferences. Overall, a fantastic dining experience!
label: Resident

name: Keith Elkins
review:
It was nice to have a late night option. The staff is very friendly and attentive. The mixed cheese plate and bread was nice but the bread was not bakery fresh like I was expecting and they didn¡¯t serve the olive oil and vinegar until later after I asked for it. They did serve two flavored oils though.

The squid pasta was interesting and new for us. Not bad.

The cheese selection was good with Brie, blue, Gorgonzola and a hard aged cheese.

Veggie soup was good.

Very nice staff but didn¡¯t have the wow factor I was hoping for but definitely worth a try
label: Resident

name: Eszter P¨¢szti
review: From the smells to the tastes through the service every single thing was great. We really enjoyed our dinner and drinks. I truly hope we will come back soon! :) gluten free food is safe with no contamination and tasty!!!
label: Resident

name: Karolina Bochenek
review:
Charming hidden gem! Just a short walk from a well-known bridge, we found this lovely little restaurant. The smell of homemade pizza drifting out was impossible to resist ¨C and we¡¯re so glad we followed our noses! The pizza was truly something special, one of the best we¡¯ve ever had. The carbonara? Absolutely delicious.
Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Todd XoX
review:
We had pasta and gnocchi but we definitely should have had the pizza. The past it was fine and the service was great however I would recommend getting the pizza there are many different varieties to choose from period everyone around us had pizza and we wish we had chosen likewise.

It after main Street but definitely worth finding for some of the best pizza in the area.
label: Resident

name: Lexa O
review:
We came to Malibran 20 years ago, two years in a row in Venice. It was the best pizza we had in Italy, and we always remembered the place.

We happened to be in Venice for the afternoon on a Croatia cruise and decided to come here. They were playing loud pop music, and the restaurant was empty when we got there, so the atmosphere was a bit odd.
We decided to order pizza and got the diavolo (pepperoni). The pizza was okay, not bad; but not to die for like previous visits. The crust was a bit thicker, not as crunchy or crisp. The pizza had a lot of cheese on it.
A lot of the bread they gave us was in individually wrapped plastic.
The caprese was pretty good.

All in all, not bad if you¡¯re nearby but probably not worth a cross-city trek.
label: Resident

name: John Page
review:
Been twice to the bar while staying locally
Natalia is a fantastic host with a great personality,and makes a fab cocktail
Will be back later this evening
label: Resident

name: William Harrison
review: Natalia is an awesome bartender and absolutely hilarious.  She made my dad, brother-in-law and I feel welcome in Venice.  Her ¡°Italian Old Fashioned¡± was excellent, as was the Limoncello Spritz (and I don¡¯t even like Limoncello).  She was somehow managing to serve the whole place and still chat with us.  Beers also were ice cold.  I wish we had more time to come back!
label: Resident

name: Shiba Rocket (RocketShiba)
review: Service and atmosphere are great. Nice decoration! But the food are just normal...very normal. There are others local restaurants/osteria nearby the hotel, which offers better food.
label: Resident

name: Karl TV
review: Food was delicious. Octopus is a must to try
label: Resident

name: Erin White
review: Team at the restaurant and bar are very friendly and engaging.  Food & drinks were wonderful. Overall the dining and bar area was super cute and cozy.
label: Resident

name: AussieIslander
review: Very good quality dinner with a variety of choices on the menu. Fine service and a calm atmosphere. Good price. I have no hesitation in recommending this restaurant.
label: Resident

name: Mic and Gena Rodriguez
review: They put up with us and took great care of us, after a hard and long journey.
label: Resident

name: Stephen Sander
review: We were staying at the Hotel Monaco & Grand Canal, so initially we booked dinner here for convenience. We were more than happy with that choice. The restaurant has a wonderful menu, the food was absolutely divine, and the service was friendly and thoughtful. I enjoyed a beautiful pasta dish with lobster and a traditional Venetian liver dish which was a flavour explosion. The restaurant is also on the Grand Canal with beautiful views which adds to the incredible experience.
label: Resident

name: Braulio A.
review:
Grand Canal Restaurant and Bar is a quiet little spot tucked along the Grand Canal¡ªperfect if you¡¯re looking to escape the hustle and bustle of Venice and its famous attractions for a moment.

We stopped in for a few drinks and a chance to recharge before our next adventure. The staff was friendly and welcoming, and the bar was fully stocked to quench any thirst. They offer an excellent menu, though we didn¡¯t eat this time since we weren¡¯t quite ready for lunch.
label: Resident

name: Rie Richardson
review: A lovely aperol place to have a drink, watching all the boats go by. The service was excellent, no need for booking but may need to wait when the terrace is all full.
label: Resident

name: Suzanne Kelly
review: Beautiful Grand canal side cafe with amazing food and presentation but horrid welcome.  The Maitre d¡¯ completely ignored me to help a couple that came in after us.  When I said excuse me ¡°we were here first¡± he got quit snappy with me and said ma¡¯am.. ¡° I am a cleaver man¡± actually no¡­ you are not clever, you are rude and inconsiderate.  If you had just acknowledge me from the first¡­ that would have been clever and doing your job. Oh¡­ if you do make it past the inconsiderate maitre d¡¯ be sure to try the desserts! Those made it all better! ??
label: Resident

name: Ivon Nikolova
review:
I¡¯m surprised by the negative reviews. Contrary to what others have written, we had a wonderful early evening experience. We received a lovely complimentary snack from the restaurant ¡ª olives and nuts ¡ª and the coperto was minimal, unlike many places where you get no nice extras and the coperto is high! We had Bellinis, Aperol, and coffee.

Yes, it¡¯s expensive ¡ª but with that kind of view and such attentive service, I think it¡¯s absolutely justified. Our waiter was very polite and attentive, and it was totally worth sitting down and enjoying the moment!

While we were there, some people were complaining they weren¡¯t being let into the restaurant ¡ª but to be fair, there is a dress code for the restaurant (not the bar), and it¡¯s clearly displayed at the entrance along with the menu and prices, so there really shouldn¡¯t be any surprises.
label: Resident

name: Shane Black
review: Absolutely faultless service - definitely the best service I¡¯ve experienced in Italy so far. I had dinner here with my wife and children and the staff went out of there way to ensure that we were comfortable and the kids were happy. This included cooking a custom pasta dish for one of my kids who is a picky eater. The food was amazing, the wine was great, and the location is right on the grand canal. We¡¯ll definitely be going back!
label: Resident

name: Ali_srour05
review:
I don¡¯t usually write reviews, but our experience here was unacceptable. From the moment we arrived, we felt unwelcome. The staff treated us with clear disrespect and even showed racist behavior toward us.

I ordered a medium steak, and it was served nearly raw. When I politely asked for it to be fixed, they ignored me. I also asked for water, and the waiter poured it carelessly, even throwing the bottle in a very rude way. So we left leaving our plates!

This kind of behavior is not just poor service¡ªit¡¯s offensive. I would not recommend this place to anyone who expects basic respect and decent hospitality.
label: Resident

name: Jay Finesilver
review:
Venice fine dining on the waterfront just next to Hotel Monaco.  The food was great and impeccably prepared.  Servers in coats w ties, very old school.  The tiaramisu was the best so far in a week in Italy.

Very expensive $100 person without alcohol.

My biggest complaint is all the Europeans smoking on the patio (where the restaurant is located) during dinner.  This wouldn't happen in the states,   the table next to us had 5 smokers at the table, all smoking at the same time, truly disgusting.
label: Resident

name: Julia Fleisher
review: So grateful Gam Gam Goodies exists! It¡¯s the only ¡°dairy¡± kosher in Venice. They have some gelato, a fridge section of some pre packaged kosher grocery goods (like salami, cheeses etc), wine and snacks. This is a take away place, you order and go, but sooo good! We got pizza after Shabbat (which was totally worth sending my husband while I stayed in the Airbnb with our sleeping toddler) and in the morning got a breakfast pita with fresh pita. The dough is made in house in front of you and it is a delicious! The cookies and baked goods are also amazing. They are recipes from the Rebbetzin of the community and so so good!
label: Resident

name: Daniel Heideman
review: This pizza GETS THE JOB DONE. When you¡¯re walking around Venice taking in the scenery and you suddenly get hungry, this is a great place to go. It¡¯s not near the shops but it¡¯s a good walk and the pizza is worthy of the walk. They also sell funky packaged salami and other meats. The guy making the pizza was super nice and suggestive, and the pizza was good. Not the best, but does the job! Pizza flop was 5/10 and slice overall was 6.1/10
label: Resident

name: Rivky
review: Such a plus to have delicious food with high kashrus standards , was delicious there pizza on late motzia shabbos , kids loved there gelato, they made us very good egg scrambled sandwiches, . They were unbelievable accommodating when we asked they should prepare us 8am our sandwiches , everything was ready , neat and gusto.
label: Resident

name: Palesa ¡°Terry¡± Khoza
review:
Served by Emanuelle they have the best view of the sunset, restaurant is also surrounded by a beautiful garden.

I would recommend they add citronella candles as the mosquitoes make the stay a bit uncomfortable
label: Resident

review:
This place functions as both a restaurant (indoors) and a coffee shop (outdoors). We sat outside and enjoyed drinks and desserts. The atmosphere was wonderful¡ªset in a charming garden with beautiful views overlooking the lagoon.

While the setting and vibes were excellent, the drinks and food were just okay¡ªnothing particularly special.

name: Marija Petrovska
review: Great spot to enjoy a drink near the Biennale! The atmosphere was relaxed, and the Campari was excellent ¨C perfectly served. Highly recommend for a quick break or a leisurely drink.

review: Experience the best tiramisu and coffee in the sunshine! The waiter was so nice that brought us additional table on the spot ???? thank you

We had the most wonderful view and this area is so much quieter that it was actually enjoyable being close to the water since you didn't have as many people walking by.

The desserts we had were delicious too but a bit on the pricier side but that's expected for a place that has nice views.

There are better restaurants in this price range.

review: We had one of our best meals at Trattoria de Bruno. The service and food were 5 stars. We had a great pasta dish as the first serving, followed by delicious steak tips in an incredible gravy with peas and carrots.  For dessert, we enjoyed a refreshing flan-like dessert. Also,  the restaurant was well air conditioned.  Anyone who has experienced the heat and humidity of Venice knows how important air conditioning is.  I would highly recommend this restaurant.

name: Indra Ba??¨©te
review: Wonderful service, prosecco as a welcome drink, compliment from the kitchen, food great, prices adequete, and limancello at the and, according to Italian traditions. Very cosy athmosphere! Recommend!

The comprehensive and unobtrusive accommodation of my partner's dietary intolerances was particularly appreciated. The splendid dishes were not only delectable but also presented with flair and top-tier precision. We express our gratitude, and eagerly anticipate our return.

I don't tend to look at reviews and it was only when we had sat down at this bistro that I thought to look... it was perfectly lovely and appealing from the street, the waiters were welcoming and friendly... so we gave it a go.

I don't think any of the really awful reviews are fair- the food was good, service was good, drinks were good (but pricey).

Yes the waiters mentioned the tip situation but so what... doesn't mean you have to do it - it's a personal choice.

There's loads of fab places to eat in Venice. This wasn't the best meal we had but it was perfectly enjoyable.

We ordered 2 expressos at which the waitress told us the price and asked us if we were able to afford it.

Avoid at all cost this place.

This is the worst place so far we experienced in Venice.  The waitress was unfriendly.  The least. The food was ok, but nothing great.  I don¡¯t recomend fish. Later the waiter reminded us about tips two
or three times.  The bill was the highest for the last three days in Venice.

Next to Gritty Palace so a beautiful area, unfortunately the food was horrible. We got sucked in by the location and figured it would be OK, we were wrong!  Didn¡¯t experience bad or rude wait staff like others. They were fine, not super friendly, but fine. The drinks were cold and tasty, but the seafood platter was unfortunately inedible.

One star as I¡¯m sure you don¡¯t really want to eat here! Much better places nearby.

review:
Worst restaurant in Venice. Hope they close this shithole sometime soon.

Ordered the octopus and scallops. Both were disgusting. Asked them for a check and to take the terrible food off the bill after one bit and refused. Locked us inside and threatened to call police for if we didn¡¯t pay full price for disgusting food and immediately yelled at my wife when she asked to remove the plates even though they were not eaten.

If you want to have an enjoyable time in Venice, I would avoid this place at all costs.

Hopefully the police will shut this scam down soon.

Asked to drink coffee, were seated in the corner and unserved table. Okay.
The coffee was really sour and costed 5.50 EUR per cup.
However, we are also charged 'maggiorazione' (service tax) 12% while there was no service, just silently brought coffee.

name: joana kartalewa
review:
It is quite average. The only plus is that is between San Marco and ponte Antico.
We got caprese salad (i suppose the plastic tomatoes are plastic because of the season. Alora), the three soups that they offered - the fish one was quite full of all the sea species - cool, tomato - ok, vegetable - ok.
Pasta with vegetables - average, lamb chop - average, lasagne - probably out of the freezer¡­.
White wine was good. And¡­. It was like two times more expensive than most of the other places we have been to around San Marco.
Some of the staff were not exactly friendly, or nice. But some of them were really, really professional.
When part of our friends sat down (we were on our way) the staff informed them that they can¡¯t get their order before everyone are gathered. Which is fine, if they presented the food requiring it.
The thing that I should¡¯ve noticed from the start that all of the guests were foreigners. Witch is not bad, but is a red flag in a restaurant in Italy.
Overall - if you are dying of  hunger and like white wine - please, enjoy.

review: As far as Italy goes, the food was below the quality I expected. The idyllic location makes you think it be a great food experience but there has to be better food in other places in Venice. Definitely not my first recommendation to have a meal.

I asked for price of a cake, I never said I wanted it (8 for a small Tiramisu - too expensive) but they still brought it to my table. When I said I dont want it and never asked for it, the waiter (mid 50s) became so offended, took the cake away and threw it on other table; with a grumpy voice, he said ¡°you dont want it then dont eat it, dont destroy my new day¡±. Even worse, two of our group members got bad tummy ache after drinking coffee here!

We stayed nearby the place but never came back again! They need to enhance their customer service a lot. Until then, I dont recommend to come eat or drink here, instead go to centre Venice or small canal, way better service!

Great food, horrible charge for guests.  Do better!

As for the food, the risotto alla secole was spectacular and my wife loved her fried shrimp and calamari.  The panna cotta was just ok, I¡¯ve had better and my wife enjoyed her tiramisu.  The staff did a great job making sure the spaghetti was safe for our kids with food allergies.

name: Matt M
review:
Let me just start with a little background.

My wife and I have been to Japan and have been to 3 and 2 Michelin starred restaurants. As a local Californian, we¡¯ve tried the most famed (from local Japanese-American citizens) restaurants as well. We have acquired a great taste of high quality sushi.

Reading some of the older reviews have some ridiculous comments, but everyone is entitled to their opinion. Some comments do not make sense when complaining of the size of the rolls or lack of children¡¯s menu, or not understanding Japanese/English. Sushi rolls are not meant to be big. Big rolls doesn¡¯t mean better sushi... I can go on but let¡¯s get to our experience.

We arrived around 1900hours, not too busy. We were greeted by Mauro(not sure if that¡¯s the right spelling) and he was very polite and welcoming. We sat right next to the window on the first floor. We ordered the historic Seared Salmon with truffles, toro, and unagi. All the dishes were amazing and brought flashbacks to Japan. The Seared Salmon was great and the truffles added an Italian twist. The toro and unagi tasted as if we were in Japan. The fish was fresh and most importantly the rice was perfect! It¡¯s the rice that will separate regular sushi places to high quality sushi restaurants. Trust me...the quality of ingredients was perfecto!

We were so satisfied we came back the next day. This time we made reservations. (Make reservations, Basara can get busy especially during the weekend)

Our second time we had the miso black cod, seared tuna with truffle, unagi with foie gras, and a lobster nigiri. All of these dishes just melted in our mouth and we¡¯re amazed! A special shoutout to the chefs behind the counter. Christian and Steve looked busy but were able to prepare all these dishes to perfection. Each dish was carefully crafted and they presented them in a beautiful way The wait staff did her best in understanding our English and she was attentive and very polite.

My wife will always remember our experience. Both the staff and food was amazing! Also, the decor a design of the bathroom was cool!

Not only does Venice have the beautiful historic churches, places, people, gondolas, scenic views, etc... it has one of the best sushi places in Italy!

name: Tiffany Simone' (The Tiffany Simone Show)
review:
Had not been to Kona Grill for almost a year now, and it was one of my favorite go-to spots ¡ª especially when I want to grab my laptop, settle in, and get some work done while enjoying great food and an easygoing atmosphere. The staff is usually friendly, the music is upbeat but not too loud, and there¡¯s always a good energy whether you¡¯re at the bar or tucked into a booth.

One of the biggest reasons I kept returning is the happy hour menu. It¡¯s hard to beat the value and variety ¡ª from sushi to small plates, there¡¯s something for every craving. The Hawaiian steak any bad either.  My absolute favorite has to be the Angry Edamame ¡ª it¡¯s heavenly! Spicy, garlicky, and perfectly addictive. The lettuce wraps are another consistent win ¡ª still just as flavorful and fresh as when I first started coming.

Now, not everything hits the mark. The meatballs on the happy hour menu were a bit of a miss for me ¡ª dry and lacking flavor compared to other options. The chicken satay was just okay; the sauce was fine, but the chicken itself could use a little more tenderness and seasoning. The rice was also just average ¡ª not bad, but not memorable either.

That said, the sushi always delivers ¡ª fresh, nicely portioned, and well-plated. It¡¯s my usual go-to when I want a light meal while working. I¡¯ve spent many afternoons here with a roll or two, a drink from happy hour, and my laptop plugged in, and I¡¯ve always felt comfortable and welcome.

Overall, Kona Grill remains one of those rare places that balances a relaxed vibe with good food and decent prices. Even with a few minor misses, it¡¯s still one of my top casual dining spots, especially when I need a place to unwind, work, or treat myself to sushi and my beloved Angry Edamame.

Thanks to my server Mike,  the chef Jermaine visited the take as well

-
label: Resident

name: Antonio T
review:
What made my first visit to Kona Grill unforgettable was our server, Reggie. Friendly, funny, and absolutely on point with every recommendation¡ªhe set the tone for the whole night.

A close friend (who I may or may not be hopelessly smitten with¡­ another story for another time) suggested we check this place out. So maybe my enthusiasm is slightly biased¡ªbut only slightly.

The standout for me? Macadamia Nut Chicken. I¡¯ve had plenty of sweet-and-savory dishes before, but this version is unique. My pro tip: add a bit of the pineapple marmalade on top of the macadamia sauce, then finish with just a dash of soy sauce¡ªabsolute flavor magic. I also don¡¯t usually like green beans, but their sweet chili version that comes with the chicken? Devoured.

The veggie sushi roll was surprisingly excellent¡ªproof that sushi can shine even without fish. And the soy Brussels sprouts? A total revelation. Definitely a new go-to for me here.

Between the great food, warm atmosphere, and a staff that clearly cares, Kona Grill won me over. I¡¯ll be back¡ªprobably sooner than I should.
