Glorious day, glorious setting, glorious food, glorious staff! HIGHLY RECOMMEND going along when on Murano.
label: Resident

name: Joseph OSullivan
review: Best restaurant we've been to in the whole of Venice. Good priced Lasagne, bread basket was fresh and views were great.
label: Resident

name: Meredith Tillson
review: Amazing food and atmosphere! We stopped here the last day of our vacation. It was the best meal we had over the course of 2 weeks. The owner and staff were attentive and professional. The view was amazing. The dishes were creative and inspired. The prices were inexpensive for the caliber of the meal, but more pricey than the other restaurants in the area. We both choose the $75 tasting menu without hesitation.  Simply amazing. There is huge Michelin potential here!
label: Resident

name: Csongor ¨¢kos Kiss
review:
Dear management ,

We had a terrible experience at this restaurant. We came in for lunch and wanted to sit at one of the empty tables with a nice view, but the waiters didn¡¯t seem to care that we wanted to be seated. Despite the restaurant being nearly empty, they wouldn¡¯t let us sit there. We ended up having to put together our own table in a corner ¡ª there were five of us, but only four chairs. They just tossed us a bottle of water without even noticing that one of us didn¡¯t have a seat. We managed to grab another chair ourselves, but it was disappointing that they didn¡¯t even notice we were missing a glass.

After waiting for 15 minutes, not a single waiter came to take our order or even said a word to us. I honestly think they didn¡¯t even notice when we left.

Never again ¡­
label: Resident

name: F'burg Foodie
review:
We came here for lunch on a weekday, choosing this spot because it offers seating along a canal.  The staff was helpful and friendly and we got a nice seat right along the canal.  Their menu is filled with appealing local eats, many including seafood.  They have an interesting carafe menu for their wine, you can get wine by the glass, or in various milliliter amounts up to a liter, so we chose a liter of chardonnay.  That wine was amazing, and only 17eu!

We started by splitting their salad and that salad was beautiful and tasty!  Made with fresh ingredients, it was excellent when dressed with simply EVOO and balsamic!  Our entr¨¦es were also excellent.  I loved their pesto in the Maltagliati Scampi e Pesto!  The shrimp in it offered a nice flavor too!  The Tagliatelle ai Funghi Porcini was a huge hit as well, especially with is being porcini season!

Definitely would recommend stopping in, but make a reservation, they seem to get busy quickly!
label: Resident

name: Quentin Mari¨¦thoz
review:
We came there for a honeymoon dinner. The only reason we give 4 stars is that we felt it was too high end for us.

Food was interesting and amazing. Waiters were helpful and knowledgeable. View was amazing.

But for the same price we would have gone to three other places and would have had a more down to earth and amazing experience too.
label: Resident

name: Louis-Philippe & L¨¦o
review:
We¡¯ve generally avoided hotel restaurants in America, but decided to give Aromi a try because it was so conveniently located for us. (And we¡¯re in Venice!)

We were pleasantly surprised by the quality of the experience.  Very knowledgeable , accommodating staff (Riccardo REALLY knows his stuff and is very ¡®Cordiale e disponibile ¡ä) combined with an interesting menu and wine selection made for a memorable experience.  We also learned of regional specialties, such as the Sant¡¯Erasmo artichokes . Perfection. A great way to spend our anniversary evening. Merci beaucoup. Grazie mille !!
label: Resident

name: Daniel Medrano
review: The food was the best around! But the high light was our serve Greta, which if you change the letters around is great because that¡¯s what we got was great service! Also the other gentleman that was with her was amazing as well! As well the best view!
label: Resident

review: We had an exquisite dinner at Aromi! The food was delicious!! The staff was very attentive and answered our questions about the menu. They have small tables to put your purse on. We ate outside on the terrace. It was a beautiful evening. We highly recommend this amazing restaurant. Enjoy!

Quando li chiami non rispondono mai.
Esperienza fallimentare.
label: Resident

name: Christopher Ciraulo
review: This was our second visit to Brunch Cafe while visiting Venice, and it was even better than the first! Even during the busy rush of the breakfast hour, the hostess prepared our table and took our order while we waited outside and we sat within 10 minutes. Our food and coffee was delivered within a few minutes of sitting and it was delicious. Avocado toast with Bacon did not disappoint and the Nutella Pancakes were fantastic! They took a little longer than the rest of the food, but the entire staff was on top of it and made sure we were comfortable and happy the entire time. The crew here is top-notch and the food is well worth the wait. Definitely recommend this cozy spot for anyone passing through the busy streets of Venice!
label: Resident

name: David
review:
The food was amazingly fun.
From what I gather everyone goes here for the ¡°pancakes¡±. They are very light and fluffy, more like a meringue.
Very small establishment, there is a fresh pasta place right next door as well. There are warning of pickpockets everywhere. We never had any issues. Service and food was great all around. In most
Places in Europe we learned you must ask for the check. Or you will be waiting.
label: Resident

name: E
review: Wanted a egg omelette and came to the right place. We didn't make reservations and ended waiting about 3-5min in a que outside, the place was busy was seated quickly. I had the egg omelette and partner had the fluffy pancakes eggs Benedict type plate along with Apersol and cappuccino! Everything was delicious and would recommend if your looking for a non-traditional Italian breakfast.
label: Resident

name: Micheal Ostoforoff
review:
Nice place! Great service, excellent food, decent price.  We all enjoyed our meals!
The fluffy pancakes were delicious!  I had a savoury version with eggs and bacon - and ate everything! Including the salad on the side,  and I don't generally do that at breakfast...
Presentation on the plate was exceptionally well done.
Definitely recommend.
label: Resident

name: Matthew Drinkall
review: WOW!! Amazing breakfast had by all here! Bit of a wait to get a table, but the line moved quickly. Lovely cosy indoor seating. Kind, friendly service with quick food delivery. The highlight was the gluten free pancakes- they were out of this world! How they make them so fluffy and tasty, we would love to know! Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Matt
review:
This place was so good! We got in with no reservation luckily around 9am at the end of May. When we left there was a wait however.

We ordered a cappuccino and a caramel macchiato to drink. The macchiato was a nice looking drink and very delicious. Not overly sweet either. Cappuccino was pretty as well and tasted like a normal cappuccino.

For food we got the breakfast combo with eggs, bacon, and toast. Came with a salad with a pesto dressing that was delicious. This was a solid, simply meal.

The puffy pancakes we tried the trio of Nutella, mango/coconut, and salmon. All of these were really good! I may swap the salmon out if we were to order again but it was good. The other two I¡¯d definitely order again. They were great.

Service was good as well and nice we got in with no reservation. Weirdly the coffees were 1 euro more on our receipt than advertised on the menu, but no big deal. Was just something to note.
label: Resident

name: Jakob Bierbaumer
review:
The name doesnt do this justice, you can order everything at every time.. i think!
The Menus seem pretty good value aswell but i just went in for a quick snack.

I Got the Classic Avocado Toast and it was exactly what I needed.

Staff is very nice, the Atmosphere is calm & relaxed -oh and they are playing Christmas music in October.
Take from that what you will but to me thats a big plus??
label: Resident

name: Louisa Sampson
review: We had breakfast at this lovely local caf¨¦. Amazing croissants, especially the pistachio and the coffee is great too! A little off the beaten track but that just makes it feelore authentic. Our total was 7 for two coffees and two filled croissants. Highly recommend.
label: Resident

name: Sally-Ann Jackson-Chaplin
review: Definitely not in the most picturesque of areas  in Venice, but it's a charming little coffee shop tucked away in a quiet street. The pistachio croissants are the AMAZING and the coffee is tasty too! Feels like a locals spot but barista speaks fluent English and very friendly :)
label: Resident

name: Damjan Pushkovski
review: Amazing place, amazing coffee, amazing sandwiches. AMAZING!! Highly recommend, even though it only has two tables to sit!!
label: Resident

name: Mary Kate Charron
review: We had our morning cappuccino here every day in Venice and it was perfect. The coffee was delicious, the baristas were quick and kind. Thank you Boresso!
label: Resident

name: Solange Bohling
review: Really nice little place that we went to for breakfast multiple times. It was in a quiet area that was a nice break from the crowds and it seemed like a place the locals frequented. Good coffee and good pastries! Highly recommended :)
label: Resident

name: M Torres
review: A true gem in VeniceDa Mario & I Fioi is the kind of caf¨¦ you hope to stumble upon but rarely do. The coffee is rich and smooth, the croissants are fresh and flavorful, but its the warmth of the service that truly stands out. Piero and Donar treat every guest like family, creating an atmosphere thats as comforting as the food. A favorite among locals, this spot is welcoming, genuine, and absolutely worth a visit.
label: Resident

name: PaulS
review: It¡¯s a bar that happens to serve coffee. This is not a specialty coffee shop. Coffee was fine, nothing objectionable nor terribly exciting either. Did not enjoy the croissant. It was mushy and heavy. A good croissant is supposed to be light and crusty.
label: Resident

name: Cecilia Lu
review: Great coffee with simple local food from  two friendly good looking hosts - a great start to a perfect morning in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Brian Schoene
review: We visited this cafe both mornings we were in Venice in June.  The servers were very nice, the pastries and cappuccino were perfect.  If you are a chocolate lover try a Marocchino!
label: Resident

name: Eric Pieper
review: Great little cafe. The food it was so good we forgot to take pictures ahead is fine. Fun atmosphere and full of locals in the morning. Just what we needed in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Hannah KHoury
review: This place was awesome! Great coffee and delicious sandwiches. The staff and service were friendly and made recommendations. This is a great local spot to start your day!
label: Resident

name: Irene Tan
review:
A superb place for breakfast with a great boss and assistant. Coffee and sandwiches were yummy.
Thumbs up ??!!
label: Resident

name: Vanessa Ma
review: Not very friendly staff, pigeons flying into the indoors which is terrible, but probably the best cafe option around the Giardinni Biennale area that¡¯s of reasonable price and with a range of light food options, also pretty nice botanical environment (if there would not be pigeons!)
label: Resident

name: Chun-Han Chen
review: I went there 1 year ago. The food is just okay you can find other good choice nearby. And then they refuse me for one cappuccino, a very tipical breakfast, this year. Just remind you they just want take your money. If your order is not enough, they will kick you out the restaurant even though 50% of seats are empty. This restaurant is just for nice Instagram photos not food.??????????
label: Resident

name: Kuan Hou ¡°Angus¡± Lee
review: 7.5 euro for one drink and 2 ciccheti! This wasn't shown on the menu nor told to us, but luckily the staff were kind to do this deal by default if you order accordingly. Drinks were good. Food were good. Amazing place to come and relax after a long day walking around Venice!!
label: Resident

name: William Harrison
review: Excellent spot for a quick lunch in Venice.  The cicchetti we had all was delicious.  I asked one of the ladies for her recommendations and she suggested several of the small sandwiches; codfish, spicy salami, etc.  Not one disappointed, nor did the ice-cold draft beer.  Very reasonably priced and comfortable seating.
label: Resident

name: Amber
review:
Cute little cafe perfect for breakfast or a mid-day snack. They have the best croissants ever, they are so soft buttery and flaky (I had 5 ??). The donuts with cream, chocolate or pistachio filling are awesome too.

Nice ambiance with Italian music playing in the background. Staff are smiling and pleasant to interact with.
label: Resident

name: Lou
review:
We walked all the way here from other side of town and it was totally worth it. Beautiful sandwiches and delish coffee (take extra shot if espresso with your oat cappuccino if that¡¯s your usual coffee)
Beef and turkey sandwiches r to die for ???? Perfecto
label: Resident

name: Bella Reviews
review: One of the best pistachio croissants I¡¯ve had in Italy so far! I got a second one right after! It¡¯s warm inside, the pistachio is a bit melted. Perfect for a cold morning! Went at 9-10am on a Sunday.
label: Resident

name: Patr¨ªcia
review: The croissants and cappuccinos were excellent; fresh, tasty, and definitely worth trying. The staff was very friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. The only downside is that it gets quite busy, so there was a bit of a line, and we were lucky to find a spot to sit. That said, the view is absolutely amazing, right in Venice overlooking the canal, which makes it a perfect place to enjoy a coffee and pastry.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review: Just stopped here to grab an afternoon Cappuccino, was pleased to see that they had non-dairy milk options to pick from. By far the most reasonably priced coffee I had in Venice. It was also one of the better coffees I had in my time here. Vibes were nice, sitting right in front of a canal / square.
label: Resident

name: Zena T
review: Great place to grab a slice of pizza and go. Perfect to eat something good and fast if you don't want to loose time in a restaurant. The location is pretty small, suitable for 1-2 let's say 3 persons. I had a slice of pizza, and ice coffee and a bottle of water and I paid 12.5 euros.
label: Resident

name: Cate Carroll
review: A little gem in Venice with coffee and panini for our brunch. Easy to find from the ferry and using Google maps! Outdoor seating facing the Maddalena church and square and close to canal with frequent gondolas passing. Staff were warm and friendly and in spite of efforts to speak Italian, they responded in English for clarification.
label: Resident

name: Suresh Kumili
review:
It's my favorite bakery in Venice. My daily routine was to wake up and head straight to 1000 bakery. The fresh croissants and refreshing espresso would wake me up and prepare for the 25K steps I would do each day :)

Try their flagship croissant, which is with pistachio filling. If you do not like sweet pastry in the morning, you can try their butter croissant as well. It's equally good.
label: Resident

name: Dana Montes
review:
I visited on weekday. The place has inside and outside places to eat. The pizza was nice and the ingredients were good but I won¡¯t say is the best though a good one and the price was really good compared to other restaurants in the area.
The staff is friendly, there¡¯s toilets, the place is clean and overall was a good experience.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review:
We got to Venice very late and decided to checkout this Panini place. Unfortunately at 8:00 PM they were all out of cicchetti which we were looking forward to trying with the sandwich. I saw many other places still had them, but it is more of a early day food.

With that said, we ended up getting a panini and it tasted really good, I asked for it to be toasted and the server ended up having a conversation with locals while the food was cooking, so as you can see, it got burnt. Overall, it tasted good, but the service left a lot to be desired.
label: Resident

name: Eric Witiw
review: The cicchetti were awesome and the staff are so friendly and kind. Great place to stop for a coffee and some food!
label: Resident

name: Emily Jarrard
review: Best cappuccino and croissant I¡¯ve had in Italy so far! The two women working were lovely and the vibes sitting outside were perfect :)
label: Resident

name: Nia
review:
Kind staff that was very good answering all of our questions about the menu. They had something to accommodate all of our needs - a meat lover, a kid that doesn¡¯t like vegetables and a vegetarian :)
Paninis were fresh, prepared quickly and tasted great. The prices were very reasonable too. Thumbs up!
label: Resident

name: Vlad Ivancu
review: I stopped at Caff¨¨ del Doge and had a double espresso and tiramisu. The coffee was excellent ¨C strong, intense, and just a bit bitter, but in a positive way that really highlights the flavor. The tiramisu was classic and well made, a proper version of this iconic dessert. Cozy atmosphere and a great spot for real Italian coffee in Venice. Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Andrew Yit
review: Best coffee i had in italy in 10 days. They sell beans from various places in the world. Pastries are amazing. My barista is a cool chap
label: Resident

name: Tanya Carlson
review: The coffee is amazing. Smooth, strong but not bitter. The service is friendly, helpful (as we spoke little Italian) and the food is fresh. Be sure to try the pistachio croissant. We will be back.
label: Resident

name: Richard Owen
review:
Real fresh milk in the coffee! This was the first place we found that used fresh whole milk for the flat white. They are a proper coffee shop with beans sourced from all over the world for you to choose from.

We had a cannoli as well which was divine.

Unfortunately, I did not think to review this place until we had finished.
label: Resident

name: tim 84
review: The taste of cappuccino is amazing. We tasted the best cappuccino in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Zhenqing YING
review: Very lovely restaurant . Maya is adorable and the food is delicious! We love this place.
label: Resident

name: G Sash
review: A little hole in the wall with. Fabulous atmosphere. A 5 min walk from the main bus and taxi station on Venice. Great for breakfast and some veggie options available.
label: Resident

name: Mayank Sharma
review: Lovely little place close to the train station. This is my second visit to this place and they have great chicchetti along with chichettos (mini sandwiches). Service is pleasant and cocktails were quick. Overall this place has great vibes
label: Resident

name: angiewong600
review: We were on the way to the Piazzale Roma bus station and spotted this little bar along the happening riverbank. We went in to enquire about the food and we were very pleased with the friendly boss who introduced the food to us. It is his friendliness that attracted  us. The food we ordered were Lagsana ,Spagetti and Italian popular drinks , Spritz Aperol. Very authentic italian food and drinks! Highly recommended to All.
label: Resident

name: Laura Zarina
review: Very sweet staff who was very kind and helpful! Their menu online isn't really the same as on the location, but it doesn't matter cause the service was superb! And the sandwiches were so good! Same with the prices!??
label: Resident

name: Gabi Sz
review: I really enjoyed my late breakfast here!! c:
label: Resident

name: Maggie Chang
review: This was one of the best paninis I have ever had. I got the vegan panini (and I¡¯m not strictly vegan or vegetarian) of roasted seitan, beetroot salad, caramelized onions, and artichoke. I ordered at the bar and was lucky to snag a table outside right along the canal. The panini was made fresh to order. The bread was perfectly toasted and so hot and fluffy. Sandwich was really flavorful. I was really impressed. I¡¯m thinking of going back just for that sandwich alone with my few days here in Venice. I grabbed a spritz bianco for my drink for 3.5 euros. Really light on the alcohol, but decent for the price.
label: Resident

name: Tim Brown
review: There is a notice outside that they don't do cicchetti. However, what they do serve are platters and big sandwiches with cheeses and cooked meats. My small cheese platter was actually a generous size and my glass of red wine was excellent.
label: Resident

name: Marija Lagutina
review: Great freshly made panini. You can choose from the menu or create your own panini. Also you can get selection of cheese and meat accompanied by your favorite drink. Canal view is great.
label: Resident

name: Max Urazaev
review: Love this place! Truly wine connoisseurs, they gave us the opportunity to taste several varieties of wine, perfectly meeting our needs. We'll definitely be back!
label: Resident

name: Alice M
review:
Really delicious panini. We created our own which was great and so tasty & fresh. The bread was still slightly warm!
The staff were very friendly and helped us decide. Good selection of wines and everything is excellently priced!

Nice little seating area around the side by the canal.
Would¡¯ve gone back if I had more time in Venice!
label: Resident

name: lara addison
review: Incredible service and the wine was amazing. It was great value, had lots of choice and great grazing board options
label: Resident

name: Kevin Lottis
review: I chose this cafe because I heard they are serving some of the best coffee in Venice and it's true. There were welcoming and friendly to us. It seemed like mostly locals outside which is another reason to come here. The pastry and cakes were delicious.
label: Resident

name: Arwin
review: I stopped by Bottega del Caff¨¨ Dersut and was pleasantly surprised by the quality of their fresh juice. I ordered the L¡¯Arancione and it was truly refreshing ¡ª vibrant, naturally sweet, and bursting with freshness. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients. The atmosphere is cozy, with a relaxed Venetian charm, and the staff are welcoming and attentive. It¡¯s the perfect little spot to take a break, enjoy something light, and soak in the local vibe. Highly recommended if you¡¯re looking for a genuine and refreshing drink experience in Venice. ????
label: Resident

name: Jake (developerjake)
review:
Very inhospitable staff. Wouldn't let my partner use the restroom without purchasing something, so she bought a coffee, and while waiting for her we sat on the chairs outside. Once she came outside with her coffee they gave us a total of 5 seconds on their seats before warning us that we couldn't sit there because the coffee was takeaway unless we wanted to pay them more. We were literally reading the map to see which way we were going, and every table except ours was empty... They really set a low bar, especially compared to some of the other lovely local restaurants.

I'd recommend you stay away.
label: Resident

name: Ella Taubman
review: I honestly wish I could give this cafe a higher  rating they are truthfully one of the kindest staff caf¨¦s. I have been to in Italy. I have been studying abroad here in Venice and this coffee shop has been my go to every single morning before class as an American. I truly do enjoy my iced coffee and they have been nothing but kind to make me an iced coffee every single day. Their croissants are better than any I¡¯ve ever had and they truly make my morning better.
label: Resident

Very nice caf¨¦ with lots of things to order

Very good place to hide when it's hot outside. Not a lot of noise from the outside was big plus for us
label: Resident

name: Kristina James
review: We spent a lot of time there. A super nice bartender (the owner) I would come back anytime ????????????
label: Resident

Cappuccino was nice,  has a slight bittered taste.
Seems like it's an authentic place, as there is no "to go" option and no place to sit, so be ready to stay.
Also you can buy coffee beans inside

They accept card payment.
label: Resident

review: Decent creamy coffee (3/5) friendly service, best prices coffee in Venice, nostalgic atmosphere could do with some work and some seating, heaps of potential serving coffee like that.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: ModeParChocolat
review:
We found the bakery and stuck to it for breakfast for the entire time in Venice.
The pizza were quite something. We took them and saved it for later and STILL just has yummy. We came back for the pastries and it did not disappoint.

As I type this, I¡¯m missing Venice even more. What a place
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Michael Moore
review: This place looks amazing as you walk by and makes you want to immediately come in. It was pretty busy as we entered around 1030 and hoped it was worth all the hype. Instead of something sweet we opted with two savory options with their quiche and ham and cheese sandwich. The quiche was delicious for a couple of warm bites but then there were a couple of cold bites since it was not heated all the way. The ham and cheese sandwich was ok. The bread and ham had no really taste but the cheese was really good. I would recommend other options in Venice if you have the chance.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Richard Pettigrew
review: Pizzas are wonderful! Square and a thicker base than the usual ones you get in Venice. Very tasty sauce. We got extremely kind customer service following an incident with a seagull!
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Julia H?rmayer
review: We had high hopes but the desserts weren¡¯t quite as good as expected. We thought the berry tart had custard but it was really just whipped cream with sugar, nothing special. The coffee was good. The small tart was very good according to my mum.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Katharina P.
review: Very good coffee and amazing pistachio croissants. We enjoyed our breakfast a lot! ??
label: Resident

name: Pandaz
review:
I stopped by after the museum to refuel.
There's a wide selection of food (pastries, cakes, little bites or even meals) and beverages (soft drinks, coffee, cocktails etc.)
Service was quick and super friendly. The experience was pleasant with an exceptional view of the square.
Price was reasonable compared to the neighbourhood!
I quite enjoyed the tiramisu??
label: Resident

name: Nurettin Tuncer
review: Nice place to take a break, and if you¡¯re lucky, you can get a table with a beautiful view of St. Mark¡¯s Basilica. Perfect spot for a coffee or dessert while enjoying the scenery.
label: Resident

name: Ethan Choi
review:
This is a place where you can enjoy exceptionally cheap and delicious coffee compared to other cafes in the square. It used to be Napoleon's office, and you can access it by going up to the museum entrance. Since it¡¯s a small space, seats might not always be available (a museum ticket is not required).

You¡¯ll need to go through a security check upon entry, so don¡¯t be surprised. Once inside, head to the second floor, and you¡¯ll see it on the left.
label: Resident

name: Nick Gray
review: The view over San Marco is the obvious star but good coffee and cake, with willing attentive service makes this a great place to rejuvenate.  We visited after a couple of hours walking around the museum, very pleased to take a breath here before continuing to the next sight-seeing adventure.
label: Resident

name: Jackson Lam
review: Good coffee and croissants. Had a nice view of the square, the basilica and the tower. Good quiet location as well. Note: need to pass security checkpoint but need not have a ticket for museum.
label: Resident

name: Jonathan Lane
review: Location, location, location! In the middle of many overpriced and overrun cafes in the Piazza San Marco, this was a breath of fresh air. I imagine it remains quieter because you need to enter through the museum security to get to it. Provided perfect views of the Piazza and a quiet place to retreat to during the busy day.
label: Resident

name: Javier Sanguinetti
review:
Great spot to grab a coffee with views to Piazza San Marco. If you are able to get one of the tables by the window it would probably be the best coffee spot in the piazza.
The prices are really good considering the setting.
You must go through a queue and security because it shares entrance with museum
label: Resident

name: Beth Miller
review: We came here a few times back in March 2024 and returned today for really delicious and fresh juice, coffee, super fresh sandwiches and a really lovely employee. This place is immaculate, well priced for what it is, and in a quiet and clean neighborhood with easy vaporetto access. I think the negative reviews come from people not understanding the type of sandwiches they'll get; there aren't sauces, spreads, or condiments that come on these sandwiches, just fresh ingredients and freshly sliced deli meats, which can be super delicious. No bathrooms. When you tell the sandwich maker that you'd like take away, they, without fail, warn you of the seagulls, which is kind and not something many people would do. If your children like sandwiches without a tomato or whatever, it's easy to remove for them and still share a great sandwich.
label: Resident

name: Shoon Lae Lae Oo
review:
The fresh lettuce & tomato with the big size of sandwich humbles my stomach!!
I love the way the food is yummy while having the fresh taste of vegetables inside the sandwich!!!
The best to try in Venice!
label: Resident

name: Roman Goncharenko
review: My god, those sandwiches are absolutely delicious! A rare find in Venice¡ªgrab one and head straight to Punta della Salute to enjoy it with the view!
label: Resident

name: Amelia Johnson
review: Fantastic! Really amazing sandwiches, crispy, made fresh then and there. Lovely fresh ingredients. Go for the fresh juice, delicious! Great coffee. The fact lots of locals came in was a great sign. The young lady serving was so friendly, efficient and fun!
label: Resident

name: Jas Y
review: The sandwiches were good, and tasted fresh, the fresh juice was really good! A little tucked away which made it feel special in a way...
label: Resident

name: Lisette Marin
review: Cute little sandwich shop right by the basilica! San Marco panini was simply delicious! Fresh orange & lemon juice was refreshing
label: Resident

name: Michaela Tangredi
review: We were able to stop at 60/40 Take Away on our recent vacation. I highly recommend this location for the food, drinks, and service! I tend to dislike coffee since I live in the US, but I loved it here. This was certainly the best cappuccino of my entire holiday. I hope to return someday.
label: Resident

name: Farimah
review: We had a wonderful experience here. We came for breakfast, and it was amazing! We tried both the salted and the sweet breakfast ¡ª and both were absolutely delicious. The atmosphere was lovely, especially since we sat in the garden. It was such a nice and relaxing spot. Definitely a must-visit when you¡¯re in Venice!
label: Resident

name: Heloise St-Pierre
review: We came back 2 days in a row. Everything is perfect here. Very good food a pastries are amazing. Staff is friendly and gives good recommendations. The place is beautiful in a peaceful garden. Absolutely recommend for coffee, cocktails or breakfast.
label: Resident

name: Diana Cecilia Barahona Rivera
review: A jewel in Venice. Moro Caf¨¦ feels like stepping into a living masterpiece ¡ª vibrant colors, palace-like d¨¦cor, and desserts that look and taste like art. The garden is serene, but inside you¡¯ll find a true fairytale, especially with the ¡®Exotic¡¯ dessert. Don¡¯t hesitate, just cross the bridge across the Accademia!
label: Resident

review: Lovey little cafe. Very conveniently located in proximity to the Arsenale. A lovey view, a small delicious offering of refreshments and friendly staff.
label: Resident

name: Le Kumara
review:
Very nice place, will be one of my favorites memories of Venise ! ??
Prosecco con Safran : i will made it at home, going back to France !

review: Wonderful place to chill ????

review: Sweet! ??

review: Un posto meraviglioso in un angolo sperduto di Venezia.

What I appreciate the most is the consistency: both times I came here, the service was kind, and the taste was just as wonderful as I remembered. Farini has definitely become one of my favorite spots in Venice, and I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to enjoy delicious pizza in a relaxed and authentic setting. I quite enjoyed the tiramisu??

name: Christos Ioannou
review:
Caff¨¨ Florian in San Marco Square is one of those rare places where history, elegance, and atmosphere blend into an unforgettable experience. The moment you step inside, you¡¯re transported to another era¡ªthe interior is refined, romantic, and steeped in Venetian grandeur, with gilded details and antique charm that make simply sitting there feel special.

The live music adds a magical touch to the setting. Listening to the ensemble play as the piazza glows outside is nothing short of enchanting¡ªtruly the soundtrack of Venice.

As for the treats, the "Sunrise Cake" was absolutely delightful¡ªfresh, bright, and beautifully balanced. And the signature "Florian Spritz" is a must-try: refined, refreshing, and unique, a perfect Venetian indulgence. While most offerings shine, the chocolate cake was a rare miss for me¡ªsurprisingly flat in flavor compared to the rest and slightly overbaked.

Despite that small hiccup, Caff¨¨ Florian remains a timeless Venetian icon. Come for the beauty, stay for the music, and savor the flavors that make this historic caf¨¦ so special. A quintessential Venice experience.

1.	Morocchino 5/5

A specific instance involved an order for rum, which was doubled in price and the incorrect brand was served. Furthermore, we encountered problems with the "plate of the day," which was not listed on any menu, and the staff were unhelpful in clarifying its cost. An unrequested and unannounced extra charge for burrata was also added to our bill after the waiter suggested adding it to a dish without disclosing the additional fee. Ultimately, when we questioned the bill, the waiter admitted to using a completely separate menu (not the one intended for customers) to determine our charges.

Lots of pastries choices too! I¡¯ll be back.

Cumple su funci¨®n para
Tomar un caf¨¦ durante tu caminata tur¨ªstica. Tienen promoci¨®n de un Aperol Spritz y 3 cicchetti por 10 euros. El caf¨¦ muy rico. La atenci¨®n excelente. S¨²per recomendable
label: Resident

name: Tania Leagun
review: Ottimo caff¨¨ e il personale gentilissimo!
label: Resident

name: Christopher Ciraulo
review: This was our second visit to Brunch Cafe while visiting Venice, and it was even better than the first! Even during the busy rush of the breakfast hour, the hostess prepared our table and took our order while we waited outside and we sat within 10 minutes. Our food and coffee was delivered within a few minutes of sitting and it was delicious. Avocado toast with Bacon did not disappoint and the Nutella Pancakes were fantastic! They took a little longer than the rest of the food, but the entire staff was on top of it and made sure we were comfortable and happy the entire time. The crew here is top-notch and the food is well worth the wait. Definitely recommend this cozy spot for anyone passing through the busy streets of Venice!
label: Resident

name: David
review:
The food was amazingly fun.
From what I gather everyone goes here for the ¡°pancakes¡±. They are very light and fluffy, more like a meringue.
Very small establishment, there is a fresh pasta place right next door as well. There are warning of pickpockets everywhere. We never had any issues. Service and food was great all around. In most
Places in Europe we learned you must ask for the check. Or you will be waiting.
label: Resident

name: E
review: Wanted a egg omelette and came to the right place. We didn't make reservations and ended waiting about 3-5min in a que outside, the place was busy was seated quickly. I had the egg omelette and partner had the fluffy pancakes eggs Benedict type plate along with Apersol and cappuccino! Everything was delicious and would recommend if your looking for a non-traditional Italian breakfast.
label: Resident

name: Micheal Ostoforoff
review:
Nice place! Great service, excellent food, decent price.  We all enjoyed our meals!
The fluffy pancakes were delicious!  I had a savoury version with eggs and bacon - and ate everything! Including the salad on the side,  and I don't generally do that at breakfast...
Presentation on the plate was exceptionally well done.
Definitely recommend.
label: Resident

name: Matthew Drinkall
review: WOW!! Amazing breakfast had by all here! Bit of a wait to get a table, but the line moved quickly. Lovely cosy indoor seating. Kind, friendly service with quick food delivery. The highlight was the gluten free pancakes- they were out of this world! How they make them so fluffy and tasty, we would love to know! Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Matt
review:
This place was so good! We got in with no reservation luckily around 9am at the end of May. When we left there was a wait however.

We ordered a cappuccino and a caramel macchiato to drink. The macchiato was a nice looking drink and very delicious. Not overly sweet either. Cappuccino was pretty as well and tasted like a normal cappuccino.

For food we got the breakfast combo with eggs, bacon, and toast. Came with a salad with a pesto dressing that was delicious. This was a solid, simply meal.

The puffy pancakes we tried the trio of Nutella, mango/coconut, and salmon. All of these were really good! I may swap the salmon out if we were to order again but it was good. The other two I¡¯d definitely order again. They were great.

Service was good as well and nice we got in with no reservation. Weirdly the coffees were 1 euro more on our receipt than advertised on the menu, but no big deal. Was just something to note.
label: Resident

name: Jakob Bierbaumer
review:
The name doesnt do this justice, you can order everything at every time.. i think!
The Menus seem pretty good value aswell but i just went in for a quick snack.

I Got the Classic Avocado Toast and it was exactly what I needed.

Staff is very nice, the Atmosphere is calm & relaxed -oh and they are playing Christmas music in October.
Take from that what you will but to me thats a big plus??
label: Resident

name: Louisa Sampson
review: We had breakfast at this lovely local caf¨¦. Amazing croissants, especially the pistachio and the coffee is great too! A little off the beaten track but that just makes it feelore authentic. Our total was 7 for two coffees and two filled croissants. Highly recommend.
label: Resident

name: Sally-Ann Jackson-Chaplin
review: Definitely not in the most picturesque of areas  in Venice, but it's a charming little coffee shop tucked away in a quiet street. The pistachio croissants are the AMAZING and the coffee is tasty too! Feels like a locals spot but barista speaks fluent English and very friendly :)
label: Resident

name: Damjan Pushkovski
review: Amazing place, amazing coffee, amazing sandwiches. AMAZING!! Highly recommend, even though it only has two tables to sit!!
label: Resident

name: Mary Kate Charron
review: We had our morning cappuccino here every day in Venice and it was perfect. The coffee was delicious, the baristas were quick and kind. Thank you Boresso!
label: Resident

name: Solange Bohling
review: Really nice little place that we went to for breakfast multiple times. It was in a quiet area that was a nice break from the crowds and it seemed like a place the locals frequented. Good coffee and good pastries! Highly recommended :)
label: Resident

name: M Torres
review: A true gem in VeniceDa Mario & I Fioi is the kind of caf¨¦ you hope to stumble upon but rarely do. The coffee is rich and smooth, the croissants are fresh and flavorful, but its the warmth of the service that truly stands out. Piero and Donar treat every guest like family, creating an atmosphere thats as comforting as the food. A favorite among locals, this spot is welcoming, genuine, and absolutely worth a visit.
label: Resident

name: PaulS
review: It¡¯s a bar that happens to serve coffee. This is not a specialty coffee shop. Coffee was fine, nothing objectionable nor terribly exciting either. Did not enjoy the croissant. It was mushy and heavy. A good croissant is supposed to be light and crusty.
label: Resident

name: Cecilia Lu
review: Great coffee with simple local food from  two friendly good looking hosts - a great start to a perfect morning in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Brian Schoene
review: We visited this cafe both mornings we were in Venice in June.  The servers were very nice, the pastries and cappuccino were perfect.  If you are a chocolate lover try a Marocchino!
label: Resident

name: Eric Pieper
review: Great little cafe. The food it was so good we forgot to take pictures ahead is fine. Fun atmosphere and full of locals in the morning. Just what we needed in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Hannah KHoury
review: This place was awesome! Great coffee and delicious sandwiches. The staff and service were friendly and made recommendations. This is a great local spot to start your day!
label: Resident

name: Irene Tan
review:
A superb place for breakfast with a great boss and assistant. Coffee and sandwiches were yummy.
Thumbs up ??!!
label: Resident

name: Vanessa Ma
review: Not very friendly staff, pigeons flying into the indoors which is terrible, but probably the best cafe option around the Giardinni Biennale area that¡¯s of reasonable price and with a range of light food options, also pretty nice botanical environment (if there would not be pigeons!)
label: Resident

name: Chun-Han Chen
review: I went there 1 year ago. The food is just okay you can find other good choice nearby. And then they refuse me for one cappuccino, a very tipical breakfast, this year. Just remind you they just want take your money. If your order is not enough, they will kick you out the restaurant even though 50% of seats are empty. This restaurant is just for nice Instagram photos not food.??????????
label: Resident

name: Kuan Hou ¡°Angus¡± Lee
review: 7.5 euro for one drink and 2 ciccheti! This wasn't shown on the menu nor told to us, but luckily the staff were kind to do this deal by default if you order accordingly. Drinks were good. Food were good. Amazing place to come and relax after a long day walking around Venice!!
label: Resident

name: William Harrison
review: Excellent spot for a quick lunch in Venice.  The cicchetti we had all was delicious.  I asked one of the ladies for her recommendations and she suggested several of the small sandwiches; codfish, spicy salami, etc.  Not one disappointed, nor did the ice-cold draft beer.  Very reasonably priced and comfortable seating.
label: Resident

name: Amber
review:
Cute little cafe perfect for breakfast or a mid-day snack. They have the best croissants ever, they are so soft buttery and flaky (I had 5 ??). The donuts with cream, chocolate or pistachio filling are awesome too.

Nice ambiance with Italian music playing in the background. Staff are smiling and pleasant to interact with.
label: Resident

name: Lou
review:
We walked all the way here from other side of town and it was totally worth it. Beautiful sandwiches and delish coffee (take extra shot if espresso with your oat cappuccino if that¡¯s your usual coffee)
Beef and turkey sandwiches r to die for ???? Perfecto
label: Resident

name: Bella Reviews
review: One of the best pistachio croissants I¡¯ve had in Italy so far! I got a second one right after! It¡¯s warm inside, the pistachio is a bit melted. Perfect for a cold morning! Went at 9-10am on a Sunday.
label: Resident

name: Patr¨ªcia
review: The croissants and cappuccinos were excellent; fresh, tasty, and definitely worth trying. The staff was very friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. The only downside is that it gets quite busy, so there was a bit of a line, and we were lucky to find a spot to sit. That said, the view is absolutely amazing, right in Venice overlooking the canal, which makes it a perfect place to enjoy a coffee and pastry.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review: Just stopped here to grab an afternoon Cappuccino, was pleased to see that they had non-dairy milk options to pick from. By far the most reasonably priced coffee I had in Venice. It was also one of the better coffees I had in my time here. Vibes were nice, sitting right in front of a canal / square.
label: Resident

name: Zena T
review: Great place to grab a slice of pizza and go. Perfect to eat something good and fast if you don't want to loose time in a restaurant. The location is pretty small, suitable for 1-2 let's say 3 persons. I had a slice of pizza, and ice coffee and a bottle of water and I paid 12.5 euros.
label: Resident

name: Cate Carroll
review: A little gem in Venice with coffee and panini for our brunch. Easy to find from the ferry and using Google maps! Outdoor seating facing the Maddalena church and square and close to canal with frequent gondolas passing. Staff were warm and friendly and in spite of efforts to speak Italian, they responded in English for clarification.
label: Resident

name: Suresh Kumili
review:
It's my favorite bakery in Venice. My daily routine was to wake up and head straight to 1000 bakery. The fresh croissants and refreshing espresso would wake me up and prepare for the 25K steps I would do each day :)

Try their flagship croissant, which is with pistachio filling. If you do not like sweet pastry in the morning, you can try their butter croissant as well. It's equally good.
label: Resident

name: Dana Montes
review:
I visited on weekday. The place has inside and outside places to eat. The pizza was nice and the ingredients were good but I won¡¯t say is the best though a good one and the price was really good compared to other restaurants in the area.
The staff is friendly, there¡¯s toilets, the place is clean and overall was a good experience.
label: Resident

name: Kevin Barker
review:
We got to Venice very late and decided to checkout this Panini place. Unfortunately at 8:00 PM they were all out of cicchetti which we were looking forward to trying with the sandwich. I saw many other places still had them, but it is more of a early day food.

With that said, we ended up getting a panini and it tasted really good, I asked for it to be toasted and the server ended up having a conversation with locals while the food was cooking, so as you can see, it got burnt. Overall, it tasted good, but the service left a lot to be desired.
label: Resident

name: Eric Witiw
review: The cicchetti were awesome and the staff are so friendly and kind. Great place to stop for a coffee and some food!
label: Resident

name: Emily Jarrard
review: Best cappuccino and croissant I¡¯ve had in Italy so far! The two women working were lovely and the vibes sitting outside were perfect :)
label: Resident

name: Nia
review:
Kind staff that was very good answering all of our questions about the menu. They had something to accommodate all of our needs - a meat lover, a kid that doesn¡¯t like vegetables and a vegetarian :)
Paninis were fresh, prepared quickly and tasted great. The prices were very reasonable too. Thumbs up!
label: Resident

name: Vlad Ivancu
review: I stopped at Caff¨¨ del Doge and had a double espresso and tiramisu. The coffee was excellent ¨C strong, intense, and just a bit bitter, but in a positive way that really highlights the flavor. The tiramisu was classic and well made, a proper version of this iconic dessert. Cozy atmosphere and a great spot for real Italian coffee in Venice. Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Andrew Yit
review: Best coffee i had in italy in 10 days. They sell beans from various places in the world. Pastries are amazing. My barista is a cool chap
label: Resident

name: Tanya Carlson
review: The coffee is amazing. Smooth, strong but not bitter. The service is friendly, helpful (as we spoke little Italian) and the food is fresh. Be sure to try the pistachio croissant. We will be back.
label: Resident

name: Richard Owen
review:
Real fresh milk in the coffee! This was the first place we found that used fresh whole milk for the flat white. They are a proper coffee shop with beans sourced from all over the world for you to choose from.

We had a cannoli as well which was divine.

Unfortunately, I did not think to review this place until we had finished.
label: Resident

name: tim 84
review: The taste of cappuccino is amazing. We tasted the best cappuccino in Venice.
label: Resident

name: Zhenqing YING
review: Very lovely restaurant . Maya is adorable and the food is delicious! We love this place.
label: Resident

name: G Sash
review: A little hole in the wall with. Fabulous atmosphere. A 5 min walk from the main bus and taxi station on Venice. Great for breakfast and some veggie options available.
label: Resident

name: Mayank Sharma
review: Lovely little place close to the train station. This is my second visit to this place and they have great chicchetti along with chichettos (mini sandwiches). Service is pleasant and cocktails were quick. Overall this place has great vibes
label: Resident

name: angiewong600
review: We were on the way to the Piazzale Roma bus station and spotted this little bar along the happening riverbank. We went in to enquire about the food and we were very pleased with the friendly boss who introduced the food to us. It is his friendliness that attracted  us. The food we ordered were Lagsana ,Spagetti and Italian popular drinks , Spritz Aperol. Very authentic italian food and drinks! Highly recommended to All.
label: Resident

name: Laura Zarina
review: Very sweet staff who was very kind and helpful! Their menu online isn't really the same as on the location, but it doesn't matter cause the service was superb! And the sandwiches were so good! Same with the prices!??
label: Resident

name: Gabi Sz
review: I really enjoyed my late breakfast here!! c:
label: Resident

name: Maggie Chang
review: This was one of the best paninis I have ever had. I got the vegan panini (and I¡¯m not strictly vegan or vegetarian) of roasted seitan, beetroot salad, caramelized onions, and artichoke. I ordered at the bar and was lucky to snag a table outside right along the canal. The panini was made fresh to order. The bread was perfectly toasted and so hot and fluffy. Sandwich was really flavorful. I was really impressed. I¡¯m thinking of going back just for that sandwich alone with my few days here in Venice. I grabbed a spritz bianco for my drink for 3.5 euros. Really light on the alcohol, but decent for the price.
label: Resident

name: Tim Brown
review: There is a notice outside that they don't do cicchetti. However, what they do serve are platters and big sandwiches with cheeses and cooked meats. My small cheese platter was actually a generous size and my glass of red wine was excellent.
label: Resident

name: Marija Lagutina
review: Great freshly made panini. You can choose from the menu or create your own panini. Also you can get selection of cheese and meat accompanied by your favorite drink. Canal view is great.
label: Resident

name: Max Urazaev
review: Love this place! Truly wine connoisseurs, they gave us the opportunity to taste several varieties of wine, perfectly meeting our needs. We'll definitely be back!
label: Resident

name: Alice M
review:
Really delicious panini. We created our own which was great and so tasty & fresh. The bread was still slightly warm!
The staff were very friendly and helped us decide. Good selection of wines and everything is excellently priced!

Nice little seating area around the side by the canal.
Would¡¯ve gone back if I had more time in Venice!
label: Resident

name: lara addison
review: Incredible service and the wine was amazing. It was great value, had lots of choice and great grazing board options
label: Resident

name: Kevin Lottis
review: I chose this cafe because I heard they are serving some of the best coffee in Venice and it's true. There were welcoming and friendly to us. It seemed like mostly locals outside which is another reason to come here. The pastry and cakes were delicious.
label: Resident

name: Arwin
review: I stopped by Bottega del Caff¨¨ Dersut and was pleasantly surprised by the quality of their fresh juice. I ordered the L¡¯Arancione and it was truly refreshing ¡ª vibrant, naturally sweet, and bursting with freshness. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients. The atmosphere is cozy, with a relaxed Venetian charm, and the staff are welcoming and attentive. It¡¯s the perfect little spot to take a break, enjoy something light, and soak in the local vibe. Highly recommended if you¡¯re looking for a genuine and refreshing drink experience in Venice. ????
label: Resident

name: Jake (developerjake)
review:
Very inhospitable staff. Wouldn't let my partner use the restroom without purchasing something, so she bought a coffee, and while waiting for her we sat on the chairs outside. Once she came outside with her coffee they gave us a total of 5 seconds on their seats before warning us that we couldn't sit there because the coffee was takeaway unless we wanted to pay them more. We were literally reading the map to see which way we were going, and every table except ours was empty... They really set a low bar, especially compared to some of the other lovely local restaurants.

I'd recommend you stay away.
label: Resident

name: Ella Taubman
review: I honestly wish I could give this cafe a higher  rating they are truthfully one of the kindest staff caf¨¦s. I have been to in Italy. I have been studying abroad here in Venice and this coffee shop has been my go to every single morning before class as an American. I truly do enjoy my iced coffee and they have been nothing but kind to make me an iced coffee every single day. Their croissants are better than any I¡¯ve ever had and they truly make my morning better.
label: Resident

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos

review: Great coffee bar in central location on the Lido. Great atmosphere and always great music. Super friendly service. Nice decor.
label: Resident

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos
name: §¡§Ý§Ö§ß§Ñ §³

review: Amazing pastries, great coffee and kind service
label: Resident

review: Great value. A lot of local people.
label: Resident

place_title: CAFES DO BRASIL di Maria Jose Dos Passos

review: It¡¯s a very friendly place and the prices are really low.

name: Sam Symons
review: Barman was a legend! Had a quiet beer and a feed for a great price, and had some great conversation as well. Thanks!
label: Resident

name: Ma?y Pierd
review:
Very nice caf¨¦ with lots of things to order
Cappucino costed 1.8e and was, to my pleasant surprise, bigger than any other in the town for this price. Taste mild, not burned- it was nice :D
Very good place to hide when it's hot outside. Not a lot of noise from the outside was big plus for us
label: Resident

name: Kristina James
review: We spent a lot of time there. A super nice bartender (the owner) I would come back anytime ????????????
label: Resident

name: James
review:
Cappuccino was nice,  has a slight bittered taste.
Seems like it's an authentic place, as there is no "to go" option and no place to sit, so be ready to stay.
Also you can buy coffee beans inside

They accept card payment.
label: Resident

name: Reaction Timing
review: Decent creamy coffee (3/5) friendly service, best prices coffee in Venice, nostalgic atmosphere could do with some work and some seating, heaps of potential serving coffee like that.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: ModeParChocolat
review:
We found the bakery and stuck to it for breakfast for the entire time in Venice.
The pizza were quite something. We took them and saved it for later and STILL just has yummy. We came back for the pastries and it did not disappoint.

As I type this, I¡¯m missing Venice even more. What a place
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Michael Moore
review: This place looks amazing as you walk by and makes you want to immediately come in. It was pretty busy as we entered around 1030 and hoped it was worth all the hype. Instead of something sweet we opted with two savory options with their quiche and ham and cheese sandwich. The quiche was delicious for a couple of warm bites but then there were a couple of cold bites since it was not heated all the way. The ham and cheese sandwich was ok. The bread and ham had no really taste but the cheese was really good. I would recommend other options in Venice if you have the chance.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Richard Pettigrew
review: Pizzas are wonderful! Square and a thicker base than the usual ones you get in Venice. Very tasty sauce. We got extremely kind customer service following an incident with a seagull!
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Julia H?rmayer
review: We had high hopes but the desserts weren¡¯t quite as good as expected. We thought the berry tart had custard but it was really just whipped cream with sugar, nothing special. The coffee was good. The small tart was very good according to my mum.
label: Resident

place_title: Majer - Santa Margherita Bakery Pastry Coffee Bar
name: Katharina P.
review: Very good coffee and amazing pistachio croissants. We enjoyed our breakfast a lot! ??
label: Resident

name: Pandaz
review:
I stopped by after the museum to refuel.
There's a wide selection of food (pastries, cakes, little bites or even meals) and beverages (soft drinks, coffee, cocktails etc.)
Service was quick and super friendly. The experience was pleasant with an exceptional view of the square.
Price was reasonable compared to the neighbourhood!
I quite enjoyed the tiramisu??
label: Resident

name: Nurettin Tuncer
review: Nice place to take a break, and if you¡¯re lucky, you can get a table with a beautiful view of St. Mark¡¯s Basilica. Perfect spot for a coffee or dessert while enjoying the scenery.
label: Resident

name: Ethan Choi
review:
This is a place where you can enjoy exceptionally cheap and delicious coffee compared to other cafes in the square. It used to be Napoleon's office, and you can access it by going up to the museum entrance. Since it¡¯s a small space, seats might not always be available (a museum ticket is not required).

You¡¯ll need to go through a security check upon entry, so don¡¯t be surprised. Once inside, head to the second floor, and you¡¯ll see it on the left.
label: Resident

name: Nick Gray
review: The view over San Marco is the obvious star but good coffee and cake, with willing attentive service makes this a great place to rejuvenate.  We visited after a couple of hours walking around the museum, very pleased to take a breath here before continuing to the next sight-seeing adventure.
label: Resident

name: Jackson Lam
review: Good coffee and croissants. Had a nice view of the square, the basilica and the tower. Good quiet location as well. Note: need to pass security checkpoint but need not have a ticket for museum.
label: Resident

name: Jonathan Lane
review: Location, location, location! In the middle of many overpriced and overrun cafes in the Piazza San Marco, this was a breath of fresh air. I imagine it remains quieter because you need to enter through the museum security to get to it. Provided perfect views of the Piazza and a quiet place to retreat to during the busy day.
label: Resident

name: Javier Sanguinetti
review:
Great spot to grab a coffee with views to Piazza San Marco. If you are able to get one of the tables by the window it would probably be the best coffee spot in the piazza.
The prices are really good considering the setting.
You must go through a queue and security because it shares entrance with museum
label: Resident

name: Beth Miller
review: We came here a few times back in March 2024 and returned today for really delicious and fresh juice, coffee, super fresh sandwiches and a really lovely employee. This place is immaculate, well priced for what it is, and in a quiet and clean neighborhood with easy vaporetto access. I think the negative reviews come from people not understanding the type of sandwiches they'll get; there aren't sauces, spreads, or condiments that come on these sandwiches, just fresh ingredients and freshly sliced deli meats, which can be super delicious. No bathrooms. When you tell the sandwich maker that you'd like take away, they, without fail, warn you of the seagulls, which is kind and not something many people would do. If your children like sandwiches without a tomato or whatever, it's easy to remove for them and still share a great sandwich.
label: Resident

name: Shoon Lae Lae Oo
review:
The fresh lettuce & tomato with the big size of sandwich humbles my stomach!!
I love the way the food is yummy while having the fresh taste of vegetables inside the sandwich!!!
The best to try in Venice!
label: Resident

name: Roman Goncharenko
review: My god, those sandwiches are absolutely delicious! A rare find in Venice¡ªgrab one and head straight to Punta della Salute to enjoy it with the view!

name: Christos Ioannou
review:
Caff¨¨ Florian in San Marco Square is one of those rare places where history, elegance, and atmosphere blend into an unforgettable experience. The moment you step inside, you¡¯re transported to another era¡ªthe interior is refined, romantic, and steeped in Venetian grandeur, with gilded details and antique charm that make simply sitting there feel special.

The live music adds a magical touch to the setting. Listening to the ensemble play as the piazza glows outside is nothing short of enchanting¡ªtruly the soundtrack of Venice.

As for the treats, the "Sunrise Cake" was absolutely delightful¡ªfresh, bright, and beautifully balanced. And the signature "Florian Spritz" is a must-try: refined, refreshing, and unique, a perfect Venetian indulgence. While most offerings shine, the chocolate cake was a rare miss for me¡ªsurprisingly flat in flavor compared to the rest and slightly overbaked.

Despite that small hiccup, Caff¨¨ Florian remains a timeless Venetian icon. Come for the beauty, stay for the music, and savor the flavors that make this historic caf¨¦ so special. A quintessential Venice experience.

Was light throw and slam but was not necessary because I already finished my order. Still giving 3 stars for Morning staffs were very friendly with big smiles
label: Resident

name: Elizabeth Heiner
review: Delicious food. Ignore the comments regarding rude service. We did our best to speak Italian and it¡¯s amazing what happens when you try to speak the native language. We saw some very rude Americans which was embarrassing for us as fellow Americans. We ordered cappuccinos, caffe, and hot chocolate along with a myriad of sandwiches. Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Hiten Umarnani
review: Firstly served cold food despite clearly stating on the menu that the food was hot. Secondly, it was incredibly overpriced with the menus online being cheaper clearly lying about the prices. Thirdly, we specifically asked for non seeded bread as a member of our group had a severe food allergy, it wasn't even a modification as on the menu the bread was non seeded. Yet still they served our freezing cold sandwiches with seeded bread because they ran out apparently. After this attempted homicide, they refused to give us a new sandwich and that member had to go outside. There's specifically a bearded waiter/manager who was incredibly rude. He shouted at us  to not drink outside drinks despite the fact that we only had the bottles in our bags and gave us a darn ultimatum to leave despite people on our table already having food. Genuinely, one of the worst experiences I've ever had in a restaurant. This is worse than some  inner city Denny's I've been in.
label: Resident

name: Martin Kluk
review: Not sure how anyone could complain about this shop. The service and food/drinks were great and the prices were very reasonable compared to the insane prices of the square down the road. Thanks for having us!
label: Resident

name: Nadya Fetisova
review: We stayed next door, and came for breakfast the day we were leaving. Esther, the owner, was very nice and welcoming, and explained the menu for us. Service was fast, coffee was great, all good impression. I also liked how they bring coffee in big bright cups all colour mixed, very cheerful. They also sell packaged coffee, we got it as a nice souvenir from Italy.
label: Resident

name: Craig Bentley
review:
Valerio  and Ricardo hosted us today on2 separate sittings.

You could not ask for a nicer or more welcoming restaurant to eat and drink.

The drinks are fresh and fast as soon s you order. As well as the foot and ice cream.

I will always come back here.

Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Noemi Garcia
review:
wish I could give this place zero stars. From the second we sat down, the staff (guy in the picture) was unwelcoming. Our group of five simply wanted to order cappuccinos, but we were told that they ¡°don¡¯t serve cappuccino to five people.¡± They refused to explain and instead told us to leave. The guy put his hand on my niece. YOU DON¡¯T DO THAT, especially to a CHILD.

This was hands down the worst service I¡¯ve ever encountered. If you expect even a basic level of respect as a customer, do yourself a favor and take your service where you¡¯ll be respected!
label: Resident

name: Yuriy Yakovenko
review: I should have checked google reviews prior to going there. They brought me a cold cappuccino. When I pointed that out, they just added some hot water, so it didn¡¯t taste like anything at all. No apology. Paid 24 euros for just two croissants, watered down cappuccino and horrendous service. Avoid this place. There are a lot of good places around.
label: Resident

name: A L
review: This was our favourite bacaro in Venice. We returned here a few times as it was so central and lively in the evening. The bar is right on the steps of a bridge, next to large open area and church which makes it the perfect spot for a select spritz and cicchetti. Well recomended.
label: Resident

name: Patrick Kluver
review: Fantastic experience. Great value and quality for money! Best priced cicchetti in town! Meat 2, fish 2.50. We¡¯ll be back!
label: Resident

name: Ricky Lamar
review:
This little bar and cicchetteria has it going on! Georgia with her mischievous twinkle will mix you up a traditional Italian spritzer offering up tasty tapas to tempt those taste buds.
And Lorenzo, in charge of the fun made this a night to remember.
label: Resident

name: Nima Lama
review: We made a quick stop for some drinks and ended up trying their cicchetti. It was delicious! The staff were super friendly, and we were lucky to get a seat right by the airco. Grazie mille!
label: Resident

name: Rhakria and or Embisch
review:
Impeccable bacaro experience.
I'll list some positives :
- Wine selection, as well as enough other drinks for those who don't want wine
- freshly prepared Cicchetti with enough variety to suit all tastes
- very stylish music playlist of classic 80-90s disco
- lovely / lively people behind the counter

The negatives :
- can't think of one...

Thank you for the well spent evening ??
label: Resident

name: David Wilson
review: We stopped in for a coffee and water.  Everyone was so friendly and the cappuccinos were VERY tasty. Grazie Mille!
label: Resident

name: Avy-Loren Cohen
review:
A lovely place to have coffee and a variety of pastries and baked good such as a surprisingly delicious croissant.
Food is delicious and fresh.
Service is polite and friendly.
Atmosphere is modern and clean and merchandise is clean as well.
Reasonably good value.

Great place to nosh
label: Resident

name: Sara
review:
I was looking for a good pistacchio croissant and couldn't find it...One older lady told me there is pasticceria so I checked and it was amazing!
I tried pistacchio croissant and also frittella, they were absolutely great!
label: Resident

name: Valeriya Smirnova
review: Awesome lemon roll cake - very tender cream, not too sweet - just perfect
label: Resident

name: Alexis Kokonuzz
review: Cant speak about the croissants, maybe those are good, but the pistacchio cannoli is disappointment made pastry. Terrible artificial cream flavor, horrible value for money, sad sad sad.
label: Resident

name: RnV Marty
review: We tried a few flavors of cannoli and they were all delicious. Glad we stopped in.
label: Resident

name: Alex O.
review: No pistachio in my cannoli, only sugar
label: Resident

name: David Zemdegs
review: Lovely place for coffee and pastries.
label: Resident

name: Mike Tretola
review: Wanted to try the cannolis that were in the window and were highly disappointed. The filling was like a whipped cream, not traditional ricotta or mascarpone. Cappuccinos were excellent, though.
label: Resident

name: zeke monroe
review: Fantastic spot, the first night we arrived in Venice we had arrived late and even though they were in the process of closing the man running the shop, Sandro, sold us some water. The next day we came up he remembered us and set us up with a fantastic meal and even set some roses and a lamp on the table. Ordered the Focaccia Ricotta E Spinaci and the Tiramisu. 10/10 recommend. You will not be disappointed.
label: Resident

name: Allie Arnaiz
review:
Great food for the price, meals came with so much. A coffee, entree, and a bread. Really good food. Good place to stop at if you're hungry in the morning before going further into the city.
Great view
Don't specifically remember the service but I feel like that is a good thing. Not exceptional but not bad.
label: Resident

name: Gabriela Gisca
review:
J¡¯ai bien aim¨¦ les pasta ???? it was so delicious
I recommend it for price and for its taste
label: Resident

name: Nonso Come
review:
A nice little spot in Venice
Stopped by for a quick coffee and snack. The service was friendly and the atmosphere cozy. Prices were reasonable for the area. Nothing fancy, but a pleasant place to take a short break while exploring the city.
label: Resident

name: Bosanski Ljiljan
review: Great Pizza, low Prices and a great Customer Service
label: Resident

name: Susanna Kantelinen
review: The Bellini was beautiful and the vegetarian chicetti delicious and fresh. My waitress, Alice, was just wonderful.
label: Resident

name: Dione
review: I speak English & staff were very understanding while I ordered. I young lady remembered me from the day before & knew my order, so excellent service
label: Resident

name: Monika Esseov¨¢
review: The coffee was good, but the toilet could have been cleaner, and there was only 1  available.
label: Resident

name: Cellist ESTHER
review:
The steps are really friendly and fast. I was happy with the service they provided.
The inside of the store was pretty clean and well organized. If you hate waiting in line, I recommend you go in the morning.
label: Resident

name: Fi Lip
review: Franchise owner, can you please review the toilet maintenance in this starbucks. Very inconvenient and totally visitor unfriendly to keep the toilet closed for a long time
label: Resident

name: Bryan
review: Tiny place with fresh gelato with delicious flavors. This was probably the best gelato I had in Venice. The owner was very friendly. Definitely recommend, if in the area and it was fun to eat next to a not-so-busy canal.
label: Resident

name: Kris
review: Easy 5 stars! Friendly, reasonably priced, no queues and¡­ amazing ice cream!! The best we had all summer!
label: Resident

name: G M
review: What to say? A very good ice cream spot in Venezia: great location near the gondolas workshop, many students in the area as well, great ambiance and of course very tasty ice cream: few choices for an efficient selection with creamy and tasty ice creams. Very nice welcoming and service level, I recommend the place.
label: Resident

name: Moon
review: This was such a nice gelato shop. It was so tasty and creamy. The guy was also very friendly. Wish I could come back here again.
label: Resident

name: na na
review: Maybe I would have rated this place more highly were it located anywhere else other than the country of amazing gelato. The texture was too icy and hard, more like regular ice cream and not really like gelato ¡ª the picture and shape of the scoop already shows this is not a regular gelato. The flavors we got, pistacchio (recommended by other reviewers) and nocciola (always a safe choice) tasted way too artificial. I¡¯d gladly go to a chain before coming back here.
label: Resident

name: alan chang
review: Amazing flavours! The owner is super friendly. I got the salted Carmel and pistachio.
label: Resident

name: Oliver Wehner
review:
Never go there with children.
Very unfriendly.
Children where sitting calmly on the table playing some Table games.
After the second warning we were requested to leave.
They were never loud nor disturbing someone.
Just ridiculous.
Never again
label: Resident

name: Eric3 Skywalker
review:
Sat down at a table at 2pm to have a dessert and a drink. Most of the other tables were empty. We got sent away because "they only accepted people for lunch".
Clowns...
label: Resident

name: Alan Hewitt
review: Lovely atmosphere by yhe Rialto bridge with the water right next to you. The service was very friendly, but the food was average. Not enough flavor or brightness in the pasta dishes. Better value for similar prices elsewhere.

name: Jo?e Habinc
review: All three pizzas we tried tasted great, the service was solid and the price, considering the location, was also good. However, there were a lot of leftovers on the tables (but everyone could clean up after themselves, as they have trash cans installed), the pigeons between the tables were not pleasant to watch, and what bothered me the most was the strong operation of the air conditioners and ceiling fans. They really overdo it with the cooling.
label: Resident

name: Bhawana Agarwal
review:
?? 5 stars

One of the best pizza places you can try¡ªan absolute hidden gem! The pizza is a must-have, even if you¡¯re not hungry. It¡¯s that good! A total steal for the quality you get. Perfect for dine-in or takeaway, and the coffee is just as amazing. Friendly and welcoming staff make the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit¡ªBon app¨¦tit! ??
label: Resident

name: Moji gh
review: We ordered two caff¨¨ lattes and two croissants. The coffee was average but acceptable compared to other places in Italy. The croissants were fresh and tasty. The main downside was the service ¡ª the staff member who served us was unfriendly and unwelcoming.
label: Resident

name: Kaitlyn Affuso
review:
The BEST pizza I had while in Italy.
Went to Venice, Florence, and Rome and this was by FAR my favorite. We visited several times while we were in Venice for 3.5 days.

Highly reccommend. Get several different slices and share because they're all delicious!
label: Resident

name: CCG G
review: Good place for croissants and fresh yogurt with fresh fruit and granola. The coffee was honestly one of the grossest coffee ups I¡¯ve ever tasted - it was very burnt I just threw it away. Place is small with limited seating so it¡¯s chaotic and not a good place to sit and eat. Highly recommend if you¡¯re craving yogurt though.
label: Resident

name: Ficho Stainov
review: I sat down in the Caf¨¦ on a weekday in October and I had to work for a few hours on my laptop. The coffee, food and Aperol were great and the staff was friendly and did not object me working there. The prices are also reasonable for Venice. All in all a great caf¨¦ and good for home office.
label: Resident

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
We stopped at this caf¨¦ for a quick coffee, even though we had seen some negative reviews beforehand. The prices for coffee at the table are on the higher side (I know it¡¯s common to have a difference compared to the counter, but it still felt a bit much). The coffee itself was fine, though not among the best we tried in Italy.

The atmosphere was lively, and we noticed that local regulars were greeted very warmly by the staff, which gave the place a certain charm. Our own experience was more neutral ¡ª the service was efficient but not particularly attentive. Overall, it¡¯s a stylish caf¨¦, though perhaps better suited for locals who already know it well.
