name: Jess Owen
review: If you want good chocolate in Venice this is the place. The cashier was so friendly and was more than happy to talk me through the flavours and give her own recommendations. They all looked so delicious I couldn't choose and had one of each and some chocolate bark and it only came to 25 which is an insanely good bargain. I'm a patisserie chef myself and after trying them I was immediately wondering if I had time to go back!
label: Resident

name: Beverly Zygmunt
review: While there is a beautiful selection of chocolate candies in the shop, I only had the hot chocolate and pastries. The hot chocolate is thick, dark, and delicious. I had it twice, with whipped cream. One day I had it with a piece of dense chocolate cake. For my second outing, I had a salted-caramel topped brownie. Everything was excellent and the staff are very friendly.
label: Resident

name: Deepak Deshpande
review: Amazing falafel wraps. The falafels were sooooooo good and fresh. Super good for vegetarian vegan folks. Gujarati, Tamil Brahmins, Jain folks from India who are strict vegetarian will love this place as the kitchen is vegetarian (no meat but they instead use substitute like beyond meat).
label: Resident

name: Julia Fleisher
review: So grateful Gam Gam Goodies exists! It¡¯s the only ¡°dairy¡± kosher in Venice. They have some gelato, a fridge section of some pre packaged kosher grocery goods (like salami, cheeses etc), wine and snacks. This is a take away place, you order and go, but sooo good! We got pizza after Shabbat (which was totally worth sending my husband while I stayed in the Airbnb with our sleeping toddler) and in the morning got a breakfast pita with fresh pita. The dough is made in house in front of you and it is a delicious! The cookies and baked goods are also amazing. They are recipes from the Rebbetzin of the community and so so good!
label: Resident

name: Daniel Heideman
review: This pizza GETS THE JOB DONE. When you¡¯re walking around Venice taking in the scenery and you suddenly get hungry, this is a great place to go. It¡¯s not near the shops but it¡¯s a good walk and the pizza is worthy of the walk. They also sell funky packaged salami and other meats. The guy making the pizza was super nice and suggestive, and the pizza was good. Not the best, but does the job! Pizza flop was 5/10 and slice overall was 6.1/10
label: Resident

name: Shmuel dovid Uhlman
review:
Tasty kosher pizza, falafel and a wide variety of freshly made pastries. ??
You can also purchase wine beer or kosher basics like cheese and cold cuts. ??
Take out only-but you can eat you food in the nearby chabad house ??.
label: Resident

name: Rivky
review: Such a plus to have delicious food with high kashrus standards , was delicious there pizza on late motzia shabbos , kids loved there gelato, they made us very good egg scrambled sandwiches, . They were unbelievable accommodating when we asked they should prepare us 8am our sandwiches , everything was ready , neat and gusto.
label: Resident

name: Kellyanne Matt
review:
Most amazing cicchetti and aperol spritz
The prawns are so fresh and delicious. The bruschetta is amazing. The mozzarella incredible. The speck was delicious. The cannoli the kids went mad for them !  Just is such a lovely little Hidden gem in Venice. Wonderful staff. Amazing food!
We came one day in the morning and absolutely loved it so we came back in the evening the next day
label: Resident

name: Sara Fischer
review: Overall, meh. Bread was very fresh. Cannelloni was alright and served in a weird white sauce. Should have been in a pomodoro sauce or alfredo. We also ordered breaded fish and fries. Fries were good. Fish looked like it was out of the box. Waiter was a bit lax, so had to go to the bar to get my own ice. Overall, edible and cheap.
label: Resident

name: Elleevy
review: Actually, a pretty decent place! The staff was friendly, the food was delicious and the prices were normal. Total VFM. It could be a bit more polished.
label: Resident

name: Facundo Cesa
review:
Our experience wasn't as bad as others I've read. They didn't try to con us at least.

But it's true, this was the absolute worst cannolo in the world. If by accident you try this disgusting thing filled with a sugary hydrogenated vegetal crap, please, don't believe you've tried an ACTUAL cannolo.

The waitress didn't understand our Italian. Which, come on... Isn't THAT bad...

Maybe Italian wasn't her first language either. Maybe she comes from a country without formaggio grattugiato or smiles.
label: Resident

name: Joana Pack Melo Sousa
review: The worst coffe shop ever!! The taste of the cannolli was greasy. The price of the cappuccino outside says 3,50. They charge 6,00 inside + 2 euros of service. They were rude when we asked about the price.
label: Resident

name: Mitch Tornquist
review: DO NOT be fooled by the great location and beautiful window display. Absolute worst cannolo I¡¯ve ever had in my life. In fact I¡¯m not sure how they could have made it any worse. The filling tasted like icing and sugar. Sickeningly sweet. I did not finish it, and that¡¯s crazy, cause I eat everything.
label: Resident

name: F.M. Zowczak
review: Terrible service, you have to pay table cover fee - 2 per person (even though you¡¯ve ordered 2 small cookies). Food is very poor quality. Cookies are extremely sweet without any flavor (it¡¯s so sweet that you want to vomit). Toilets are not clean, tables are placed to close to each other so you can¡¯t barely move. Do not recommend¡­
label: Resident

name: Giovanni Balzi
review: Don¡¯t fund this kind of business, the guy overcharged a spremuta just because we sat down and this extra charge was not specified anywhere for the client to see. The person at the counter was very rude when he confronted us. I would say to stay away from this type of establishment.
label: Resident

name: Leo Babayan
review: Place was one of the only spots open late so we went without checking the reviews, we spend $49 euros for 3 ppl and we all got sandwiches, let me tell you the sandwich¡¯s at the gas station and train stations taste way better. It was plan and 1 start out of 5. The workers were nice but food sucks l.
label: Resident

name: john fing
review: We stopped in for coffee and tea very early one morning. Now asking for a black tea with milk on the side is not a usual Italian request. Accomplished, water in a pot steaming hot, good quality tea brand and milk on the side in a little jug. Came with a little biscuit on the saucer and a sachet of honey. The coffee was, well, coffee, not great but also not bad, a well made average coffee.
label: Resident

name: Kiara Charlier
review: just an incredible bakery, the girls and i came back like 4 times during our 5 days in venice!  any and all cornetti are amazing, honestly pick any pastry and you¡¯ll have the best time ever! heads up: lots of pigeons outside!
label: Resident

name: Magdalena
review:
We ate pizza by the slice, croissants with pistachio and almonds, a ham sandwich and drank coffee, everything was delicious and fresh. The only downside was the pigeons that tried to eat our breakfast..
P.S. in the middle of the cafe we ??left our notarial deed in a white folder, please destroy and throw it away.
label: Resident

name: Jason Lin
review: Amazing, definitely worth the walk. After a gorgeous walk along the south coast of Venice and then along this equally gorgeous street there¡¯s this wonderful spot. The tea smelled so good and tasted amazing with a packet of sugar and the pastry was just as good. So cheap too. I got the ¡°red tea¡± some type of fruit tea made of wild berries, hibiscus, and rose hips. There¡¯s also outdoor seating so you can enjoy the wonderful atmosphere.
label: Resident

name: Erik B.
review: Very nice cafe/snack bar. Early in the morning you can have breakfast and they have pizzas and paninnis during the day. Definitely worth it.
label: Resident

name: Sirodjah Datzkey
review:
Absolutely best sweets and cakes. Not extremely sweet and balanced textures.
Frendly and experienced staff. Guys if you read it - thank you very much!
Strongly recommended place.
label: Resident

name: Max Urazaev
review:
We spent great time having coffee with astonishing views on Ducale Palazzo, Basilica San Marco and the bay.
The price is higher than average but I believe you pay here for the atmosphere and splendid views. Staff was respectful and caring.
The only thing we could advice for the future is just make a clear disclaimer about price difference to have drinks here or take away.
label: Resident

name: baby shark
review: Everything was perfect ¡ª delicious food, wonderful wine, and excellent service. The staff were elegant and attentive, and the view of the lagoon made the experience unforgettable. Highly recommend this place if you want to feel the real Venice ?????
label: Resident

name: Julia Lee
review: Prob one of the best croissants I¡¯ve had ever. It was warm and slightly sweet from the brioche dough. I¡¯m def going to go again tomorrow before we leave Venice bc it was that good. I highly recommend it!
label: Resident

name: Alexandra Woolsey-Puffer
review: My favorite food shop in the neighborhood - great breads and pastries, good selection of wines and local spirits, plus choice meats and cheeses to make it easy to put together a snack to eat back at home or at the canal
label: Resident

name: Berlin Gal
review: This delicatessen shop became instantly my go-to-place for the most delicious artisan made cookies (!! highly addictive), small batch olive oil, craft beer, wines and so many other tasty products that are carefully curated from small and very often local makers. I also enjoy chatting with Luisa and Alessandro, the owners, about locally made and sourced foods. A hidden gem in Cannaregio!
label: Resident

1.	Morocchino 5/5

The menu has plenty of options, from different coffees and croissants to brioches, burgers and juices.

Here¡¯s what we ordered with our ratings
1.	Morocchino 5/5
2.	Cappuccino 4/5
3.	Brioche with tomato and mozzarella 4/5
4.	Brioche with ham, mozzarella and tomato 4/5

My macchiato is always amazing- good coffee in general.  I¡¯ve had a spritz before and it was not my favorite. To the negative reviews about prices changing, I can see this happening. We have had this happen once to us here but my husband said something and it was a mistake/ confusion on what we ordered and quickly corrected. My husband who is Italian will speak up however if there is a problem and it could be true they treat foreigners differently.  It is a local bar that many Venetians who live here go to. I have to say that staff/ women who serve us every morning however have been so kind and remember us. I love my perfect macchiato every day! *EDIT: For those who do not know, the price is different if you drink your coffee standing up at the bar as opposed to sitting outside. ?? Very disappointed with Bialetti moka pot (100 model)

The outer finish started fading and changing color after just a few weeks.

Coffee started tasting metallic, which is worrying for a high-priced item.

I contacted support ¡ª their advice? Clean it with baking soda (which is abrasive) and ¡°watch the temperature¡±, but they gave no specific guidelines.

One of our servers recommended a white wine (the Soave) and we thoroughly enjoyed.  I personally loved the music that was playing too - some good throwback songs in there! A magnificent restaurant in the heart of Venice. A pleasant, welcoming atmosphere. The service is excellent, and the staff are very friendly. The food was a delightful surprise! The waiter refused to provide a menu with current pricing and instead presented us with a different, more expensive menu than the one available online, resulting in inflated costs. A specific instance involved an order for rum, which was doubled in price and the incorrect brand was served. Furthermore, we encountered problems with the "plate of the day," which was not listed on any menu, and the staff were unhelpful in clarifying its cost. An unrequested and unannounced extra charge for burrata was also added to our bill after the waiter suggested adding it to a dish without disclosing the additional fee. Ultimately, when we questioned the bill, the waiter admitted to using a completely separate menu (not the one intended for customers) to determine our charges.

name: Alex
review:
My 3-year-old spent almost a month in Summerlin¡¯s PICU, and on the last day, I was informed they had opened a CPS case against me. I¡¯d been there every single day and night.

Their reasons? ¡°Housing concerns¡± because I brought some of her own toys. You should take a kid away for having toys, I guess. Most the toys in the room was even theirs but I guess the rotating staff didn¡¯t recognize that.

Another concern was her hygiene. There was no shower or wash basin in the room for me to clean her. I used wipes and paper towels just to keep her fresh. I brought hair supplies and changes of clothes from home. At every other hospital (Sunrise, St. Rose, and UMC), a nurse or CNA cleaned her and provided fresh gowns. Her stays there weren¡¯t nearly as long. At Summerlin, I had to bring personal clothes just to keep her changed. Instead of doing what I assumed was their job (a posted CNA checklist even includes ¡°bathes¡±), they told CPS I didn¡¯t do it.

They also said I ¡°slept too much.¡± Forgive me ¡ª had anyone shared that concern, I would have happily explained I¡¯m pregnant and exhausted after sleeping in a hospital chair for a month surrounded by alarms and crying babies. No one asked how I was doing, just judged. For that, I had 24 hours to take a drug test. Not that it mattered ¡ª but insulting and inconvenient to say the least.

The real tension started when my daughter¡¯s dad accidentally triggered her ankle alarm trying to take her to the gift shop for candy. His mistake upset staff, but I thought we¡¯d moved past it. Dr. Koo and her nurse even said she was allowed downstairs with notice, and that they would remove her tag if parents were present. That turned out to be false ¡ª because when he later asked again, they refused. They denied her own dad the chance to give her a short walk and candy bar after weeks without fresh air. Then they trespassed him and used that to start the CPS investigation. All he did was express frustration with words. There¡¯s a camera in the room that would show the truth.

That same ankle monitor left a rash or mark on her leg the first day, and no one explained how it happened or whether it was treated.

We are present, loving parents who were treated like criminals. I worry for the babies who don¡¯t have parents there daily ¡ª who¡¯s checking their hygiene and comfort then?

Summerlin PICU was negligent, failed to communicate, and instead of mediation or accountability, chose retaliation and CPS. I am still shaken and disturbed.

review: I went to TMC with an emergency kidney infection. I was there for eight days due to kidney damage and treatment, including surgery. I found the nurses and technicians to be hard-working, empathetic, and very helpful. I was assigned excellent doctors, as my urologist had recently closed her practice, and I was without a permanent position. Everything was thoroughly explained to me, and all procedures performed were explained and followed up clearly. Plans for discharge were clear and helpful. The food was quite edible, and there were many options. I had an excellent experience under the circumstances and will return to TMC, if I need arises in the future, without hesitation.

I was taken to TMC in an ambulance needing orthopedic surgery. The attending physician asked me if I had an orthopedic surgeon? They then called in Danielo Sotello who just got off probation for amputating the wrong leg of a Marine, 2 days off probation and they consider this guy their top guy. Regardless, I have never had a bad experience in this hospital after dealing with doctor Taczander I will never come back to this hospital again, and I will definitely voice my experience to others about this doctor. label: Resident

name: Paul
review:
Do yourself or your family member a huge favor and go elsewhere!

I was supposed to have surgery there in 3 days. The staff was so stupid that they couldn't figure out how to reach me on my cell phone (pretty sure they had the wrong number). So instead they blew up my emergency contact (my ex-wife) while she was at work. When she finally answered, they told her to tell me to call and that it was urgent. They didn't leave any information...department, extension, name, anything. I called the hospital and shuffled around a few places before finding the right person, a nurse in the surgical department. First thing she tried to do was transfer from the phone she was on to the phone in her office. It didn't work. She tried again. Again, she couldn't make it work, so we stayed on that phone. They wanted to go over my pre-surgery instructions. I couldn't take the time at that moment and told them to call back. She said they'd call tomorrow morning. I said that was fine, but that if they couldn't reach me NOT to call my emergency contact for something that clearly is NOT an emergency. This nurse started arguing with me, saying that if they're not supposed to call her then I need to remove her as my contact. I said I was not going to remove her because she is my emergency contact. I tried to explain that not being able to give pre-surgery instructions does not constitute an emergency, not even close. It's inconvenience, and they're not to hassle my contacts over inconveniences. She started raising her voice saying she'll contact her if she thinks she needs to and that she wasn't going to argue over what's an emergency. I told her to cancel my surgery.

My father died of staph infection after a basic surgery due to incompetent hospital staff. I'm not about to put myself in the same position with staff so incompetent that modern phones are a challenge. I definitely won't trust a hospital that won't respect the simple request of, "Don't contact my emergency contact unless it's an emergency."
label: Resident

name: Laura
review: Less stars would be more appropriate. My mom was sent there by the 24 hour nurse line. She needed to have a catheter replaced or new one depending on what was found by Dr. We arrived & checked in by 6:30 (right before shift change) so there was a bit of a wait. No problem. They put her in a room, drew blood, took vitals etc. The nursing staff was great. When it was time to take her to IR, the tech came & got her, never introducing herself. I asked where she was going & expressed that I would like to go with her. She replied, well there isn't much of a waiting room but whatever. So, I went with her anyway. On the way down, she was bumping her bed into walls, chairs etc. So, I grabbed into the bed after we got off the elevator & tried to help. She snapped "I've got this". I said you have bumped her into every possible surface that was available. I was just trying to help. She left us downstairs & the nurse came out to prepare her for what all the possible risks. The wheeled her back & I met her back in her room after she was done. She informed me that they couldn't use the existing access site & had to create a new one. She was awake the whole time & informed me that the Dr's that were doing the procedure were bad mouthing UNM Dr's & they didn't like the way UNM put the original catheter in, stating that they don't know how to do their jobs & complained during the entire procedure. Such an inappropriate place to have this conversation. So unprofessional. When we finally got discharged & headed home. We were almost home when I turned to my mom & asked if she spilled something on her jacket. She said no, I looked a little closer & it was blood, a lot of blood. We happened to be close to Lovelace Westside & I took her to the emergency room there. As I'm checking her in, she is soaked from the shoulder to her waste & dripping blood all over the floor. The lady at registration collected her information & I asked if we could get someone right away to triage her because she is dripping blood everywhere. She said Oh she's bleeding? I said, clearly (maybe with a little sarcasm)! She was finally wheeled back to get triaged. The nurse made sure she was comfortable & provided gauze & a towel to sop up the blood. Another nurse came in & helped her get into a gown & cleaned her up again & place towel & gauze all around her. They never took her blood to see where her numbers were at & considering how much blood she lost, that is concerning. The Dr came in the redress her catheter & assess the situation to see if the new access cath was bleeding or the stitched-up area. The Dr asked the nurse for more gauze & towels. They didnt have any, so had to order some from another floor. (this is the ER) Why aren't they prepared for this? They were able to get her somewhat cleaned up again & then patched up. They informed us that there may be a little bleeding but if it soaks through like it did before, to come back to the ER. Shannon came in to discharge us & she was awesome & let us know that we can call her direct & that she would be on ICU unit if we needed her. When we got home, it did in fact start bleeding quite a bit & I was not about to bring her back to Lovelace. I took her to UNM SRMC, problem solved. They assessed the situation & cleaned her up well, took blood to check platelets & hemoglobin (both appropriate for this situation & should have been done at Lovelace Westside). Applied medication & anticoagulant, did some imaging to be sure there was nothing that was missed. Needless to say, I am disgusted at how my moms care was handled at both Lovelace location. I didn't have a choice but to add at least one star. :/

review:
Update: they did accepted our dispute and zeroed our balance. I appreciate corporate doing the right thing.

I loved this hospital for a time but if I knew then what I know now, I would not recommend this place to anyone. Our minor child came to this emergency room so severely dehydrated that he needed IV fluids. The doctor on duty that night sat bragging about how much experience he had in pediatrics while telling me that he wasn't worried about my child. He said that if he didn't look worried, I shouldn't be worried.

Thank God nurse Natalie advocated from my child when the doctor was trying to send me home. When the labs came back and they finally put him on IV fluids they did a test for a bacterial infection despite my son already having been on an antibiotic. It has been made very clear to us by multiple pediatricians that being on an antibiotic will cause a false negative when a child has this infection. Our pediatric office also said that  an ER doctor should know this, especially one bragging about his experience in pediatrics.

Despite the fact that this doctor should have known not to run this test when my son was already on antibiotics, he then told us that we needed to pack bags because we were going to the hospital. So he went from telling us he's not worried and we shouldn't be either to telling us we couldn't go home and calling an ambulance... talk about whip lash.

The following week became an absolute hell of trying to convince doctors that our son did in fact have the infection we said he had. Because this doctor said that this test was negative and it was false negative due to the antibiotics our son was on... Other doctors believed him rather than me. The hospital that we were transferred to, in the end, using data from our labs and recordings that I made while in the hospital, sent us an apology letter for what happened to us and told us to disregard our bill.

Without getting into all of the details, this hospital has not done the same. In fact, they put us through a complaint process with patient advocates that often do not call us back, they have continued to send us letters in the mail telling us that we owe them money for their mistake, and they have not apologized for any of the physical and emotional toll that their improper diagnosis caused to me and my son.

review:
Do yourself or your family member a huge favor and go elsewhere!

I was supposed to have surgery there in 3 days. The staff was so stupid that they couldn't figure out how to reach me on my cell phone (pretty sure they had the wrong number). So instead they blew up my emergency contact (my ex-wife) while she was at work. When she finally answered, they told her to tell me to call and that it was urgent. They didn't leave any information...department, extension, name, anything. I called the hospital and shuffled around a few places before finding the right person, a nurse in the surgical department. First thing she tried to do was transfer from the phone she was on to the phone in her office. It didn't work. She tried again. Again, she couldn't make it work, so we stayed on that phone. They wanted to go over my pre-surgery instructions. I couldn't take the time at that moment and told them to call back. She said they'd call tomorrow morning. I said that was fine, but that if they couldn't reach me NOT to call my emergency contact for something that clearly is NOT an emergency. This nurse started arguing with me, saying that if they're not supposed to call her then I need to remove her as my contact. I said I was not going to remove her because she is my emergency contact. I tried to explain that not being able to give pre-surgery instructions does not constitute an emergency, not even close. It's inconvenience, and they're not to hassle my contacts over inconveniences. She started raising her voice saying she'll contact her if she thinks she needs to and that she wasn't going to argue over what's an emergency. I told her to cancel my surgery.

My father died of staph infection after a basic surgery due to incompetent hospital staff. I'm not about to put myself in the same position with staff so incompetent that modern phones are a challenge. I definitely won't trust a hospital that won't respect the simple request of, "Don't contact my emergency contact unless it's an emergency."

She said this is not the first she¡¯s heard of this and since starting surgeries with PSL and explained that they had written a script and it was at a Walgreens, we asked why it would go there and that¡¯s when we learned PSL had a Walgreens inside of it. I did get pain meds from my care team eventually that day and have been recovering well. Thankful to have follow ups with my care team and not going back to PSL. Decent place for surgery but you have to be your own advocate even right after surgery and be lucid enough to know what to ask for.

review:
Update: they did accepted our dispute and zeroed our balance. I appreciate corporate doing the right thing.

I loved this hospital for a time but if I knew then what I know now, I would not recommend this place to anyone. Our minor child came to this emergency room so severely dehydrated that he needed IV fluids. The doctor on duty that night sat bragging about how much experience he had in pediatrics while telling me that he wasn't worried about my child. He said that if he didn't look worried, I shouldn't be worried.

Thank God nurse Natalie advocated from my child when the doctor was trying to send me home. When the labs came back and they finally put him on IV fluids they did a test for a bacterial infection despite my son already having been on an antibiotic. It has been made very clear to us by multiple pediatricians that being on an antibiotic will cause a false negative when a child has this infection. Our pediatric office also said that  an ER doctor should know this, especially one bragging about his experience in pediatrics.

Despite the fact that this doctor should have known not to run this test when my son was already on antibiotics, he then told us that we needed to pack bags because we were going to the hospital. So he went from telling us he's not worried and we shouldn't be either to telling us we couldn't go home and calling an ambulance... talk about whip lash.

The following week became an absolute hell of trying to convince doctors that our son did in fact have the infection we said he had. Because this doctor said that this test was negative and it was false negative due to the antibiotics our son was on... Other doctors believed him rather than me. The hospital that we were transferred to, in the end, using data from our labs and recordings that I made while in the hospital, sent us an apology letter for what happened to us and told us to disregard our bill.

Without getting into all of the details, this hospital has not done the same. In fact, they put us through a complaint process with patient advocates that often do not call us back, they have continued to send us letters in the mail telling us that we owe them money for their mistake, and they have not apologized for any of the physical and emotional toll that their improper diagnosis caused to me and my son.

It was interesting to be part of the first surgeries with the partnership change. PSL still uses paper charting and some EMR. The check in process for surgery is very streamline but the QR code would not load in the app day of the surgery and the check in manager sitting at the desk seemed annoyed that I needed help with checking in for surgery. The other lady that did check in was very helpful.
A few of the staff while preping for surgery expressed their thoughts on the change in a passive way not to me directly but overall about the hospital and how it¡¯s handled. Post op was very rushed. I didn¡¯t see my care team afterwords but they spoke to my husband before he came to my room. I was very uncomfortable post op, I was given a muscle relaxer and that kind of helped. We were more rushed out of the post op room than not. I told them my pain was getting worse and they said I¡¯ll feel better at home. When we got home we realized they never went over post op pain meds, that no one went over anything pain medication wise. My husband tried to call the hospital but there was not a phone number for this support. We called my oncology team  and they were able to prescribe pain meds for my husband to pick up. The nurse on my care team asked what discharge we had for pain meds and my husband explained what post op was like and how no one gave us meds. She said this is not the first she¡¯s heard of this and since starting surgeries with PSL and explained that they had written a script and it was at a Walgreens, we asked why it would go there and that¡¯s when we learned PSL had a Walgreens inside of it. I did get pain meds from my care team eventually that day and have been recovering well. Thankful to have follow ups with my care team and not going back to PSL. Decent place for surgery but you have to be your own advocate even right after surgery and be lucid enough to know what to ask for.
I¡¯m surprised the hospital didn¡¯t send out a hospital based survey, and only this Google review ask. But also not surprised with the over all experiance we had.

review:
This may have been one of the worst experiences I¡¯ve ever had at a hospital.

I went in recently for severe shoulder and neck pain. I was in very severe 10/10 pain, which I had never in my life experienced. The physician who saw me didn¡¯t perform any exam of my neck or shoulder - she didn¡¯t even touch me once. No imaging was ordered; instead she said she would give me a ¡°muscle relaxer¡±. It was Valium, and anti-anxiety medication. She thought my pain was just me being a hysterical woman! I was appalled, especially to receive this treatment from another woman.

Thankfully, I was able to get some assistance from the military health system soon after. Imaging revealed that the pain was due to a rotator cuff tear and two severely slipped discs in my neck.

The fact that I was actually severely injured and was treated for anxiety in this ER boggles my mind. I¡¯m usually willing to forgive people who are just having a rough day at work but honestly? When I finally woke up, I remember them asking her multiple times if she had eaten, if she needed anything.  They made sure she was comfortable which meant the world to me.

When it was time to go, my nurse went over the discharge paperwork completely making sure we had no questions.  She showed us how to change the catheter bags and how to drain them properly.  She didn't leave until we were 100% comfortable with what was ahead for the next week.  They even called me after my discharge to check on me.

I have recommended this facility to friends and people I meet.  If given a choice, this is the place I will come to.  I would give them 10 stars if I could.

.
I do not think that either my insurances (Cigna (thru my employer) or Humana (thru my Medicare)  OR myself should have to pay for this extra time at the hospital.
.
Other than the snafu, everyone was great! I even got a hug from Doc Strutton !

name: Lauren Meckenstock
review:
Overall was good, I didnt die and had no complications with my radical hysterectomy. But the communication and bedside experience lacked.
It was interesting to be part of the first surgeries with the partnership change. PSL still uses paper charting and some EMR. The check in process for surgery is very streamline but the QR code would not load in the app day of the surgery and the check in manager sitting at the desk seemed annoyed that I needed help with checking in for surgery. The other lady that did check in was very helpful.
A few of the staff while preping for surgery expressed their thoughts on the change in a passive way not to me directly but overall about the hospital and how it¡¯s handled. Post op was very rushed. I didn¡¯t see my care team afterwords but they spoke to my husband before he came to my room. I was very uncomfortable post op, I was given a muscle relaxer and that kind of helped. We were more rushed out of the post op room than not. I told them my pain was getting worse and they said I¡¯ll feel better at home. When we got home we realized they never went over post op pain meds, that no one went over anything pain medication wise. My husband tried to call the hospital but there was not a phone number for this support. We called my oncology team  and they were able to prescribe pain meds for my husband to pick up. The nurse on my care team asked what discharge we had for pain meds and my husband explained what post op was like and how no one gave us meds. She said this is not the first she¡¯s heard of this and since starting surgeries with PSL and explained that they had written a script and it was at a Walgreens, we asked why it would go there and that¡¯s when we learned PSL had a Walgreens inside of it. I did get pain meds from my care team eventually that day and have been recovering well. Thankful to have follow ups with my care team and not going back to PSL. Decent place for surgery but you have to be your own advocate even right after surgery and be lucid enough to know what to ask for.
I¡¯m surprised the hospital didn¡¯t send out a hospital based survey, and only this Google review ask. But also not surprised with the over all experiance we had.

name: Nancy Welch
review:
The communication process between Kaiser and PSL is really lacking right now. The  surgery scheduler gabby from kaiser did not tell PSL that I needed to have a radiation/ nuclear test before I had a melanoma removed. it delayed my day to a 12 hour day most of it waiting.
I can be compassionate and understand
that the system of communication between the surgery scheduler in Saint Joe¡¯s might have been different than the communication with PSL. It truly messed up my day and the person who drove me. I should¡¯ve been home by 2 or 3 and I was home by 9 o¡¯clock pm.
I do understand it¡¯s a new communication process as of August 31. I believe this situation can be handled and corrected.
The preop admin woman that called me to tell me how to get into HC one hung up on me after 20 minutes of trying to help I had Way to get in To communicate with the surgery department to find out where I was supposed to go what I was supposed to do hanging up on me was incredibly rude. name: Whitney Freno
review:
Update: they did accepted our dispute and zeroed our balance. I appreciate corporate doing the right thing.

I loved this hospital for a time but if I knew then what I know now, I would not recommend this place to anyone. Our minor child came to this emergency room so severely dehydrated that he needed IV fluids. The doctor on duty that night sat bragging about how much experience he had in pediatrics while telling me that he wasn't worried about my child. He said that if he didn't look worried, I shouldn't be worried.

Thank God nurse Natalie advocated from my child when the doctor was trying to send me home. When the labs came back and they finally put him on IV fluids they did a test for a bacterial infection despite my son already having been on an antibiotic. It has been made very clear to us by multiple pediatricians that being on an antibiotic will cause a false negative when a child has this infection. Our pediatric office also said that  an ER doctor should know this, especially one bragging about his experience in pediatrics.

Despite the fact that this doctor should have known not to run this test when my son was already on antibiotics, he then told us that we needed to pack bags because we were going to the hospital. So he went from telling us he's not worried and we shouldn't be either to telling us we couldn't go home and calling an ambulance... talk about whip lash.

The following week became an absolute hell of trying to convince doctors that our son did in fact have the infection we said he had. Because this doctor said that this test was negative and it was false negative due to the antibiotics our son was on... Other doctors believed him rather than me. The hospital that we were transferred to, in the end, using data from our labs and recordings that I made while in the hospital, sent us an apology letter for what happened to us and told us to disregard our bill.

Without getting into all of the details, this hospital has not done the same. In fact, they put us through a complaint process with patient advocates that often do not call us back, they have continued to send us letters in the mail telling us that we owe them money for their mistake, and they have not apologized for any of the physical and emotional toll that their improper diagnosis caused to me and my son.
label: Resident

name: Karen Pitts
review: I will never go anywhere else for emergent care. ABQ ER west was fantastic! I walked in and was seen immediately. I received excellent care from all staff I encountered. My visit was seamless and I was in and out under 2 hours even with imaging!

review: I was at Grandview for a hip replacement October 2025.  From preop staff,the OR, post op recovery and on the orthopedic floor where I stayed over night; the nurses were attentive, professional, knowledgeable, and caring.  I am an RN and a retired family nurse practitioner.   I know what to look for in the staff. I have had the misfortune to be a patient at other hospitals in town.  Grandview is head and shoulders over the others. If you have a choice,  go to Grandview.  You will get excellent care.

name: Jessica Filichia
review: I went to Grandview hospital recently as directed by my medical professionals to rule out serotonin toxicity and hypertension. The nurses and nurse practitioner were all very friendly and did a great job telling me that "Everything was NORMAL." After giving me a liter of IV fluids. They never told me what the serotonin level was and said I should limit my salt intake. A bag of normal saline has 9 gm of sodium in it. She suggested I had numbness and tingling in my hands which I didn't have before... But now I do. To top it off I would have to eat 4-11 ramen noodle soups to equal the amount of salt they just gave me in the one bag of fluid. I just couldn't help after the fact  to laugh about her advice to lower my salt intake after giving me 3.9 times the daily recommended dose in 30 min. This is why I am grateful not to be in the nursing profession anymore. I hung hundreds of bags of saline and never put two and two together until I was on the other side. P.S. I despise ramen noodles with a passion and my chest pain has since gotten worse. I didn't have high blood pressure because I'm old and my kidneys are failing. It was because of a med error by Gateway to Success prescribed me max doses of Cymbalta and Straterra which can cause two life threatening conditions serotonin syndrome and QT prolongation. I would have known sooner if given the opportunity to use the Internet or a phone sooner than just one week ago.

name: Paul
review:
Do yourself or your family member a huge favor and go elsewhere!

I was supposed to have surgery there in 3 days. The staff was so stupid that they couldn't figure out how to reach me on my cell phone (pretty sure they had the wrong number). So instead they blew up my emergency contact (my ex-wife) while she was at work. When she finally answered, they told her to tell me to call and that it was urgent. They didn't leave any information...department, extension, name, anything. I called the hospital and shuffled around a few places before finding the right person, a nurse in the surgical department. First thing she tried to do was transfer from the phone she was on to the phone in her office. It didn't work. She tried again. Again, she couldn't make it work, so we stayed on that phone. They wanted to go over my pre-surgery instructions. I couldn't take the time at that moment and told them to call back. She said they'd call tomorrow morning. I said that was fine, but that if they couldn't reach me NOT to call my emergency contact for something that clearly is NOT an emergency. This nurse started arguing with me, saying that if they're not supposed to call her then I need to remove her as my contact. I said I was not going to remove her because she is my emergency contact. Post-Op & Discharge: After a 3-hour wait (expected for emergency cases), RN Jin Young Lee handled our discharge with exceptional care. She reviewed all instructions clearly, ensured we understood follow-up care, and was genuinely kind.

Hospital Environment: Yes, the building is older, but it¡¯s well-kept, clean, and organized. Even the restrooms were neat¡ªsomething I don¡¯t take for granted in busy hospitals!

I¡¯m truly saddened by the negative reviews because our experience was flawless in a high-stress situation. If you need emergency care (especially pediatric) or a skilled orthopedic specialist, I highly recommend NUMC¡ªand ask for Dr. Omar Saleem if you need bone-related care.

name: Seokjin Ham
review: The gardens were created in 1807 by order of Napoleon Bonaparte and designed by Gianantonio Selva as a public green space for the citizens of Venice. Today, they serve both as a peaceful retreat and as a major cultural venue. Since 1895, the gardens have hosted the Venice Biennale, beginning with its first International Art Exhibition. During the Biennale, approximately 65% of the gardens are dedicated to exhibition space, while the remaining 35% remains open as public parkland. Starting in 1907, individual nations began constructing their own pavilions within the gardens. Today, there are 29 national pavilions, making the gardens one of the central hubs for contemporary art during the Biennale.

name: Manu - Emanuele Gentili
review:
The park in Italy was simple but beautiful, with wide stretches of bright green grass that looked almost painted under the sunlight. It was the kind of place where everything felt calm and open. The grass was soft and freshly cut, carrying that clean, earthy smell that comes after a morning watering. People sat on blankets or benches, talking quietly or just watching the day go by.
Children ran across the open field, their shoes kicking up tiny bits of grass as they played, and every so often a light breeze would move through, making the leaves in the nearby trees rustle softly. There were a few old stone paths that curved through the park, leading to shaded spots where the grass grew a little taller and wilder.
From one end, you could see the red roofs of nearby houses and hear the faint sounds of a caf¨¦ ¡ª clinking cups, laughter, and the hiss of an espresso machine. It wasn¡¯t a large park, but it had a peaceful feeling, like it belonged to the people who used it every day. The green grass stretched out like a soft carpet, inviting anyone to sit, rest, or just enjoy the view. It was simple, sunny, and perfectly Italian in its quiet charm

name: Asiyah Noemi Koso
review: During the walk from St. Mark's Square to the Academy Bridge, we pass this beautiful square. This elongated square is one of the most elegant in Venice. Campo Santo Stefano or Campo Morosini is one of the largest fields in Venice. It is surrounded by beautiful churches and palaces such as the church of Santo Stefano, the church of San Vidal, Palazzo Morosini, Palazzo Loredan and Palazzo Pisani and the adjacent Campiello Pisani. It is a favorite gathering place of locals and guests, sometimes concerts, Christmas and other fairs are held on this square. And it is certainly very interesting to walk here during the Carnival. In the center of the field stands the statue of the writer Niccol¨° Tommaseo, the work of Francesco Barzaghi from 1882. The pleasant atmosphere is complemented by coffee bars, patisseries and restaurants such as "Ristorante Pizzeria Santo Stefano", Ristorante "Le Caf¨¦", Ristorante " A Beccafico ", Ristorante / Bar" Art Blue Caf¨¦ ", Osteria" Ethnos ", Osteria" Doge Morosini ", Gelateria" Paolin "and on the corner and opposite the church the popular Bar" all'Angolo ". Right behind the ¡°Art Blue Cafe¡± and Palazzo Morosini Gatterburg is the far-famed Venetian Music Conservatory of Music Benedetto Marcello. Every time we pass there we hear good music spreading towards the square from the building. It is interesting and good to know that the Church of San Vidal is a place where classical music concerts of the famous Rondo Veneziano are held. The pride of the church is its very fine Gothic portal, created in 1442 by Bartolomeo Bon. The interior is decorated with ingenious works by Tintoretto.

The chocolate ice cream I picked up from Conad City was a delightful treat. It had a rich and satisfying flavor that instantly won me over. It¡¯s clear that Conad City prioritizes quality, as evidenced by the freshness of their products.

Moreover, the staff at Conad City were exceptionally friendly and helpful. They were knowledgeable about their inventory and eager to assist with any inquiries. Their positive attitude added to the overall pleasant ambiance of the store.

Conad City is now my go-to grocery store. Whether you¡¯re looking for everyday essentials or special treats like chocolate ice cream, Conad City is sure to meet your needs with top-notch quality and service. I highly recommend paying them a visit!

We did not overstay our welcome nor the stores official time but very unpleasant to grab a few essential items needed for the morning. I would only use this store as a last resort for items otherwise bring your money elsewhere.

If you just want to go in, find what you want, pay and leave, avoid like the plague. This is a local shop for local people. As guests in the city we try to respect the residents, but after two visits to this store, never again.

review: We used this market to get our basics but stick up when you find water and milk because other days they run out of different groceries. Some days they have bread and other days they are sold out. It¡¯s an easy walk from the Rialto bridge if you are staying at a local BnB.
label: Resident

name: Larry Westcott
review:
We stayed write around the corner from this CO-OP and we actually went to the other CO-OP jut because of the reviews.  One day we decided to stop in since it was so close and I have to say ...
The staff is very friendly.  Have been in there about three times and the gentleman cashier says hello to me and Merry Christmas and asks about things.  We even sang We Are the World together...LOL.
He has a sense of humor and very helpful...
As for people having a problem because they have to weigh or measure the breads or the fruits and vegetables...Just learn how it is done here...It is not a big issue.
As for the self checkouts they are available in English and Italian and I have not had an issue.  It does take cash.
The store is small but it is close and in a central area..great for quick stuff...this is not a huge grocery store or a Walmart.
Great to grab water for 25P for 1.5 liters when every restaurant was offering for 2.50...
The walkways are tight but space is limited in Venice so just accept it and smile as you work around each other.
Remember smiles are free...

name: Catherine Amadeus
review:
I was so excited to come here when I was in Venice. Everything is gluten free, obviously, and the owner very clearly cares about what products he carries. He even gave us a list of gluten free restaurants he recommends based on his own experiences. Compared to other major cities in Italy, there were not a lot of good gluten free options in Venice that I found, so having this was great.
I read some negative reviews saying that the place is not a supermarket as it's small. I think it's obvious that it would be small because it's only things that would otherwise contain gluten in a regular supermarket. I wish more people would open shops like this with many different brands of gluten free items to try. I am tired of the same dry and tasteless options carried at major grocery stores.

review:
My wife and I stopped by earlier this week before heading home to New York and wanted to share how taken we were with your shop, the people working there and the great selection of local and artisanal products.
Tonight, we will drink the Maramao Pinot Grigio we brought home with us and make a wish to return ¡ª not just to the store, but also your beautiful city. (Hopefully, the olive oil will last for a few weeks!)

Thanks also for steering us to VENETIKA ¡ª where we had two unforgettable dinners.

Warmest regards,
Ray & Deborah / New York City

review: Great supermarket if like me you are staying self catering and want to save money it's easy to get to we used the tram going and the bus coming back.  If you are staying in Venice (the island) and have a vaporetto ticket you can use it on both the tram and bus in the Mestre district.  There is a validation machine on both which is the same as the vaporetto one.

name: Babu Nawshad
review: Alright this is my second review I believe after 4 years where you got a 5 star review from me before. today I had the bitter experience in the same old local. From the beginning of my living  in the city I have been a customer of this local. I never ever got any kind of bad behaviour from any staff or the securities since then. But today, after coming from my office ( tired) , I just checked in to this local to grab two bottles of water. grabbing the bottles of water and I saw the price was 17 cents (which was a price privilege for the customers who had the Palm panorama card). in a hurry I forgot to punch the card on the scanner before scanning my water bottles. And the security guard on duty ( 09/11/24 at 14:30 more or less) Came behind me and told me that, " if you don't know how to use the self payment machines , why didn't you go to the sales woman?? " I replied to him "  I know, it was a mistake that I didn't punch my card before, and because the price is showing 57 cents for a bottle that's why I'm going to another machine".. he kept on saying that " you first better learn to speak Italian!!!!"  But excuse me!!! is that the way that a service man should treat a customer??????? I really felt offended, rather insulted by the words of this man . I also frequent the Pam under the Ca'mestre . and their services are awesome!! especially the security guards, always smiling and eager to help you anytime. even out suggest this man should go and search for himself to see how the security guards behave with the customers in interspar, Coop or Simply... I've been living in the zone for 20 Long years. never had this bad experience before. I hope the direction gives some guidance to this gentleman and teaches him how to behave with customers. no matter which race and country  they come from. I am really feeling bad. my weekend was jeopardised by this gentleman's behaviour. sorry to say that. I hope your customer care gets better in this local especially. best of luck.
label: Resident

name: Andrea
review: Personale cortese. Piccolo supermercato sull Isola. Spesso mi fermo in barca a far la spesa. Peccato per la mancanza di ormeggi! Ogni volta bisogna far le corse sperando di non trovare danni.

review: Indispensabili per l'isola
label: Resident

review: Wonderful local wine shop. Sommeliers was excellent. And she has great bottles as well as bulk local wine. She filled us a water bottle of the best local wine for 3 euros!!!
label: Resident

name: Ben Aulim
review: Small grocery. We paid 6 for a sixpack of water (6x 0,5l) which on average shouldn't cost more than 2 even in Venice. Takes advantage of not having to compete with anyone in Sant'Elena since the closest supermarkets are in Castello.  Won't see us again.
label: Resident

name: David Christopher
review: Superb local fruit and veg shop.  Great quality, good prices and lovely people too.
label: Resident

name: Michelle Lin
review: Lovely selection of bread and cheese. I always get their sourdough it¡¯s oven baked and whole wheat. Makes a perfect avocado toast. The man working at the store is really nice and generous when you ask to try the cheese. Would highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: nicoline flarup svenningsen
review: This is the best shop for specialities, cheese and wine. Having been in Venice for 4 months, no other place is quite as nice. You will experience the best service and care here. They have so much knowledge about the local traditions and the best combinations of crudo, cheese and wine. I cannot recommend this place enough.
label: Resident

name: Sailor Pear
review: Marvellous homemade pastries - fresh and truly delicious. Owner was so lovely, helpful and nice to talk to. Prices were so reasonable too! I would come here every day if I lived here.
label: Resident

name: Annie
review: Excellent local bakery.  Small shop off the beaten path with excellent, hand made baked items.  Croissants, creme (actual custard) filled pastries, cookies, tarts, etc¡­  Looked to be a mother and daughter ownership. The daughter spoke excellent English and was very friendly and helpful.
label: Resident

name: Callum rj
review: Ordered a an apricot and chocolate croissant to takeaway. They were very fresh and delicious, seems like a good spot for the locals to go to. The lady was very welcoming and friendly. Very good value and a good selection of sweet and savoury bakery items. Perfect
label: Resident

name: Bruno Venga
review: What a wonderful surprise in Venice! A small emporium that sells wines from small local producers, straight from the barrel, as well as gluten-free cookies, bulk essential oils, and natural cosmetics. Excellent prices¡ª1 liter of freshly bottled wine for 2.70.
label: Resident

name: Berlin Gal
review: Super cute shop focusing on sustainable, wholesome products by small-batch makers or local designers. Brilliant place for handmade soaps, whole-wheat pasta or else without packaging. Amazing gift ideas for friends who love Venice!
label: Resident

name: Victoria G
review: More than groceries - fantastic collection of handiwork by local artisans

review: They won¡¯t let you choose your own fruit (ok all Italy is like that) but then they should have a responsibility to give you edible fruit. We picked up two apples and two peaches on our way to the beach and didn¡¯t check them until we got there: one apple was rotten, the other had visible worms and the two peaches were so hard and green they were good enough only for pellets. Very disappointing. CHECK WHAT THEY GIVE YOU BEFORE YOU LEAVE THE SHOP!

review:
Food was good and the price is very affordable as well. The shrimp one was flavourful, tomatoey, fresh. The cuttlefish pasta was amazing as well, although I would appreciate slightly more flavour with the sauce, the cuttlefish itself was so so fresh, cooked to perfection and generous portion as well.

The only thing is that the bread is a bit stale and tough at the same time haha, out of the 2 breads they have us one was really really chewy, while the other was hard like a rock. Service is also slightly rushed and forgetful, had to asked 3 times to get the olive oil and salt.

Other than that definitely recommend this place because it is cheap, good, and quick!
label: Resident

name: Ben Morris
review: Best spaghetti with seafood I've ever had. The staff couldn't do enough for us. Very accommodating. Did us a calamari starter to share, prepared a plain spaghetti dish for my 6 year old girl, with some oil and Parmesan, which she loved. The portions are amazing and the quality of the food is excellent. Been in Italy just over a week. Eat in Peschiera, Verona and now here, this was our best meal out.
label: Resident

name: Mark Wojciechowski
review: Hosted us without a reservation despite the busiest of afternoons due to the proximity to the marathon finish line.  Great staff and even better food.  The very best meal we had in Venice over the past several days. Good value too.
label: Resident

name: Jasper Vercammen
review: One of the best pizza¡¯s we ever had. And finally a decent limoncello spritz, not the chemical fluorescent stuff we were server regularly in other places.
label: Resident

name: Alice Stou
review: A cozy restaurant with local vibes and food that felt homemade in the best way! Had the scallops and two pasta dishes - the pasta with clams and tomatoes was incredible! Great portion sizes, and a Venezian menu that felt very different and refreshing.
label: Resident

name: Karen Saez
review: We had a great evening! Very good service in a super cozy and atmospheric setting. The pasta with onions was extremely tasty. I also loved my vegan pizza (veggie pizza with no cheese) - the olive oil and fresh basil were delicious. Recommend!
label: Resident

name: Ella Fucanan
review: Great food to welcome us upon arriving! We ordered seafood - Catch of the Day and Seafood Risotto. Everything was perfect, seafood was fresh, and lovingly prepared and served by the staff. Pictures don¡¯t give justice, the fish was already deboned by this time. They bring it straight to your table and we stood in awe of the mastery of removing the bones live.
label: Resident

name: Gulcan Candogan
review: This is the best place in Venice! The staff is incredibly friendly, and the food is absolutely delicious¡ªfrom the starters to the main courses and desserts. The grilled local calamari and the pistachio dessert were especially outstanding. I would recommend this place to anyone looking to truly enjoy their meal 10/10
label: Resident

name: William Fathers
review: Was walking by and happened to stumble upon a gem of a restaurant. Was greeted at the door and service was impeccable. Salam was an amazing waiter. Food was top tier, pasta was fresh and shrimp was cooked perfectly. Would recommend if you are in the area.
label: Resident

name: Aron Yuen
review: Coming here for dinner the second time in a row during my short stay in Venice.  The seafood pasta was just phenomenal! The service was excellent!  I can¡¯t recommend this trattoria more!
label: Resident

name: Calvin
review: Great crowd and food was delish. Tried the seafood spaghetti, caprese and beef. All were well done and flavourful. The queue might be a little long but definitely worth it!
label: Resident

name: Alina Mitskevich
review: The wait was over for 2 hours: waiting for the table, waiting for drinks, waiting for food, waiting for the bill. The wine was only brought after we reminded them. I understand that the staff are busy, but maybe they shouldn't take extra reservations then? After such a long wait, the food seemed quite good, fairly averag§Ö. If you still want to visit, book in advance.
label: Resident

name: Dongchan Lee
review:
Highest food quality at very low price.
Friendly staffs and they know what to do.
You will not regret it
label: Resident

name: Flora Fan
review: This place is warm, clean and well-decorated family style. The service was great and the staff are so nice and friendly. The food was amazing! We ordered seafood pasta and cuttlefish ink pasta. The americano caffe was good too. The tiramisu is the best I¡¯ve ever had in Italy so far.
label: Resident

name: neha sharma
review: "Amazing atmosphere, delicious food, sitting arrangments also very nice with beautiful view ??waitress was friendly, attentive, and made sure we had everything we needed. Excellent service!" I highly recommend this restaurant with great vibe
label: Resident

name: Annahans
review:
We picked Trattoria DiGiorgio from many options while looking for a restaurant with a cozy vibe. Tables placed outside, right by the water, with the heat lamps, so it was possible to sit without a jacket.
The staff were friendly, and what¡¯s most important, food was delicious. We definitely recommend this restaurant
label: Resident

name: Matthias Miesbauer
review:
We (two) were here to have a late dinner from 9PM to about 10:30PM.

Everyone was really friendly and the food was excellent.

The ambiente is perfect for a Venice holiday.

Would absolutly recommand.
label: Resident

name: Luca K¨¦sm¨¢rki
review: The atmosphere was generally good. The food was delicious, and the service was friendly. The also offer gluten-free pizza! The daily fish dish was quite expensive, so it's a good idea to ask about the price beforehand. Otherwise, I recommend the place!
label: Resident

name: George
review:
Terrible diarrhea after having a pizza here.

Had a Hawaiian pizza and my friend had a shrimp pizza. Worst pizza both of us have ever had and then we were violently ill from it the rest of the day.

My Hawaiian pizza looked like it had canned pineapple and stale ham on it, the shrimp on the shrimp pizza were obviously from a can too.

Plus it wasn¡¯t even cheap, seriously avoid this place.

Service sucked too, basically ignored the whole time despite being the only people sitting outside.
label: Resident

name: Stacy Miller
review: We randomly came across this amazing restaurant when we purposely got lost in Venice (each person picked a direction to turn at each junction). That accommodated our large party and created a table for us on their small patio in the alley. The food was amazing! Special of the day was fantastic, would highly recommend the spicy shrimp pasta dish, and the dessert special was a sliced cookie dish that is something we will dream about in the future! Service was excellent and the setting was relaxed. Lots of locals showed up as the evening went on. We would return in a minute!
label: Resident

name: Zsolt Szelecki
review: Remarkable food, but what made the place stand out for us is the service. The guys who run the restaurant, were not only friendly, relaxed, but offered some great recommendations to local food and I sights on culture. We leant what Cankara is in local slang, among other things and insights. Keep on , this was a memorable experience, with great food! We'll come back when we can.
label: Resident

name: John
review: A unicorn in a city full of hidden fees, this restaurant does not charge any cover charge (coperto) or automatic gratuity (servicio), but they earn your patronage with excellent service and fresh food. They understand that treating customers with respect encourages repeat visits, and the number of local clients shows that.  One of the best pesto sauces I've ever had was here. The chicken Milanese and sauteed spinach were also excellent.
label: Resident

name: Sasha B
review:
Service is best in Venice, waiter was extremely helpful, kind and knowledgeable.
Food was amazing: light, fresh and sea food cooked to perfection. One thing to point out is that octopus could be polarising, it is definitely pushing taste buds: bitter and sweet, fresh and smoky, crunchy and silky all at once. As for me it was just the right amount of food adventurousness ??????
label: Resident

name: Grant Jackson
review: Had the lasagne, pretty average. Wine overpriced, my partner had penne with rocket and shrimps, nicely cooked but far too much garlic. Good atmosphere but far better places around
label: Resident

review:
very disappointed with foods!
very hard bread to start( feels like it was sitting there for a while¡­) not really fresh seafood pasta and pizza was alright but cold..(i don¡¯t know how) I wouldn¡¯t go to this restaurant for food but the service was really good! they even caught flies and bees for us haha
label: Resident

name: Phillipa Graham
review: A lovely Trattoria serving local cuisine in a picturesque, quiet location. Service was discreet but attentive. Bruschetta and Tiramisu were highlights, but the local squid ink pasta and spaghetti vongole did not disappoint. Great price point for the exy Venetian locale. Highly recommend.
label: Resident

The chicken Milanese and sauteed spinach were also excellent.
