review: Had the lasagne, pretty average. Wine overpriced, my partner had penne with rocket and shrimps, nicely cooked but far too much garlic. Good atmosphere but far better places around
label: Resident

name: Leanne Morrell
review:
Fabulous service and kept us warm with heaters and blankets during our evening meal!

Risotto was to die for and the whole meal for all was incredible! A must to visit if you come out here!
label: Resident

name: Hannah M
review: Food is amazing and reasonably priced. Drinks are fantastic, and the service is incredible. Everyone here is so lovely and make you feel so welcome and they make sure you are having a brilliant time. 10/10 I would definitely recommend.
label: Resident

name: Krystal
review: Lovely ambiance for sure! The pasta with seafood was so tasty! The squid and the branzino were not as good, I wasn't too much of a fan. Decent service. Was recommended to go to this place by a local! Its set in a garden environment which is rare for Venice.
label: Resident

name: Batchie villarosa
review:
Beautiful outdoor garden area. Food was good. We had the mixed fry & mussels for appetizers! House sparkling white wine was ?
Service was ok. You can visibly see they treat locals better. We saw that in Rome and Venice
label: Resident

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
review: One of my favorite restaurants in Italy - exceptionally beautiful food that showcases not only creativity and technique, but also true to its Venetian roots and locale. I've been three times in the last month, and each time is just as good as the last. Love the sustainability and local factor, with their great care to working closely with local producers for high quality. Excellent wine selection and warm and kind service, witg high attention to detail.
label: Resident

name: Jonathan Velazquez Colon
review: We had a great experience at this trattoria. We ordered the Pizza Diavola along with two others (whose names we can¡¯t remember but were equally delicious), two local beers recommended by our friendly waitress, and ended the meal with a classic tiramisu. The food was tasty, the service was quick and attentive, and the atmosphere felt welcoming. Prices were average for the region and well worth it for the quality and experience. Highly recommend if you¡¯re in Venice looking for a solid, satisfying meal.
label: Resident

name: Jenny Zhang
review:
The food was good overall! The Florentine steak was juicy and flavorful, the grilled sea bass was really good, and the lobster pasta was also worth trying. Prices were fair for the area ¡ª not cheap, but reasonable for the location.

Everything was great until the bill arrived ¡ª there was a 3 per person ¡°coperto¡± (seat charge) and a ¡°servizio¡± fee on the bill. When I asked, the waiter said it was a tax, but I later realized it¡¯s actually a service charge. I live in the U.S., where we usually tip, so I just wish they would explain that more clearly.

The food was delicious and the atmosphere cozy. If you ask me whether I¡¯d come back ¡ª I¡¯m honestly not sure, because the extra "tax".
label: Resident

name: Simon Merriman
review: We typically tried this trattoria on our last day in Venice. What a shame as it was brilliant. Seems to mainly have locals as guests. The food was all very affordable and tasted fantastic. We had a couple of traditional starters followed by the most amazing pizzas.  The service threw us a little as the lady serving originated from England and this saved us from murdering the Italian language - she was wonderful and gave great insight. Would I go back? Yes, I wouldn¡¯t want to miss this friendly secret.
label: Resident

name: Megan McKenzie
review: Lovely little spot, family owned and operated, if you are staying in the area I highly recommend this restaurant. We had the pizza 2 nights in a row, the charcuterie &cheese board was also nice. The table next to us had the local white fish and she said it was also very good. The owner, Paulo, made us feel at home and spoke very good English. The house wine was also very good.
label: Resident

name: Katrina Avrutov
review:
What can I say? I would love not to share my opinion, so this place will stay the hidden gem forever, but we do not visit that often, here I go! We¡¯ve stayed in Venice 11 years ago and had dinner in this Trattoria every night. We came this week and we were happy to find the same amazing atmosphere of ¡®local¡¯ and ¡®homey¡¯ and ¡®only for those who know:)¡¯
But of course the most amazing is the great food - best pizza ever! Thank you, Marco! - great friendly service - thank you to all hard working people of this restaurant- and thank you Paulo for keeping this amazing place and making it even better!
We had different pizzas every evening, tried all of their pastas, several of the side dishes and local wine and beer of course - everything was perfect!
label: Resident

name: Amy Childress
review:
Delicious food, amazing staff, and they made our family celebration a perfect night to remember!

I can¡¯t say enough good things about my experience at Trattoria Rialto Novo. I was visiting Venice to celebrate my daughter¡¯s engagement and was searching for a good restaurant for the celebration dinner for the whole family. I stopped in at Rialto Novo for lunch to check out the menu and atmosphere.  The first thing I appreciated was that the restaurant is just a block away from the crowds - making it quieter and easier to connect and hear each other for a family dinner. When I shared  the situation, Tani and his team jumped in with their ideas  and options and I could see they were committed to make the night special. I visited the restaurant several times before the event to ask more questions and finalize plans and every time I arrived they made me feel welcomed and comfortable.

In the end, they worked within my budget to put together an incredible menu - I think maybe 6 courses?! - consisting of some dishes that are not on the regular restaurant menu that were very special and delicious. Our menu centered around the fresh seafood that they get in each day. But they also accommodated people in our group who were vegetarian or did not like seafood. They helped me work with the flower store just across from the restaurant to create gorgeous flower arrangements for our table.

The main course of the Mediterranean Sea bass might be the most delicious dish I¡¯ve ever had. And the dessert was incredible - I wish I had the recipe. I forgot to take pictures of every course but they were all presented beautifully!

Everything they did exceeded my expectations and I feel so lucky and grateful that I found them.
label: Resident

name: Mark Johannes
review:
Food (pizza and salad with house wine) was good. Not exceptional or bad. Service was ok. Not great, and not bad. Atmosphere, could be better(sat in courtyard and had leaves and tree parts all over the table and waiter did not clean or brush off the table when we sat).
I was surprised at the cover charge per person. It is shown on the menu (tiny letters at the bottom). To me, this is a hidden fee to get extra money. I definitely don¡¯t feel like the place deserved an extra 5 euros for the quality and service. If they need the money, put an extra euro on all items.
label: Resident

name: Arsen Barbullushi
review:
Nestled in a quiet corner of the city, is the kind of place that instantly transports you to the heart of Italy. From the warm welcome at the door to the last sip of espresso.

The menu is a love letter to classic Italian cuisine.

The wine list features several regional Italian bottles, and the staff are happy to recommend pairings. Our server, Argent, was knowledgeable and attentive without being overbearing ¡ª the kind of service that elevates a good meal into a memorable one.
I¡¯ll be back again ??
label: Resident

name: Yizhou Wu
review:
No more prawn ravioli, and no more almond tart

It's an okay place, the food isn't mindblowing. Simple flavours, pasta texture was fine

Seafood was quite fresh, portions are big
label: Resident

name: Nik Makepeace
review: Walked in off the street for lunch with my daughter and had the 'menu di mare' which was great: pickled sardines, a Venetian standard, along with a light, fresh tomato and prawn tagliatelle, and grilled king prawns with polenta. Food was rustic but good, great flavours, no pretence. The waiter was very nice to my daughter, and the place just had a good simple vibe.
label: Resident

name: Jonathan Stewart
review: The food here was extraordinary! We highly recommend the Gnocchi Bolognese and the Spaghetti al Nero di seppia. We got there as they opened and grabbed a great seat. The staff was welcoming and super helpful. Give this place a try.
label: Resident

name: Ashley
review: A last minute stop looking for an authentic meal. It did not disappoint. Welcomed in and enjoyed a delicious meal. Started with a cheese plate appetizer and a litre of wine. Followed by gnocchi and pesto pasta. We were not rushed and thoroughly enjoyed our meal here.
label: Resident

name: Cedric Chang
review: Fresh seafood, friendly service, and local flavor all in one spot. The black ink pasta is a must-try¡ªrich, savory, and authentic. Fried seafood was crispy and light, and oysters were fresh. A great value for quality. Highly recommended if you¡¯re visiting Venice.
label: Resident

name: Taylor E
review: I had this restaurant saved as a recommendation and we went after the Vivaldi concert since it was near the church. It was later in the night on a Friday and the restaurant was mostly empty. However, everything was great and delicious. Service was perfect and the food was fantastic. The bread was the fluffiest and softest bread we had in all of Italy.. it was so delicious. I ordered the spinach ravioli with sausage and my partner ordered duck gnocchi. We then split the tiramisu for dessert and watermelon was provided on the house for us. The interior of this restaurant was beautiful and charming. They had a seasonal/daily menu as well as the standard menu and it is evident that this changes often which is neat. Highly recommend!
label: Resident

name: Kye Vowles
review:
I had the Tagliatelle with porcini mushrooms & local small shrimp & my partner had the Spaghetti Carbonara. The flavours were absolutely AMAZING. Great service, quick and polite.
The white wine was the best house wine I've had here too.

Would 10/10 recommend to anyone.
label: Resident

name: Sabrina Huang
review: Wonderful local trattoria in Venice! The food was fresh and authentic, with generous portions and a cozy atmosphere. The staff was friendly and made us feel at home. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a true Venetian experience.
label: Resident

name: Life of Misstep
review: Excellent place to eat. Very fancy. Very knowledgeable server with an awesome sense of humor and made me aware of the proper way to consume grappa lol
label: Resident

name: Darcy Morgan
review: This humble trattoria is warm, friendly and serves excellent food. Traditional Venetian dishes are accompanied by seasonal specialties with local ingredients. Serving staff is attentive and knowledgeable. This would be a 5/5 if the menu prices were more in line with the rustic nature. However, it is Venice. So what isn¡¯t expensive?
label: Resident

name: Arie Rozichner
review: A local guy recommended this place to us to try out Venetian cicchetti (I memorized it as similar to tapas). And it was a great experience! We had several dishes that were available and loved them. Sardines fried and marinated, squid, tuna, salami after cold prosecco made our lunch quite unique. Laura is a true soul of the place, always positive, quick and efficient. The desert cake with espresso was delicious. Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Bill Alber
review: This a great local restaurant serving local specialties. If you're staying at the Hyatt Centric or another hotel nearby, don't miss this. The food is creative and fabulous. The staff was friendly, knowledgeable, and just the right amount of attentive. We had a raw seafood platter to start followed by wonderful mushroom and scallop risotto. Slow roasted beef and stuffed sea bass for entrees. The wine they recommended was world class delicious. This was a reasonably priced, world class dining experience.
label: Resident

name: Patrick Morgan
review:
Could not recommend enough. The food was unreal, I would strongly suggest the seafood platter, or the risotto.

A table right on the canal surrounded by Murano glass, and drinking from it. Couldn't be better.

Service was perfect. As a hospitality manager in the UK I was very impressed, particularly  how they dealt with a rude countrymen of mine who didn't realise closed means closed. They were very courteous and polite despite the described people being a pain.

Would recommend to anyone coming to Murano.
label: Resident

name: Veronika Komolova
review: A wonderful place with a calm and welcoming atmosphere, excellent wine, and truly authentic Venetian cuisine. We enjoyed it so much that we came back twice to try more traditional dishes. The staff are attentive, professional, and genuinely hospitable, which made the experience even more special. It feels like the kind of restaurant you always want to return to and keep discovering every dish on the menu. We¡¯re so glad we followed our host¡¯s recommendation ¡ª the locals really know best!
label: Resident

name: Loo Yeo
review:
Trattoria ai Cacciatori greets you warmly, the kind of place that will do its best to find you a table even when full. That welcome sets the tone, but the kitchen¡¯s efforts proved uneven across the two evenings we were there, the first being better..

The house pasta with vegetables and muddica atturata ¨C the Sicilian style toasted breadcrumbs ¨C showed promise but was let down by timid seasoning and a lack of anchovy depth. Handmade and freshly cut, yes, but more middling than memorable. The chicken breast with potato wedges followed the same line: neatly plated, but slightly dry and under-seasoned, an effort that fell short. Even the scallops with pumpkin pur¨¦e and marinated mushrooms, strong in concept, stumbled on portioning ¨C balance was lost, and the dish simply didn¡¯t come together. The daily catch, a grilled sea bream with vegetables and polenta, ought to have been the safe triumph of simplicity. Instead, it was handled carelessly, proving disappointing.

There were brighter moments. The squid-ink tagliolini with clams, bottarga, and prawn tartare struck the right note, clean and fresh if still wanting more sharpness from lemon and depth from the bottarga (7.5/10). The off-menu fritto misto was the real standout: crisp, well-seasoned, and confidently fried, it captured exactly why such a dish endures (9/10). The scampi risotto, too, was tasty if a little blurred, a clearer stock would have lifted it further (7/10). Grilled octopus with broccoli pur¨¦e and crispy leeks had potential but lacked smoke and tenderness, while the pistachio semifreddo, chosen with some expectation, closed the meal on a lighter, less satisfying note.

In the end, Ai Cacciatori offers warmth and flashes of skill, but the food too often feels like missed opportunity. With more care and sharper execution, it could match its welcome. As it stands, it¡¯s a house of highs and lows.
label: Resident

name: tuvvi d
review: The staff are friendly. It is an authentic restaurant serving the local cuisine. I had Grilled vegetables, vegetable soup, penne Arrabbiata. Everything was fresh and tasty. Tiramisu was tasty as well. I would recommend them.
label: Resident

name: Kirk Colledge
review: We decided to stop by for some drinks and an appetizer while waiting for an appointment. There were five of us and we all ordered spritzers or beer. We also had some brescetta appetizers. Since we only had thirty minutes we had no time for a full meal. The staff was very rude and seemed upset we weren't ordering more food even though they had many open tables. When the bill came they charged a cover charge for three people, even though they did not mention that when we were ordering.  The food and drinks were adequate. Wevhad great experiences at other restaurants close by. Would not recommend.
label: Resident

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Shara Guarnaccia
review: Excellent food, beautifully plated! Kind and knowledgeable staff. Great wine selection  and the interior garden is beautiful and peaceful
label: Resident

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Robert Reinius
review:
Excellent price to quality ratio. Simply great quality local specialities, with creative modifications. Very comprehensive wine list.
When you're looking for solid good food in less casual than Bistrot de Venise environment.
Really friendly service.
label: Resident

place_title: Trattoria Al Vecio Portal Laguna e Oltre
name: Roger Hawes
review:
Was recommended by hotel but it was a terribly experience, particularly compared with other local Trattoria we¡¯d been to. Host was not welcoming (which we put down to poor English skills which seemed strange based on other places) and the mains were terrible. We had lamb and a pork main off the standard menu. When they arrived they were the wrong way round and we said that, but they could distinguish between the two ! We did based on taste. The mains were no better than canteen food and no way worth the 25 euros for lamb and 18 for the pork.

It was a very disappointing evening. I¡¯m sure if we were Italian it would have been much better.
label: Resident

name: Kacper ?wierad
review: The food at Trattoria La Battigia was absolutely phenomenal. This is the kind of place that should be written about in local newspapers, as I truly believe it is by far the most worthwhile restaurant to visit in the entire region. Special attention must be given to the gnocchi in porcini mushroom sauce with scampi. This dish takes you on a culinary journey, culminating in a catharsis for your taste buds. Once is simply not enough ¨C one day, I will return here solely for this restaurant and this extraordinary dish. I wholeheartedly recommend it to everybody.
label: Resident

name: Catalina Dascalu
review: We had a fantastic dinner at Trattoria La Battigia! The gnocchi con porcini e gamberi was full of flavours ¡ª soft, homemade gnocchi with mushrooms and perfectly cooked shrimps. The Tartufo nero was a real highlight ¡ª rich and aromatic without being too much. Friendly staff and a relaxed, local feel. Would definitely come back!
label: Resident

name: Veronika V
review:
Excellent food we had the house plate spagetti with lobster - AMAZING!
recommend to try looked like locals did too
label: Resident

name: Mira Kov¨¢cs
review:
Google maps says they open at 18:30. We showed up exactly at that time. They wouldn¡¯t open for 10 more minutes so we went in and said hi. A woman told us we have to wait until 7pm. So we waited patiently in front of the door. A bit after 7pm, when they still weren¡¯t open, a woman came up to the door and locked it in front of our eyes.
Either update your google maps or don¡¯t give the wrong info to people. Very disrespectful
label: Resident

name: Jane Peshkova
review: Amazing find near St. Mark¡¯s Square! The scallops appetizer was incredible, the spinach & cheese ravioli and beef fillet Rossini were outstanding, and the sea bass was perfect. Even the tiramisu (and we don¡¯t usually like desserts!) just melted in the mouth. Their homemade limoncello was the perfect finish. Highly recommend! ????????
label: Resident

name: Laura Pariso
review: A local friend recommended this spot to me. It¡¯s tucked away behind a fish market, so it can be a little tricky to find, but once I stepped inside, I instantly loved the atmosphere. I ordered the raw bar platter, it was fresh and beautifully presented, and the squid ink pasta was absolutely delicious.
label: Resident

name: suleewan sueram
review: There is one staff, the food is delicious, and they also have a welcome drink service. They are very kind. The atmosphere in the restaurant is very good, but the entrance is a bit difficult to find because if you are not a local, you probably won't know this restaurant because you have to pass the fish market.
label: Resident

name: Maria Norton
review: The food was not great - expected fresh Italian seafood and what we got was overpriced chewy lobster. Sat to a really loud table and were moved when complained about it. If you¡¯re thinking about coming here to taste Italian food? I recommend you pick elsewhere! Even after leaving most of our meal, we were still charged full price. At 150, I¡¯d expect much better!
label: Resident

name: Veronika Komolova
review:
This trattoria is a hidden gem, tucked away on a narrow street ¡ª and I¡¯m so glad we followed the recommendation and came here. The place is cozy and full of character, with an authentic bar counter and a wonderful view of a quiet canal away from the crowds.

The staff are exceptionally warm, attentive, and professional ¡ª their hospitality and charisma make the whole experience unforgettable, and tipping here truly feels like a heartfelt thank-you. The food and local wine were outstanding. I tried the Venetian specialty Bigoli di Bassano alla salsa di acciughe, while my husband had Gnocchi di patate fatti in casa con carciofi e mazzancolle ¡ª both dishes were excellent and gave us a deeper appreciation of Venetian cuisine.

A place full of charm that we would happily return to again and again.
label: Resident

name: Total Medical & Dental Supplies
review: I came to this wonderful restaurant 20 years ago and it's still the best one. I know you have to walk around Murano to find it but believe me, will be the best experience ever. Special mention to the bacala mantecata a typical Venetian dish. It's prepared daily here with great taste, for me is the best. This is where locals come to eat, don't expect a typical turist restaurant. This is what we call cucina de la nonna (Grandsma cooking style). Don't miss spaghetti alle vongole and the tiramisu which is hand made. Every time I come to venice this is a must-visit. Away from the crowd and perfect for the real italian experience
label: Resident

name: Nick Tehrani
review: You know your in the right place when you see local Murrano residents daily.  The moment we walked in, they welcomed us like family. The cuttlefish pasta was the best I¡¯ve ever had perfectly al dente with rich, briny depth that is romatic to the senses. The risotto changes daily and ours was silky and beautifully balanced. Fresh fish caught the same day and served as if it jumped onto the plate from the ocean.  Lovely attentive service and excellent food all around an easy five stars with a smile. We will come back again and again.
label: Resident

name: Rachel S
review:
This is honestly the best gluten free pizza I've had in Venice! No way could I tell the difference in pizza base.
The service was attentive, the food came out quickly and was heavenly.
If I had more time in Venice, I would be going back.
The location is perfect. The outside seating was very picturesque and not on the main thoroughfare.
5 stars all round.
label: Resident

name: Mikhail Kazansev
review:
It¡¯s probably the most ¡°italiano vero¡± place I¡¯ve ever visited. Many thanks to the wonderful Marco, who gave us great advice and even helped us improve our Italian. It was a truly fabulous experience.
The food was absolutely delicious, and we had never seen desserts like this before. It was a truly fabulous experience.
label: Resident

name: Robert Artac
review: Cuttlefish ink pasta was good. I wouldn't do it again but has nothing to do with how it was prepared. The pasta was bang-on al dente. Perfectly prepared. Wine selection was excellent. Very kind waiter and loved his impromptu whistling. Overall would definitely go again.
label: Resident

name: §¡§Þ§Ú§ß§Ñ §¤§à§â§Ò
review:
We had an amazing experience at Trattoria del Local!
The food was absolutely delicious ¡ª full of flavor and beautifully presented. The service was top-notch: friendly, professional, and made us feel truly welcome. A perfect spot for anyone who appreciates great cuisine and warm hospitality.

And a fun detail: be prepared ¡ª the owner herself might be serving you, and you won¡¯t even realize it! ??

Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: B P
review:
I¡¯ll admit, I was sceptical about food in Venice: too many places trading on location rather than care. But Trattoria del Local completely changed my mind. The cooking here is thoughtful, generous, and full of quiet confidence.

The fegato alla veneziana was, without exaggeration, the best I¡¯ve had in my life ¡­perfectly cooked, deeply savoury, balanced with just the right sweetness. So tender. And the selection of amari and digestivi was extraordinary; clearly curated by someone who loves flavours and knows how to end a meal beautifully.

The team were warm, knowledgeable, and relaxed in the best way. They didn¡¯t perform hospitality¡­ they simply were hospitable. The key ingredients to my favourite places. I¡¯ll be back, and will rave about the place. Siete bravi!

My friend and I left feeling genuinely looked after and so happy. Thanks for doing what you¡¯re doing. Greetings to Salvatore!
label: Resident

name: Chiara Di Chio
review:
Just amazing- Giovanna Benedetta and Manuel are incredible hosts taking the atmosphere to another level. Salvatore and Wally in the kitchen brought us back to the authentic tastes of our Italian grandmas - Venice was missing a place like this, the world needs more Trattoria del Local!!
We¡¯ll definitely be back!!
label: Resident

name: Carrie (¥­¥ã¥ê©`)
review:
Absolutely amazing!!!
I encountered two italian people while waiting and they said it's the place where local loves coming.
They were so right!!!!!
We loved the food!!! Sooooooo delicious and even though they were extremely busy, they were so friendly!!!! Thank you so much!!!!!!
label: Resident

name: Ji?¨ª Vojt¨ª?ek
review: Nice restaurant with a great location near a canal. Oftentimes, you can see a grocery gondola parked nearby. The staff was very friendly and helpful. At peak hours you might have to wait a bit to be served and before your food is ready, otherwise, it was a good experience for an affordable price.
label: Resident

name: Cecile Biezanowski
review:
The best restaurant we have eaten in Venice. We are really picky with the food. Quality and price are very important for us.
The menu is short which is also a good sign. The boss is very nice and friendly. The food quality was great  Very tasty and original. Small restaurant in a nice and quiet area. We really recommend the place !!!
label: Resident

name: Caroline Bonin
review: We honestly don¡¯t understand the good reviews for this restaurant. The food was very average, overly salty, and lacked real flavor. Prices were extremely high, and we didn¡¯t see any locals dining there. We suspect they may have changed owner or chef recently. Definitely not a place we would return to.
label: Resident

name: Gregorius Deus
review: This was a very good experience for us. A tattoria with a heart. We felt very welcomed and were served with utmost care, we were treated as regulars from the very beginning. Everything went smooth and the food was really good. We've been there almost every day during our stay in Venice so you can imagine that we really liked the place. Mussels and Pizza with speck were our favorite. If you want a good food and experience you most definitely should visit Pan e Vin.
label: Resident

name: Ella Burton
review:
50% discount with the fork app but this is limited menu. Prices are very reasonable though so wouldn¡¯t make much of a difference.
Staff very friendly and welcoming. Super super fast service.
37.50 for 1 starter 2 mains 1 dessert and 2 alcoholic drinks cant complain really.
Tortellini and lasagna were really tasty but portions could be larger.
Would recommend
label: Resident

name: Ethan Carpio
review: Great local restaurant right next to the water. Away from the commotion at the city center. Was recommended to me by my local friend who lives in Venice. Food was amazing.
label: Resident

name: Caryn Suehowicz
review: Local fixed menu restaurant that everyone should take the opportunity to experience. All locals and university conference goers hoping to get a seat for lunch, but our party of 21 had a long anticipated reservation. We are so glad we booked that lunch. The pasta was fresh and the sauces were exquisite. The meat options were varied and all were delicious.
label: Resident

name: Andrew Witts
review:
Don¡¯t eat here.

I was recommended by a friend who lived locally, but as so many reviews warn, this place is a disappointment. It may once have had charm, but that¡¯s long gone. The food is cold, bland, and completely uninspired, while the service is careless, rude, and painfully slow.

The wine selection is laughable, apparently the only option is terrible Prosecco, and don¡¯t expect wine glasses unless you specifically ask. Want seasoning for your tasteless food? Good luck, most staff will tell you they only have pepper, and only one particular person seems to know otherwise.

The rudeness when attempting to pay was the final straw. I couldn¡¯t pay without a receipt, and thankfully I didn¡¯t - when finally given a card reader, the total was more than my bill, and they tried to overcharge me.

Miserable food. Miserable service (apart from the one female staff member who was actually trying to be helpful and smiley - Thank you!). Overall a miserable experience. Don¡¯t waste your time or money here.
label: Resident

name: J Wu
review: Delicious food. We had a party of 10 for lunch and our hotel concierge called ahead so they could start setting up the table. Location was very close to St. Marks square. Food was excellent. Especially the seafood salad; it was a real salad!  The pastas were not too salty which seems to have been a trend with recent local restaurants.
label: Resident

name: Miriam Oehme
review:
Zanze XVI is just a short walk from Piazzale Roma, the main entry point into the city if arriving by bus or car. It is located right at a small canal. It is considered one of the oldest restaurants in Venice, of course run by many different owners. The current owner kept the historic look and feel widely untouched, and the atmosphere as casual and relaxed as a traditional restaurant would be. Service is highly professional, and the food very inventive although based on local tradition and focused on locally sourced products.
The tasting menu contained very flavorful dishes centered around vegetables and fish. The pace was just about right, as were portion sizes. While there was no wine pairing available, the very knowledgeable sommelier presented a list of wines by the glass so that you could create your own pairing based on your personal preferences. Many wines were from the region, but also from other parts of Italy or even other wine-making countries.
Overall a great dining experience - flavor-forward food, yet light and elegant, and a very good selection of wines.
label: Resident

name: Holly Marland
review: I discovered this gem by accident 5 years ago, just 2 months before we were all brought to a standstill during lockdown. I¡¯m so happy I managed to return and have another truly wonderful meal there last night. I was blown away by the flavours - each course matched perfectly with different wine thanks to the recommendation of the gentleman serving who seems enthused by his craft and was more than happy to discuss the flavours and produce. Top marks for table presentation, atmosphere and creating wonderful dishes using local produce. I can¡¯t recommend this place highly enough. I will certainly return, hopefully in less than five years!
label: Resident

name: David Chapple
review:
This is a "must eat at" restaurant in Venice. The owners are charming and understandably proud of the quality and taste of their amazing menu.
I would have wanted to go everyday so I could try every dish.
You are also away from the crazy crowds and feel this has a genuine local feel and flavour.
label: Resident

name: John G
review: Recommended by my B&B host and it did not disappoint. Awesome pizza, very good Venetian beer and prompt service. A spot popular with locals so you can expect good things.
label: Resident

name: Nata Pie
review: Really great food, super nice service, usually a place for locals that was recommended to us by our hotel, has more to offer than the classic go-to italian dishes. Really worth a visit!!
label: Resident

name: Giuliano Porcelli
review: Trattoria che sorge dove non te lo aspetti, in un panorama di ciminiere ed edifici industriali, alcuni dei quali in decadenza. Ci siamo capitati per caso e abbiamo scoperto una realt¨¤ singolare. L'ambiente ¨¨ luminoso e molto pulito, i tavolo sono ordinati e viene immediatamente voglia di sedersi e mangiare. La cucina ¨¨ semplice e casalinga e i prezzi sono pi¨´ che onesti, con men¨´ a prezzo fisso ma flessibile nella composizione e nel prezzo. Il men¨´ ¨¨ diverso ma fisso per ogni giorno della settimana. Gli avventori sono principalmente operai della zona, ma non mancano anche i colletti bianchi. L'accoglienza ¨¨ amichevole e attenta al cliente, senza esagerare. Oggi ci ¨¨ capitata una pasta e fagioli veramente ben fatta e un secondo di carne che non avrebbe sfigurato in un ristorante di pi¨´ alte pretese. Il locale ¨¨ chiuso la sera, ma ¨¨ prenotabile per serate familiari o tra amici. Il gestore ci ha detto che la trattoria ¨¨ gestita dalla sua famiglia ininterrottamente dal 1913, ed ¨¨ molto fiero di questa tradizione. Il locale ¨¨ anche una specie di galleria d'arte, perch¨¦ le pareti sono impreziosite da molti quadri - non in vendita - di pittori soprattutto veneziani del '900, alcuni dei quali molto conosciuti ed apprezzati. In definitiva, una piacevole scoperta.
label: Resident

name: Matteo Esposito
review:
5 lettere, fa battere forte il cuore: ¨¨ Pilla.
Convenienza, cortesia, convivialit¨¤... mi vengono in mente migliaia di aggettivi per descrivere questo locale ma non li elenco perch¨¦ sarebbero una mortificazione dell'effettiva esperienza che vivrai.
Menzione d'onore per i tramezzini, belli paffuti, goduriosi... da mangiare in compagnia all'esterno come vuole la tradizione.
label: Resident

name: Katie Goll
review:
This place was a recommendation from a local gentleman and it was wonderful. Sitting next to the water and watching the world go by with a glass of wine and some good food is the best.
Even our kids ate all their food (a rare occurrence).
label: Resident

name: Natalia Dorofeeva
review: It¡¯s a family restaurant, local people love it. You can taste and Venetian food , and Sicilian. Also ask about specialties of the day. Prices are not high. Lunch is served until 3 pm. Then they will open only for dinner. I had cuttlefish with ink and pasta with gambas. Both dishes were delicious.
label: Resident

name: Eloy Yip
review:
Situated on Calle Crosera in the Venetian¡¯s Castello neighborhood, my partner, our friends, family members, and I decided on Trattoria Alla Fonte as our first meal last Saturday off the beaten path.

In Italian, ¡°trattoria" (plural "trattorie")
means a type of informal, usually small, restaurant that serves traditional, hearty, home-style meals, often with a regional focus.

We came there by chance and didn't regret it. Upon arrival, we were welcomed by the staff and got a table for six outdoors in a pleasant and cozy vibe among other patrons.

The menu has multiple language options, offering a variety of traditional, genuine Italian dishes, i.e., pizza and pasta, and has something for everyone, including vegetarians and vegans.

We decided on the Grilled Vegetables, Mixed Salad, Luna Salad, Chicken Pizza, Funghi Pizza, Margarita Pizza, and Caprese Pizza. Complimentary bread started our meal.

Every single bite was delightfully tasty, using only the freshest ingredients, ensuring that every dish was bursting with flavor. The cost was slightly cheaper than other places in the area with a perfect portion size. The staff was friendly, and the service was fabulous.

With its convenient location and delicious menu, it's the perfect spot for a memorable dining experience in the City of Canals, which we can recommend.
label: Resident

name: Sarah M
review:
The most amazing pesto pasta I have ever tasted!!! Handmade and fresh - it certainly showed!! Every single bite was so full of rich flavor and delight. Perfect portion size - it was very filling! I usually experience unpleasant symptoms when eating gluten, but I was able to eat this without negative effects!

They also do have quite a few gluten free options. My husband got a GF pizza, which was delicious. Staff were cordial and looked like they actually enjoyed working there! Nice atmosphere inside and out. I am still missing that pasta!!
(We ate lunch here on 5/19/25)
label: Resident

name: Carmen Chilson
review: Food was good but nothing special. Literally everyone dining there that evening was speaking English so it doesn't seem like a place locals eat at. Their menu has multiple language options which is useful. Slightly cheaper than other places in the area
label: Resident

name: T D
review: One of the classic Trattorie in Venice, mentioned in many guides and highly recommended. Pictures of celebrities on the walls (Woody Allen, Lionel Messi and so on) but none of the pictures are from recently. Fun fact: ? Carampane¡° used to be the prostitutes you could find around here when this part of town was the red light district. Have a good look at the logo of the Trattoria and you will notice the reference. They serve only sea food, daily dishes dependent on what¡¯s available at the Mercato Rialto nearby. We had the mixed sea food salad for starters, then the fried mixed seafood and the monkfish (that was a daily dishes). The kids had spaghetti with tomato sauce at 18 EUR (!) per dish not much else for kids. Quality was ok, the monkfish was dry though and some of the fried fish had an odd soapy taste. Service was friendly but not warm. The Interieur is classy, clean and you feel the history of the place.
label: Resident

name: Alex
review: An excellent restaurant in Venice, recommended to me by a local. I¡¯m glad I¡¯d made a reservation to dine here. The food was delicious and the environment delightful. I had cuttlefish with polenta, a glass of wine and tiramisu for dessert. I also shared an antipasto - a selection of fish/seafood.
label: Resident

name: Loo Yeo
review: Busa alla Torre is a Murano institution, named for the vanished belltower of San Stefano. Set in a sunlit square, it¡¯s famed among locals for Venetian authenticity and fresh seafood. I began with moeche fritte ¨C soft-shell lagoon crabs, a rare spring and autumn delicacy, sweet, salty, and egg-crisp, offset by fried seasonal vegetables. Then came spaghetti alla marinara: pasta perfectly al dente, with fresh shellfish, ripe tomato, and a lift of parsley ¨C proper delicious. Espresso closed with traditional biscuits: buttery esse Buranei and golden zaeti. Honest, vibrant Venetian cooking.
label: Resident

name: Jeffrey Andrews
review: Lovely unpretentious place. Authentic and appreciated by the locals who recommended it me.  Inexpensive by Venice standards with a lovely view of a church.  Seafood is a focus but everything I had was superb.
label: Resident

name: Sheyla Arango
review: The best: outside lunch with a view. Tables outside surrounded by the beautiful church and the canal are priceless. They offer a fixed menu (15) that is a good deal actually: first dish, second dish and side dish. I enjoyed the pasta alle cozze, it was the best of what I've tried. As second, I had fish that was no good no bad. No taste at all I would say. House wine was good too. Clean bathroom.
label: Resident

name: Jennifer Joy
review:
We had a wonderful meal at Trattoria Ca¡¯ Foscari al Canton! The atmosphere was cozy and authentic, making us feel like we had discovered a true local gem in Venice.

The food was excellent ¡ª fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. From the starters to the main dishes, everything was cooked with care and full of that traditional Venetian taste you hope to find. The pasta was especially delicious, and the portions were generous.

The staff were warm, friendly, and attentive, adding to the overall experience. Their recommendations were spot-on and made our meal even more enjoyable.

If you¡¯re looking for authentic Venetian food in a welcoming setting, Trattoria Ca¡¯ Foscari al Canton is absolutely worth a visit. Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Danielle Martin
review: The mixed vegetable appetizer was very good. I would skip the lamb, it was a small portion for how expensive it was, I can make it much better at home. The lasagna was good but not the best I¡¯ve ever had.
label: Resident

name: Kirk Colledge
review: We stopped for some lunch and a few drinks while walking around in Venice. The service and food were outstanding. We had the Risotto, pizza, lasagna and Bruscetta. Everything was great. Would highly recommend.
label: Resident

name: Kate?ina Osmikov¨¢
review: Perfect food and service, excellent views, sitting just above the water. Highly recommended!
label: Resident

name: Andy Britner
review:
A great experience at this restaurant.
Very attentive and friendly staff, such a nice visit.
Our table had a whole selection of food, but we didn't take pictures of everything, just 2 dishes as the others were dived in to right away!
All pastas were perfect, sauces delicious and fresh and plentiful.
If back in Venice, we would visit here again.
label: Resident

name: Lou
review:
We were looking for this place for two days after accidentally passing it during day walks around town. Found by by a chance - Thank you Universe! ???? really wanted to try cuttlefish ink risotto and yes it was very much worth the try. Fantastic bruschetta¡¯s for a starter sealed the deal.
A big plus - Italian music in the background which was so rare to find everywhere else!
label: Resident

name: Daniel Valchev
review: We liked it so much, that we visited it twice during our stay in Venice. The food, the service, the atmosphere - everything was very good. Good place to try the local cuisine.
label: Resident

name: Wyatt Cuddington
review: Go here. So delicious. Had sweet and sour seafood, spaghetti, and duck ragu. Mezzo mezzo spritzes and affogatos for dessert. Every bite was fantastic and the service was great. Lovely local neighborhood off the beaten path.
label: Resident

name: Lukas Nussbaumer
review: The food was okay, but not amazing. The carbonara was too thick and egg-heavy. The ravioli was better. The service was poor. The wait staff was friendly and attentive, but otherwise inadequate. Very slow, didn't clear away used dishes and glasses, and forgot to bring a mineral water order. There are better restaurants in this price range.
label: Resident

name: H. E.
review: We thought about eating there, but the waiter attacked our dogs. No sorry no stopping. Went somewhere else
label: Resident

name: Kevin Simmons
review: Well priced pizza was fantastic, had to ask specifically for the pizza menu though. Was given bruschetta and prosecco as a welcome but then charged for it. The charge was only 6 euros total so still worked out as a relatively cheap and filling lunch for central Venice.
label: Resident

name: Cheryl Maplesden
review: We had one of our best meals at Trattoria de Bruno. The service and food were 5 stars. We had a great pasta dish as the first serving, followed by delicious steak tips in an incredible gravy with peas and carrots.  For dessert, we enjoyed a refreshing flan-like dessert. Also,  the restaurant was well air conditioned.  Anyone who has experienced the heat and humidity of Venice knows how important air conditioning is.  I would highly recommend this restaurant.
label: Resident

name: Indra Ba??¨©te
review: Wonderful service, prosecco as a welcome drink, compliment from the kitchen, food great, prices adequete, and limancello at the and, according to Italian traditions. Very cosy athmosphere! Recommend!
label: Resident

name: Konstantinas Jacikas
review: Me and my wife were recommended to this place by local gondolier as authentic and affordable restaurant to eat. We are really glad that we took his advice as our visit was amazing. Great atmosphere, beautiful view outside and indoors, attentive service and wonderful meals. The seafood was so fresh and tasty and I would definitely recommend to try! Also we were very happy with waiters who made our dinner to feel very exclusive. A+++
label: Resident

name: Saraid Cann
review: Lovely authentic trattoria, great food, good buzz, refined and yet homely too - where the locals eat. The waiters were friendly and welcoming having gentle banter with all of the diners. The food was lovely with great portions and a pizza, wine and water came to aroybd 25 inc service. The place was pristine with spotless tablecloths and elegant glassware while the children's drawings behind the bar added family charm ??. Very buzzy but not overwhelming and no one (literally no one) had their phones out - everyone just enjoying the experience. Added bonus was that next to us was a table full of gondoliers eating heartly after a hard day's work. Best in Venice, will definitely go back.
label: Resident

name: Julia Dunbar
review:
We came across this trattoria when looking for a simple lunch. We struck lucky!
Delicious food, efficient and staff, fine wine. But don¡¯t just take our word for it
-ask the several tables of gondoliers who ate here during our visit- I assume that they know a good, value for money local restaurant. I¡¯m not sure if some of the reviewers here actually visited the same place! Highly recommended.
label: Resident

name: Lisette Marin
review: Such a great place to eat, especially if you want a break from pasta and pizza. We had the chicken cutlet; it was delicious & filling!
label: Resident

name: Kira Zatsepina
review: What an amazing place! The food was delicious, the service very friendly and the atmosphere very special. The recommended wine was great. The prices are very fair too. There¡¯s a lot of locals here. We had a reservation from a few weeks before, and I think it¡¯s best to try to have one. Dinner here was the perfect final chord for our magical day in Venice. Thank you! I cannot recommend this place enough!
label: Resident

name: Edgars Matisans
review: We stopped by for their 4 Aperol, which, while cheap, seemed to be missing enough prosecco. Overall, it was a pretty average happy hour cocktail. The place was empty, and we didn't order any food, but we were a bit put off by the personnel smoking on the guest terrace.
label: Resident

name: jonathan swaby
review:
We ate here for lunch. The place was filled with local workers taking their lunch break.
My wife and I had pizza and my son had some sort of pasta dish.
I ordered a pizza with raw ham, but when it was served there was no ham. So, it was a cheese pizza. The crust was really good. The cheese to tomato sauce ratio was also good. The flavor in general was good. Not the best pizza I have ever had, but also not the worst.
Neither my wife not soon comment on their meals, so I will assume they were average.
label: Resident

name: Blogger Review
review:
Avoid unless you are looking to spend lots of money on salty and low quality seafood.

Ordered a mussel spaghetti (look at the photo and see if you can spot the mussels) - 18 euro. Food was salty without any mussel fragrance in it. Felt like I¡¯m eating a salty tomato spaghetti.

There is also a cover charge of 3 euro per pax and an additional 10% service charge for the total bill.
label: Resident

name: Bratislav D?ombi?
review:
Tried Trattoria alla Madonna while in Venice for some authentic local cuisine. The black squid with polenta was decent¡ªclassic and traditional. The atmosphere has a nostalgic charm, and you get the sense that this place once had a strong reputation. However, it seems to be resting on that past a bit too much.

One downside: they charge both coperto and servizio, which is uncommon even for Venice. That felt a bit excessive. Also, the staff could be more welcoming¡ªservice was brisk, almost cold. Overall, not a bad meal, but there¡¯s definitely room for improvement if they want to live up to their name.
label: Resident

name: Philine Cloet
review: I had an amazing dinner at this restaurant! The food was really amazing, especially the ravioli with shrimps. The staff is beyond friendly and welcoming. I would definitely recommend this place & come back the next time I visite Venice.
label: Resident

name: Zo? Lamming
review: Absolutely beautiful seafood! Very small and cosy, staff were excellent- attentive and knew the menu very thoroughly. The menu is small and there are daily dishes based on what seafood they can get locally. I would thoroughly recommend for some delicious local and inventive seafood.
label: Resident

name: Colin Smith
review: The whole menu is Venetian style seafood and the seafood here was quite good.   The branzino and sea bass both tasted very fresh and had very nice cream/butter/herbacious sauces to go with them. The tiramisu was delicious. The service was friendly and helpful. The location outside was right on one of the waterways. My wife and I thoroughly enjoyed the meal, I'd recommend to anyone.
label: Resident

name: Julie Snyder
review: Best food we had in Venice by far. Quiet and beautiful setting with just 10 tables inside and ac! You could also sit outside on the canal. The waiter, Diego was incredible. We started our meal with a complimentary glass of presseco. Every dish was fresh and delicious and they were very accommodating of my son¡¯s allergies. Highly suggest going here. Made a reservation in advance although was probably not necessary.
label: Resident

name: Andrea
review: Can¡¯t say anything about the food, since we only had drinks. The drinks were a great price and the staff was kind. One of the waiter was truly having a great time, made great jokes and kept an eye out for our needs. The next day he recognised us on the streets again, which shows how much attention he pays to his guests.
label: Resident

name: Natalie Park
review: I came here because I was looking to have an experience with the fegato alla veneziana AKA venetian style liver and onions and found them highly recommended through my Google research. Honestly, it was a life changing meal - I never knew liver and onions could taste this good. The liver was so soft and flavorful. With that said, I usually do enjoy liver, and my fiance who hates liver tried it and noticed it was good but he still didn't like the texture/ taste of the organ meat as he kept chewing it. My fiance had a pasta dish - perfectly cooked. The table bread was better than it looked, but it doesn't come with any butter or oil, so I used the bread with my delicious dish to soak up all the flavor and enjoy it more fully. I'm not a big dessert person, but the tiramisu was very good also. The service was excellent! We were very lucky to get there last open table without reservations as we rushed to get there right when they opened, but probably would recommend having a reservation ahead of time as we watched them turn away several unreserved customers while we were eating.
label: Resident

name: John G
review: The food was excellent!  I had the mixed fried fish and it was incredible.  You could not taste the oil.  It was fried perfectly.
label: Resident

name: Gabriella Winniman
review: Small place, not fancy, but delicious food. My husband and I both had seafood risotto. It was a very generous portion with the seafood perfectly cooked. The service is friendly and efficient.
label: Resident

name: Nadia Guzun
review:
My husband and I ordered pasta and drinks, and a couple of minutes later, we decided to order some grilled vegetables. First, our drinks were served, followed by the pasta. When we asked for the bill, I noticed that the vegetables were included, even though they hadn¡¯t been brought to our table.

After mentioning this to the waiter, he spoke with his colleagues and suggested that we could pay the full price and settle the cost of the vegetables in cash. I agreed, but I was surprised that he didn¡¯t even apologize for the oversight.

What if I hadn't checked the bill? I would have ended up paying for food that I hadn¡¯t been served. Overall, I would rate the service as 2 stars. The food itself was okay.
label: Resident

name: ¹ËÎÞÓï
review: Does not let us seat after we bought ice cream and pizza at the counter. Does not kindly tell us to leave but to shoo us. Waitress tells us to wait for the second person in line when we are the first. Ngl the price was affordable but was  bad. The pizza was still cold after heated.
label: Resident

name: M.A. Bailey
review: This place is small but it had very good food. I had tortellini Bolognese it was really good tasting. I also had a side of spinach which tasted very fresh. My wife had a vegetarian lasagna. It was very good and had a flavor. She also had a salad with tomato. Very nice size.
label: Resident

name: Agata Po?omska
review: Pyszne cappuccino i lody ??
label: Resident

name: YY
review:
Was looking through the menu when we got approached by the service staff to be seated at the mini-square which was well ventilated - promptly handed the menu which came with English translations, and another menu with multiple photos illustration.

?? ordered the ¡°local fish soup¡± and gotten a seafood soup that comes with mussels and clams which we don¡¯t eat (though there were also fish and prawns too), only realised today that fish actually meant seafood after encountering the same at another restaurant

?? the escalope was actually a grilled variant (when I expected a fried one like it was in Lucerne), and the French fries came in grilled potato chunks too - pretty much not what I was used to

Whilst the menu wasn¡¯t really what we had assumed like many other reviews mentioned, there were no complaints on the quality of the food - soup was clean and good and the pork was super tender.

?? Another point to note like what others mentioned is their lack of clarity in the receipt, with no mentions of the dishes and purely the value amount of each dish and the total bill correspondingly. That said, if you don¡¯t touch the bread and limoncello isn¡¯t served, you won¡¯t be charged the cover fee.

All in all, not the best meal we had in Venice, but still an acceptable place to dine in a nice environment - definitely go with fishes like what the service team mentions though.

We then asked for something different, he came back with two different wines, arguing they were the same, when they absolutely weren¡¯t. Different labels, different years, different alcohol content. One was a Chianti Classic and was a Tuscan red. After asking to taste them it became clear he had tried to push the cheaper one on us for the same price as the other we ended up choosing. Their wine list doesn¡¯t specify what you¡¯re getting so be careful. We asked questions and the waiter became rude and aggressive when we kept calling him out on his repeated attempts to swindle us. I come to Venice often and won¡¯t be coming back here. A shame because they used to be better.

review:
TAX SERVICE CHARGE!!! 6-7 
SCAM!!!!!

I visit many pizzerias, but this experience was disappointing.

While it¡¯s common practice for restaurants to charge a service fee, it¡¯s important that this be clearly stated when placing the order, or at least on the menu, so customers can make an informed decision. In this case, there was no mention of it on the menu, nor was I told about it by the staff when I ordered. This is very disappointing and creates the feeling that something unreasonable is being "slipped" into the bill without the customer knowing.
Such practices are not only unpleasant but also unacceptable for establishments that care about their reputation and customer trust. If this charge had been disclosed, I would have had the choice to accept it or not, but it was hidden, and that will definitely impact my decision on whether to return in the future.
Recommendation: I would recommend the restaurant¡¯s management to be more transparent about their pricing and to inform customers of any additional charges before placing the order. This will avoid unpleasant situations and improve the overall customer experience.

Da Terzo is a little restaurant or better a real Italian trattoria.
It¡¯s a little business owned and operated from the same family for more than three decades.
You don¡¯t come here for the cordiality neither for the decor or for a special luxury service.
You come here  because the food is daily prepared like in a family, and you can taste some of the real dishes or the Venetian tradition in a very clean and simple environment being sure to receive the same quality and the same service.

While everything around them is changed in the last years, this trattoria still resist and deliver consistently, day after day, a good, solid flawless service, always without a smile.
Five stars for the consistency.

I wish more people would open shops like this with many different brands of gluten free items to try. I am tired of the same dry and tasteless options carried at major grocery stores.
